docs.body:
# 360° Baseline Walkthrough — Field Capture Sheet

**Customer:** ____________________  **Property:** ____________________________________
**Date:** ____________  **Walked by:** Marcin  **Year built:** ________

**Property profile (drives the ranges — record on the Roadmap Queue row):**
Sq ft ______  ·  Beds ____ / Baths ____  ·  Units ____  ·  Stories ____  ·
Exterior ____________ (wood / LP / vinyl / fiber-cement / stucco / brick)  ·
Foundation ____________ (crawlspace / basement / slab)  ·  Lot size ____________
> These scale the area-driven ranges (re-side, repaint, windows, gutters, drainage).
> A full re-side / re-clad is **not** the spot wood-rot band — see the cheat sheet.

> How to use: Walk the home **mechanical room first** (70% of emergencies live there),
> then basement/crawl, interior, exterior, roof, grounds. For each finding, jot the
> system, a plain description, an urgency, and a rough investment band. Snap photos as
> you go and note the count. Hand this back to the OS and the branded roadmap PDF gets
> built from it. Don't worry about polished wording — capture the facts, the voice gets
> added later.

## The 10 systems (use these exact labels)

1. **Roof & Gutters** — roofing, gutters, downspouts, roof drainage
2. **Exterior Envelope** — siding, trim, paint, windows, doors, exterior seals
3. **Foundation & Structure** — foundation, framing, crawlspace, basement, movement
4. **Drainage & Grounds** — grading, drainage paths, hardscape, vegetation clearance
5. **HVAC** — heating, cooling, filters, service history, comfort
6. **Plumbing** — supply, drains, fixtures, water heater, leaks, freeze risk
7. **Electrical** — panel, breakers, outlets, wiring, lighting, safety
8. **Interior Finishes** — walls, ceilings, floors, trim, attic insulation, moisture
9. **Appliances** — major appliances, built-ins, condition, replacement planning
10. **Safety & Security** — smoke/CO, handrails, trip risks, extinguishers, locks

## Condition + urgency vocabulary (use these exact words)

- **Condition** (per system): `good` · `fair` · `poor` · `critical`
- **Urgency** (per finding): `NOW` (within ~90 days, active risk/compounding) ·
  `SOON` (6–18 months, costlier if deferred past next weather cycle) ·
  `WAIT` (3–5 yr, monitor + document)
- When borderline, pick the **more conservative** bucket (NOW over SOON, SOON over WAIT).

---

## System-by-system capture

For each system: circle the overall condition, then list findings.

### 1. Roof & Gutters — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 2. Exterior Envelope — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 3. Foundation & Structure — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 4. Drainage & Grounds — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 5. HVAC — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Brand/model: ____________  Install year: ______
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 6. Plumbing — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Water heater brand/age: ____________
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 7. Electrical — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Panel amperage: ______
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 8. Interior Finishes — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 9. Appliances — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

### 10. Safety & Security — condition: good / fair / poor / critical
- Finding: ________________________________  Urgency: NOW / SOON / WAIT  $____–$____  Photos: __

---

## Investment-range cheat sheet (Clark County, fully-loaded customer price)

Use as a starting anchor; adjust to what you see. These already include HP margin —
**never show this sheet to the customer; it's for your bands only.**

| Finding | Typical band |
|---|---|
| Roof — minor flashing restoration | $650–$1,800 |
| Roof — partial shingle replacement | $1,800–$6,500 |
| Roof — full composition replacement | $14,000–$32,000 |
| Exterior siding — **spot** wood-rot restoration (section only) | $850–$4,800 |
| Exterior — **full re-side / re-clad** (whole home) | $18,000–$60,000 — scale by wall area (≈ sq ft × 1.6–2.2) & material |
| Exterior — full repaint, body + trim (whole home) | $4,500–$14,000 — ~$2–$4.50 / sq ft wall |
| Interior — whole-home repaint, walls/trim/doors | $4,500–$14,000 — ~$2.50–$5 / living sq ft |
| Windows — full-home replacement (all units) | $9,500–$30,000 — ~1 window per 100–120 sq ft × $950–$2,200 |
| Exterior trim — restoration and paint (spot) | $1,200–$5,500 |
| Gutter — cleaning and re-pitch | $425–$950 |
| Gutter — replacement (aluminum, 180 LF) | $2,800–$5,400 |
| Chimney — crown and flashing restoration | $1,400–$3,600 |
| Plumbing — single fixture replacement | $425–$1,200 |
| Plumbing — partial PEX repipe | $1,800–$5,400 |
| Plumbing — water heater (50 gal gas) | $2,400–$4,200 |
| Plumbing — tankless install | $4,800–$8,500 |
| Electrical — GFCI/AFCI (per circuit) | $250–$650 |
| Electrical — panel (200A) | $3,800–$7,200 |
| HVAC — maintenance tune-up | $285–$475 |
| HVAC — furnace replacement (80k BTU) | $5,400–$9,800 |
| HVAC — heat pump replacement (3-ton) | $9,500–$16,500 |
| HVAC — ductwork sealing/rebalance | $1,800–$4,200 |
| Foundation — crack seal (minor) | $650–$1,800 |
| Crawlspace — vapor barrier + drainage | $2,200–$6,400 |
| Window — weatherstrip/glazing restoration | $285–$750 |
| Window — single replacement (vinyl) | $950–$2,200 |
| Exterior door — weatherization | $285–$850 |
| Grading — regrade at foundation (per side) | $850–$2,400 |
| Drainage — French drain (50 LF) | $3,800–$8,400 |
| Safety — smoke/CO (whole home) | $425–$950 |
| Safety — radon mitigation | $1,800–$3,800 |

---

## Language guardrails for the customer-facing roadmap

When the findings turn into the roadmap, these are the rules the document follows
(handled automatically, but good to mirror if you debrief verbally):

- **Never say:** handyman, estimate, free, cheap, budget, fix, repair, simple, easy,
  "find a contractor," "get three bids."
- **Say instead:** restoration, resolve / return and address, investment range,
  standard of care, proactive, complimentary, vetted tradespeople, written scope of work.
- It's a **standard of care**, not a bid. Calm, specific, never alarmist.
docs.title: 360° Baseline Walkthrough — Field Capture Sheet

docs.body:
# Proactive Path — Membership Pitch (for today's walkthrough)

> **DRAFT for Marcin's review** before any of the customer-facing lines go out.
> Pricing matches the canonical source (`HP-Context.md`), the live site, and the app.

## The one move that makes this work

Lead with the roadmap, not the membership. The walkthrough and the roadmap are the
gift — complimentary, no obligation. The membership is simply *the calm way to keep
that roadmap handled* so nothing on it has to become an emergency.

Say it plainly, in your own voice:

> "The roadmap is the map of your home. The Proactive Path is how we keep it handled —
> we come back on a rhythm, watch for what's changing, and your living health record
> stays current. You've got one number to call and one team that knows your house."

## How to bridge from the roadmap to the membership

1. Walk them through the roadmap: the NOW items, then SOON, then WAIT.
2. Point at the **NOW** items: "These are the ones we'd return and address first."
3. Then the bridge: "Most folks don't want to think about the SOON and WAIT list every
   season — that's what the membership is for. We track it, we show up on a rhythm, and
   you're not the one remembering when the gutters need attention."
4. Offer the tier that fits what you saw (see picker below). One recommendation, not three.

## The three tiers

| | **Essential** | **Full Coverage** ⭐ | **Maximum Protection** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Monthly | **$59/mo** | **$99/mo** | **$149/mo** |
| Quarterly | $169/qtr | $279/qtr | $419/qtr |
| Annual | $588/yr ($49/mo) | $948/yr ($79/mo) | $1,428/yr ($119/mo) |
| Seasonal visits | 2 (Spring + Fall) | 4 (all seasons) | 4 (all seasons) |
| Labor bank | — | **$300/yr** | **$600/yr** |
| Member rates | 5 / 3 / 1.5% | 8 / 5 / 2.5% | 12 / 8 / 4% |
| Priority scheduling | — | — | **Yes** |
| | "Protect the basics." | "Four seasons + pre-paid labor." | "The full system. Priority access." |

*Member-rate tiers = discount on work under $1k / $1k–$5k / over $5k.*

**All tiers include:** annual 360° Home Scan, prioritized findings report with written
scope of work, member rates, and HP direct line. Three billing cadences.

**Full Coverage and up also include:** summer prep / weatherization, winter freeze &
moisture visit, and an annual equity documentation report.

**Maximum Protection also includes:** a dedicated HP account manager, pre-negotiated
vetted-tradesperson rates, and a home equity maintenance log.

## Which tier to recommend (read the home you just walked)

- **Mostly good/fair, a couple of NOW items** → **Full Coverage**. It's the most popular
  for a reason: four-season coverage plus a $300 labor bank that offsets the first job.
- **Older home, several NOW/SOON items, wants it fully handled** → **Maximum Protection**.
  The $600 labor bank and priority scheduling pay for themselves on a busy roadmap.
- **Newer/well-kept home, just wants to stay ahead of it** → **Essential**. Two visits,
  catch issues early, low commitment.

Default recommendation is **Full Coverage** unless the home clearly points elsewhere.

## Tie the labor bank to their actual roadmap

This is the most persuasive, honest line you can use — it's real money toward real work:

> "Full Coverage comes with a $300 labor bank each year. That goes straight toward the
> first thing on your NOW list — so the membership isn't just a line item, it's already
> working on your roadmap from day one."

## Handling the natural questions

- **"What does the walkthrough cost?"** — "The baseline walkthrough and your roadmap are
  complimentary. No obligation to continue."
- **"Can I just hire you for the one job?"** — "Absolutely — that's the Project Path, and
  we're glad to. The membership is for folks who'd rather we keep watch so the list
  doesn't pile up. Either way you're in good hands."
- **"Why a membership and not just call when something breaks?"** — "Because the cheapest
  version of almost everything on your roadmap is the proactive one. We'd rather return
  and address the small thing on a calm Saturday than meet you on the emergency."
- **Price resistance** → step down a tier or to quarterly/annual, never discount the work.
  Annual billing is the best value and the best line: "$79 a month, billed once."

## Enrolling today (taking payment)

If they say yes on the spot, the fastest, most reliable way to take the first payment is a
**Stripe Payment Link** for the chosen tier — they tap it on their phone and pay. Setup
steps and the links are in `Stripe-Live-Payments.md` in this folder. (The app's own
checkout is the follow-up path; the Payment Link is the today-safe one.)

## Voice guardrails (keep it genuine, never pushy)

- Low-pressure. One recommendation, then let them decide. Relationship over transaction.
- **Never say:** handyman, estimate, free, cheap, budget, fix, repair, "find a contractor."
- **Say instead:** restoration, resolve / return and address, investment range, standard
  of care, proactive, complimentary, vetted tradespeople, written scope of work.
- Never expose hard costs, markup, or margin math. The member rate is a member benefit,
  not a discount you're justifying.
docs.title: Proactive Path — Membership Pitch (for today's walkthrough)

docs.body:
# Baseline Walkthrough → Roadmap → Membership — Today's Playbook

Everything you need for an on-site 360° Baseline Walkthrough that ends with a branded
roadmap in the customer's hands and a membership offer. Built for a real visit happening
today; reusable for every visit after.

## Notion two-page system (primary field tool, since 2026-06-01)

The walkthrough now runs in Notion, under **Handy Pioneers → 360° Baseline Walkthroughs**.
Each customer gets **two linked pages** driven by one shared **360° Walkthrough Findings**
database (one row per finding):

- 🔧 **Internal Walkthrough** — tech-only. The capture board (by system), the priority
  board (NOW/SOON/WAIT), the internal cost-anchor cheat sheet, and the language guardrails.
- 🏠 **Customer Roadmap** — the shareable page. Customer info, what the roadmap is, and
  the findings grouped NOW/SOON/WAIT with investment ranges, condition, and photos. Cost
  anchors and internal notes never appear here. Share **only this page** (Share to web or
  by email); the internal page stays private.

Refine a finding or a price range on the internal page and the customer page updates live —
so you input everything first, then share. Start a new customer two ways: ask the OS
(*"new walkthrough for [name]"*) or duplicate **📋 TEMPLATE — Internal Walkthrough** and
repoint the three board filters. The `Capture-Sheet.md` below remains the offline/paper
fallback and the canonical spec for the systems, vocabulary, and cost anchors.

### Branded PDF from the Notion findings (the customer deliverable)

Customer roadmaps are delivered **privately by email from help@handypioneers.com**, ideally with
the **branded PDF** attached. The PDF is produced by a bridge that reuses the app's production
renderer and advisor-voice prompt:

- Script: `scripts/roadmap-from-notion.mjs` in the estimator repo
  (`C:\Users\marci\Documents\Codex\2026-04-30\backend-app-https-github-com-360method`).
- It pulls a customer's findings from the **360° Walkthrough Findings** Notion DB, has Opus 4.8
  write the prose, **preserves the on-site NOW/SOON/WAIT + investment ranges** (the model writes
  words, not money), and renders the byte-identical branded PDF via `server/lib/priorityTranslation/pdf.ts`.
- It also pulls each finding's **photos** (converts HEIC→JPEG and downscales) and embeds up to two
  thumbnails per finding under "FROM THE WALKTHROUGH." Needs `heic-convert` + `jimp` (already added).
- Run (needs the two keys the HP worker already uses — `NOTION_TOKEN` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`):
  `node --env-file=.env --import tsx scripts/roadmap-from-notion.mjs "<Property value>" "<FirstName>" "<Full address>"`
  → writes `outputs/roadmap-<firstName>-<date>.pdf`.
- The cloud GAS worker can't render the PDF (no Node); this bridge runs on the desktop/app side.
  Attach the resulting PDF to the help@ roadmap email.

## The flow

1. **Walk the home** with `Capture-Sheet.md` (print it or open on your phone). Go
   mechanical room first, capture findings + photos, mark each NOW / SOON / WAIT.
2. **Hand the findings back to the OS.** Tell Claude what you found (read off the sheet or
   send a photo of it). Claude turns it into the customer's **360° Priority Roadmap PDF** —
   same branded document the app produces, advisor voice, NOW/SOON/WAIT.
3. **Deliver the roadmap** to the customer (print, or email — draft reviewed by you first).
4. **Pitch the membership** with `Membership-Pitch.md` — lead with the roadmap, recommend
   one tier, tie the labor bank to their NOW list.
5. **Take payment** (if yes) with a Stripe Payment Link — see `Stripe-Live-Payments.md`.

## How the roadmap PDF actually gets made (the technical bit, for reference)

The generator lives in the app repo and reuses the production renderer — no database, no
AI key, no deploy needed:

- Script: `scripts/generate-roadmap.mjs` in
  `C:\Users\marci\Documents\Codex\2026-04-30\backend-app-https-github-com-360method`
- Input: a findings JSON (template: `scripts/roadmap-input.example.json`)
- Run: `pnpm tsx scripts/generate-roadmap.mjs <input.json>` → writes `outputs/roadmap-<name>-<date>.pdf`
- Smoke-tested 2026-06-01: renders a valid branded PDF end-to-end. ✅

In practice **Claude fills the JSON for you** from your raw findings (writing the advisor-
voice prose and setting the investment ranges from the cost-anchor table), runs the script,
and hands you the PDF. You just supply what you saw on the walk.

## Files in this folder

| File | What it's for |
|---|---|
| `Capture-Sheet.md` | The field form — fill during the walk |
| `Membership-Pitch.md` | Tiers, pricing, talking points, which tier to recommend |
| `Stripe-Live-Payments.md` | How to take the first payment today (Payment Links) |
| `README.md` | This playbook |

## Status / honest gaps (as of 2026-06-01)

- ✅ Roadmap generation works today via the standalone script (reliable path).
- ⏳ **In-app** "walkthrough → auto-roadmap" button isn't built yet, and the baseline wizard
  is gated to paid members (a prospect can't be captured in-app yet). That's the follow-up
  build — not needed for today.
- ⏳ **Stripe app checkout is not live** (test keys, price IDs unset, a naming bug). Use
  Payment Links today; app go-live steps are in `Stripe-Live-Payments.md`.
docs.title: Baseline Walkthrough → Roadmap → Membership — Today's Playbook

docs.body:
# Taking a Membership Payment Today — Stripe

## LIVE PAYMENT LINKS — all 9 tiers (created 2026-06-01)

Reliable, fully Stripe-hosted links in account `acct_1TPBvHLvoQGv2QYv`. Use these
to enroll members **now** (website checkout is still broken — see app-bug note
below). Open the matching link at the visit; member enters card; subscription
starts live. **Caveat:** Payment Links take payment + start the subscription but
don't carry the app's tier metadata, so the in-app CRM member record won't
auto-populate — create/confirm founding members' records manually for now.

| Tier | Cadence | Price | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| Essential | Monthly | $59 | https://buy.stripe.com/28E14o0BO1HA6xA2Tq1wY00 |
| Essential | Quarterly | $169 | https://buy.stripe.com/3cIdRafwIae609c3Xu1wY01 |
| Essential | Annual | $588 | https://buy.stripe.com/7sY9AU4S4ae6f463Xu1wY02 |
| Full Coverage | Monthly | $99 | https://buy.stripe.com/fZu8wQ3O0biaaNQ79G1wY03 |
| Full Coverage | Quarterly | $279 | https://buy.stripe.com/9B6bJ2bgscme5twdy41wY04 |
| Full Coverage | Annual | $948 | https://buy.stripe.com/bJebJ24S485Y8FI8dK1wY05 |
| Maximum Protection | Monthly | $149 | https://buy.stripe.com/bJe9AUfwIdqig8a0Li1wY06 |
| Maximum Protection | Quarterly | $419 | https://buy.stripe.com/9B600kaco4TM6xAalS1wY07 |
| Maximum Protection | Annual | $1,428 | https://buy.stripe.com/4gMcN62JWeume023Xu1wY08 |

## CUSTOMER-FACING ENROLL PAGE — `handypioneers.com/membership` is LIVE (2026-06-02)

Self-serve website checkout is **fixed and live** at **`handypioneers.com/membership`**
(tier selection → checkout form → Stripe → confirmation), wired to the live backend
`threeSixty.checkout.createSession`. Enrollments through this page auto-create the
membership + CRM customer record (no manual entry). Send new customers here.

**Root cause of the earlier breakage (the "two app bugs" were a misdiagnosis):** the
funnel POSTed to `threeSixty.createCheckoutSession`, which does not exist (404), so
checkout failed before Stripe. The real procedure is the nested
`threeSixty.checkout.createSession`. The tier-remap bug was already fixed/deployed,
and the "apiKey is not set" error was wrong (the flow uses a server-created hosted
redirect, no client Stripe key). Fixed by porting the flow onto the main site and
calling the correct endpoint. See `decisions/log.md` 2026-06-02.

**Still available as backup:**
- **Notion enroll page:** https://handypioneers.notion.site/membership (source page
  `3738807401c281218ae0e82860c006c7`) — the 9 Payment Links. Use only when you want a
  link to text someone directly; note Payment-Link signups still need a manual CRM
  record (see below).
- **Direct Payment Links** (table above) — same caveat.

## AFTER THEY PAY — create the member record (manual, every time)

Payment Links take payment and start the subscription, but **do not carry the app's tier
metadata**, so the in-app CRM member record will **not** auto-populate. For each new member,
once the Stripe payment notification arrives:

1. In the HP Estimator CRM, create/confirm the member's record: name, property/address, tier,
   and cadence (match what they paid in Stripe).
2. Note the labor bank ($0 Essential / $300 Full Coverage / $600 Maximum) and member-rate tier
   so benefits apply correctly.
3. Kick off onboarding (welcome + first seasonal visit scheduling).

This manual step goes away once the website checkout (`checkout.session.completed` → webhook →
DB) is fixed and the app handles enrollment end-to-end.

## EMAIL RECEIPTS — must be enabled in the dashboard (gap found 2026-06-02)

A live test enrollment went through but **no receipt emailed** — because the account-level
customer-email toggle was off. Receipts for Payment Link / subscription payments are an account
setting, not part of the link. One-time fix in **live mode**:
`dashboard.stripe.com/settings/emails` → under **Customer emails**, turn ON **Successful
payments** (and **Refunds**) → Save. Applies to all future payments. To resend a receipt for a
past payment: open the payment/invoice in the dashboard → **Send receipt**.

## WEBSITE CHECKOUT — broken, needs dev (as of 2026-06-01)

The funnel button at `handypioneers.com/membership` cannot enroll anyone yet. Two
app-side bugs (Stripe account/prices/webhook are all correct):
1. **Tier remap bug** — live build sends `exterior_shield/full_coverage/max`; the
   backend only accepts `bronze/silver/gold`. Already fixed in `hp-360-funnel`
   main (PR #3) but the live site runs a stale pre-fix build.
2. **Checkout session init** — the backend-generated session opens with
   `CheckoutInitError: apiKey is not set` (likely a custom/embedded ui_mode issue
   in the `pro.handypioneers.com` backend repo — not yet obtained).
Live site is a stale monolith that matches neither current repo; deploy is on
Railway (project d3a9ee5b…, service d04c68f7…). Proper fix needs the backend repo
+ deploy reconciliation in a focused dev pass.

## LIVE PRICE IDS — verified 2026-06-01

> **Account note (important):** there are TWO Stripe accounts both named
> "Handy Pioneers." The **correct, canonical one** is
> **`acct_1TPBvHLvoQGv2QYv`** — it holds the real products with the member-rate
> discount tiers and labor banks. A second account `acct_1SIgEyPkrn0rwx15` (a
> different login) had 9 prices created by mistake during setup; those are
> **stray and should be archived** — do not wire Railway to that account.

Verified via the Stripe connector against **`acct_1TPBvHLvoQGv2QYv`**. The 3
products + 9 prices below are in **live mode**, amounts match canonical pricing,
and the discount tiers / labor banks are stored as product metadata.

Products (live):
- 360° Method — Essential — `prod_UcyDdu3hFbczQy` (2 visits/yr, $0 bank, rates 5/3/1.5)
- 360° Method — Full Coverage — `prod_UcyDomBmrWozwa` (4 visits/yr, $300 bank, rates 8/5/2.5)
- 360° Method — Maximum Protection — `prod_UcyDwD7LBgRu0G` (4 visits/yr, $600 bank, rates 12/8/4, account manager)

Backend env var → price ID (Railway, `pro.handypioneers.com`). Funnel sends
`bronze`=Essential, `silver`=Full Coverage, `gold`=Maximum; product metadata uses
`tier_key` essential/full_coverage/maximum — map by amount as below:

| Env var (server reads these) | Tier / cadence | Amount | Price ID |
|---|---|---|---|
| `STRIPE_PRICE_BRONZE_MONTHLY`   | Essential / monthly   | $59    | `price_1TdiH1LvoQGv2QYvJOlvXLdJ` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_BRONZE_QUARTERLY` | Essential / quarterly | $169   | `price_1TdiH1LvoQGv2QYvuo7bOHdV` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_BRONZE_ANNUAL`    | Essential / annual    | $588   | `price_1TdiH1LvoQGv2QYvqsnHodIH` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_SILVER_MONTHLY`   | Full Coverage / monthly   | $99    | `price_1TdiGzLvoQGv2QYvpGLzkaaT` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_SILVER_QUARTERLY` | Full Coverage / quarterly | $279   | `price_1TdiGzLvoQGv2QYvAqqi7itF` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_SILVER_ANNUAL`    | Full Coverage / annual    | $948   | `price_1TdiGyLvoQGv2QYvIkFgvtl2` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_GOLD_MONTHLY`     | Maximum / monthly   | $149   | `price_1TdiGtLvoQGv2QYvdi7B8nK7` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_GOLD_QUARTERLY`   | Maximum / quarterly | $419   | `price_1TdiGtLvoQGv2QYvPAfeD9RM` |
| `STRIPE_PRICE_GOLD_ANNUAL`      | Maximum / annual    | $1,428 | `price_1TdiGtLvoQGv2QYvmUCgQvbV` |

> Confirm the exact env-var names against the `pro.handypioneers.com` backend code
> before relying on them — names above come from this doc's go-live notes, not a
> code read of that repo. Member-rate discounts and labor banks are tracked as
> product metadata (benefits applied in the app), not as Stripe coupons on the
> subscription.

**STATUS — LIVE & VERIFIED 2026-06-01.** All 9 tier/cadence checkouts return live
Stripe sessions (`cs_live_…`) end-to-end via `pro.handypioneers.com/api/360/checkout`.
Confirmed reads: the backend uses the env-var names exactly as listed above
(`STRIPE_PRICE_{BRONZE|SILVER|GOLD}_{MONTHLY|QUARTERLY|ANNUAL}`) — **no `360_`
naming bug**. Live `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` / `STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` / webhook secret
for `acct_1TPBvHLvoQGv2QYv` are set in Railway.

Remaining before relying on it for paying members:
- **Real enroll + refund test:** run one real card through a tier, confirm the
  member record appears (checkout.session.completed → webhook → DB), then cancel +
  refund. (Only the checkout-session creation is verified so far, not the full
  webhook→member-record loop.)
- **Rotate the webhook signing secret:** it appeared in plaintext in server error
  output during setup. Stripe → webhook endpoint → Roll secret → update Railway.
- **Sales tax (separate workstream):** activate Stripe Tax + WA registration in the
  dashboard, tag the 3 products with a tax category, and add `automatic_tax` +
  service-address-as-shipping to the checkout session in the backend. Until then no
  tax is collected.

---

# Taking a Membership Payment Today — Stripe

> **Bottom line:** The app's built-in 360° checkout is **not live yet** (it's still on
> Stripe test keys, the live price IDs aren't set, and there's a code bug in the setup
> script — see "App checkout" below). So for **today**, take the first payment with a
> **Stripe Payment Link**. It works on your phone, needs no app wiring, and deposits to
> the same Stripe account.

---

## TODAY — Stripe Payment Links (the reliable path)

Do this once, ahead of the visit, on your phone or laptop. ~10 minutes.

### Step 1 — Make sure you're in LIVE mode
1. Go to **dashboard.stripe.com** and sign in.
2. Top-right, find the **Test mode** toggle. Make sure it is **OFF** (live mode). If it's
   on, your links won't take real money.

### Step 2 — Create a Payment Link for each tier you want to offer
Start with **Full Coverage monthly** and **Full Coverage annual** — that covers most
customers. Add Essential and Maximum if you want the full menu.

1. Left sidebar → **Payment Links** → **+ New** (or **Create payment link**).
2. Choose **Product**, then **+ Create new product**.
3. Fill in:
   - **Name:** exactly as below
   - **Price:** the amount below
   - **Recurring**, billing period as below (this makes it a subscription, not a one-time charge)
4. Click **Add product**, then **Create link**.
5. **Copy the link.** Text it to yourself or bookmark it so you can pull it up at the visit.
6. Repeat for each tier/cadence.

**Exact products to create (type these precisely):**

| Product name | Price | Billing period |
|---|---|---|
| Proactive Path — Essential (Monthly) | $59.00 | Monthly |
| Proactive Path — Essential (Annual) | $588.00 | Yearly |
| Proactive Path — Full Coverage (Monthly) | $99.00 | Monthly |
| Proactive Path — Full Coverage (Annual) | $948.00 | Yearly |
| Proactive Path — Maximum Protection (Monthly) | $149.00 | Monthly |
| Proactive Path — Maximum Protection (Annual) | $1,428.00 | Yearly |

*(Minimum to create today: the two Full Coverage rows. Add the rest if you have time.)*

### Step 3 — Turn on a couple of options (recommended)
While creating the link (or in **Settings → Customer emails**):
- **Email receipts** to customers: ON.
- **Collect customer address:** ON (you'll want it for the property record anyway).

### Step 4 — At the visit
- Customer says yes → open the right link on your phone → hand them the phone (or text them
  the link). They enter their card and confirm.
- You'll get a Stripe notification + email when it goes through. Done — they're enrolled.

### Step 5 — Prove it works before you rely on it
Either: subscribe yourself to the monthly link with a real card, confirm it charges, then
**cancel + refund** from the Stripe dashboard. Or trust Stripe and run it live with the
customer — Payment Links are well-tested. Your call.

---

## After today — making the APP's own checkout live (follow-up, not for today)

The in-app `360.handypioneers.com` checkout will be the long-term path. To switch it from
test to live, here's what's needed (this is dev + dashboard work — I can do the code parts;
the dashboard/Railway parts need your hands):

1. **Create the 9 live prices.** Run the setup script with your **live** secret key:
   `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_... pnpm tsx scripts/stripe-setup.ts`
   It prints 9 price IDs.
2. **⚠️ Fix the naming bug first.** The script prints variable names like
   `STRIPE_PRICE_360_SILVER_MONTHLY`, but the server actually reads
   `STRIPE_PRICE_SILVER_MONTHLY` (no `360_`). If you paste the script's output as-is, the
   app won't find the prices. I need to correct this in `scripts/stripe-setup.ts` (or rename
   in Railway) before go-live.
3. **Set live env vars in Railway → Variables:**
   - `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...`
   - `STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_...` and `VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_...`
   - the 9 `STRIPE_PRICE_{BRONZE|SILVER|GOLD}_{MONTHLY|QUARTERLY|ANNUAL}` price IDs
   - `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...` from the live webhook (next step)
4. **Create the live webhook:** Stripe Dashboard (live mode) → Developers → Webhooks →
   add endpoint `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/stripe/webhook`, copy its signing secret
   into `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
5. **Redeploy** on Railway, then run one real end-to-end enrollment and refund it.

### Two things I need to reconcile in the code before app go-live
- **Naming bug** (above): `stripe-setup.ts` output vs what the server reads.
- **Labor-bank mismatch:** `stripe-setup.ts` product descriptions say "$200 labor credit"
  (Silver) and "$500" (Gold), but the canonical pricing is **$300** (Full Coverage) and
  **$600** (Maximum Protection). The Payment Links above are correct on price; this only
  affects the in-app product copy, but it should be fixed so customers see one consistent story.
docs.title: Taking a Membership Payment Today — Stripe

# Proactive Path Membership — Pre-Launch Checklist

> **Owner: Handy Pioneers.** The Proactive Path (360° Membership) is HP's recurring
> annual program — HP delivers the **360° Method framework** to its members. The
> 360° Method brand/IP itself is tracked separately in
> `../../360 Method/360° Method/STATUS.md`.

**Product:** Handy Pioneers Proactive Path — 360° Membership  
**Funnel:** [360.handypioneers.com](https://360.handypioneers.com)  
**Backend:** [pro.handypioneers.com](https://pro.handypioneers.com) (HP Estimator + Stripe)  
**Status:** Pre-launch — Stripe in TEST mode, no live members  
**Last updated:** 2026-04-19

---

## Technical (Backend & Infrastructure)

- [ ] Fix `/360-welcome` confirmation page on `client.handypioneers.com`
- [ ] Verify Stripe webhook handles `checkout.session.completed` event
- [ ] Test full flow end-to-end in test mode (checkout → webhook → member record in DB)
- [ ] Set up Twilio credentials in Railway (SMS notifications)
- [ ] Set up Gmail/SMTP credentials in Railway (email confirmations)
- [ ] Switch Stripe to LIVE mode — create 9 live price IDs (3 tiers × 3 cadences)
- [ ] Update `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` in Railway to live key (`sk_live_...`)
- [ ] Update `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` in Railway to live webhook secret
- [ ] Disable old Manus Stripe webhook endpoints
- [ ] Verify member record appears in HP Estimator CRM after enrollment

---

## Funnel & Conversion

- [ ] Review and finalize copy on `360.handypioneers.com` (homeowner page)
- [ ] Review and finalize copy on multifamily/portfolio page
- [ ] Add trust signals (testimonials, guarantees) — placeholder for now
- [ ] Confirm pricing displays correctly for all 3 tiers × 3 cadences
- [ ] Test full funnel on mobile
- [ ] Add Google Analytics or basic tracking pixel

---

## Member Onboarding

- [ ] Define what happens after enrollment — welcome email content
- [ ] Define first visit scheduling process (how does member book their first 360° scan?)
- [ ] Create welcome email template
- [ ] Define SOP for fulfillment of each tier:
  - **Bronze:** 2 visits/year
  - **Silver:** 4 visits/year + $200 labor bank
  - **Gold:** 4 visits/year + $500 labor bank + priority scheduling

---

## Business Operations

- [ ] Define how labor bank credits are tracked in HP Estimator
- [ ] Create member intake form / property intake checklist
- [ ] Define cancellation and pause policy
- [ ] Confirm test prices match desired live prices before switching Stripe to live mode

---

## Launch

- [ ] Internal soft launch — enroll first member (Marcin as test)
- [ ] Fix any issues surfaced from soft launch
- [ ] Set go-live date
- [ ] Announce to warm leads and existing customers

# Handy Pioneers — Project Status

**Last updated:** May 16, 2026

---

## Current State

- **HP Estimator** fully built and live — React 19 + Express + tRPC, MySQL, Stripe, Cloudinary
- Migrated off Manus hosting; now running on **Railway**
- **Live URLs:** [pro.handypioneers.com](https://pro.handypioneers.com) (staff portal) · [client.handypioneers.com](https://client.handypioneers.com) (client portal)
- Email + password auth operational; DB migrations auto-run on deploy
- All Manus dependencies removed — OAuth, storage, PDF, debug spy fully stripped
- Stripe webhook live at `pro.handypioneers.com/api/stripe/webhook`
- Business operates in Clark County, WA (Vancouver); Marcin owns customer-facing work, labor is subcontracted locally

---

## In Progress

- Wiring **360° Method enrollment funnel** backend to HP Estimator
- End-to-end checkout flow testing underway

---

## Folder Structure
Reorganized May 2026 — modeled on Golds pattern.
- `01_Operations` · `02_Subcontractors` · `03_Finance` · `04_Marketing`
- `05_Technology` → HP_Estimator docs + AI_Workflows (10 task files, 4 domains, full skills/prompts layer)
- `06_Clients` · `07_Legal_HR`
- AI_Workflows README: `05_Technology/AI_Workflows/README.md`

---

## To Do

- [ ] Switch Stripe from test mode to live mode — live price IDs + live webhook
- [ ] Run full end-to-end enrollment test
- [ ] Configure Twilio SMS and Gmail credentials in Railway environment variables
- [ ] Systematize Vancouver operations — SOPs, dispatch workflow, margin enforcement
- [ ] Complete replacement of Housecall Pro with HP Estimator

---

## Vision

Scale nationally as a **remote-managed, locally-executed** home services business. The Vancouver playbook — subcontracted local labor, centralized ops management — gets replicated into new markets without requiring Marcin on the ground.

HP Estimator evolves into the **proprietary field operations platform**: estimating, client management, dispatch, and margin control in one system. Each new market runs on the same OS.

docs.body:
# Manus build brief — Membership funnel (size-aware quiz, consult-first)

> This is the task prompt sent to Manus via `scripts/manus.py` against the existing
> Handy Pioneers website project. Goal: build/redesign the membership enrollment
> page **inside the current site, matching its existing design system** (fonts,
> colors, nav, components). Do not restyle the rest of the site. Deliver a
> **preview** for review — do not publish until approved.

## What to build
Replace the membership enrollment page with a **quiz-style, single page** that
tailors pricing to the visitor's home, then routes them to book a consultation.

### Step 1 — "Tell us about your home"
- Ask **property type**: Single-family home · Condo or townhome · Investment property.
- If **Investment property**: skip pricing and show a short "investment properties
  are tailored — book a consultation" block with a CTA to the baseline consult.
  (Self-serve investment pricing comes later.)
- For single-family or condo/townhome, ask **home size**:
  - Primary input: an approximate **square-footage slider** (range ~800–8,000 sq ft,
    live readout).
  - Fallback: a "not sure on square footage? use bedrooms" toggle → a bedroom
    select that maps to a size.

### Step 2 — Show the three plans at the price for THIS home
Reveal three plan cards with the monthly price already tailored to the home.

## Pricing model (CRITICAL — internal only, never shown to the visitor)
Square footage maps to an internal price band. **Never display the band name, the
square-footage bracket, the word small/medium/large, or any cost/markup/margin
math.** The visitor only ever sees the final monthly price for each plan.

Internal bands (do not surface these labels anywhere in the UI or page source text):
| Internal band | Sq ft | Essential | Full Coverage | Maximum |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| (band 1) | under 2,000 | $49/mo | $85/mo | $129/mo |
| (band 2 — anchor) | 2,000–3,499 | $59/mo | $99/mo | $149/mo |
| (band 3) | 3,500–4,999 | $79/mo | $129/mo | $199/mo |
| (band 4) | 5,000+ | $99/mo | $159/mo | $239/mo |

Bedroom fallback → square footage used for banding: 1–2 bd = 1,100 · 3 bd = 2,300 ·
4 bd = 3,000 · 5 bd = 4,200 · 6+ bd = 5,500.

Show monthly price; note "quarterly & annual options at your consultation." Do not
invent quarterly/annual numbers on the page.

## Plan names and content (use these exact names)
Customer-facing names are **Essential**, **Full Coverage**, **Maximum** — replace
any existing "Silver"/"Gold" labels on the site. Mark **Full Coverage** as "Most
Selected." Every plan runs the full 360° Method (Baseline Walkthrough, living
health record, four seasonal visits/year, NOW/SOON/WAIT roadmap).

- **Essential — "I know what my home needs."** Complimentary Baseline Walkthrough;
  all four seasonal cadence visits (assessment + documentation); living health
  record in the client portal; NOW/SOON/WAIT roadmap refreshed quarterly; priority
  scheduling on Project Path work. Seasonal visits are assessment-focused;
  execution quoted separately.
- **Full Coverage — "My seasonal maintenance is handled."** Everything in Essential,
  plus in-scope seasonal execution included (gutter clearing, HVAC filter
  replacement, deck surface care, exterior washing, weatherproofing + seasonal
  caulking audit); vendor coordination for specialty trades; preferred response
  window between visits.
- **Maximum — "My home is a managed asset."** Everything in Full Coverage, plus
  monthly concierge touchpoint; annual capital project roadmap; annual asset
  valuation review; first-right-of-service on the project calendar; direct line for
  strategic questions.

## CTAs — consult-first (this version)
Every plan's button = **"Schedule a Consultation to Enroll"** routing to the
existing Baseline Walkthrough booking flow. Pass the chosen plan and the home info
(property type + square footage) into that flow so the team has context. The
Baseline Walkthrough comes first; the tier is confirmed after. No online payment in
this version (direct Stripe checkout is a later phase).

## Design + guardrails
- Match the existing site's look exactly (typography, color, nav, footer, card
  components). This page should feel native to the current site.
- Affluent audience: never make a homeowner feel their home is "small/basic." No
  size labels anywhere — size only changes the price silently.
- No cost, markup, or margin math anywhere.
- Mobile responsive.

## Deliverable
A preview link to review. Do not publish to the live domain until approved.
docs.title: Manus build brief — Membership funnel (size-aware quiz, consult-first)

docs.body:
---
id: HP-DOC-001
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: Membership Funnel Redesign Plan
status: draft
initiative_status: building
created: 2026-06-04
updated: 2026-06-04
---

# Membership Funnel Redesign Plan

Turn the Proactive Path funnel from flat pricing into **size-aware** pricing
without ever making a homeowner feel their house is "small." Two axes:

- **Scope tier** (what the member sees and picks): Essential / Full Coverage /
  Maximum. Unchanged from today.
- **Home size** (invisible): a quiz collects the home's details and the price for
  each scope tier is tuned to the home behind the scenes. The client never sees a
  size label, a band name, or any cost math.

The typical single-family home is the **anchor** (the "medium" case) and pays
exactly today's prices. Smaller homes price down, larger homes up.

## Decision Tree

### Fork 1: Property Type
- Personal Home (build first, ship by 2026-06-15)
- Investment Property (build immediately after)

### Fork 2A: Personal Home
The client answers a short "tell us about your home" quiz (square footage, with
bedroom count + property type as a fallback proxy if they don't know the sq ft).
That silently maps the home to one of four internal size bands. Then the page
shows the three scope tiers (Essential / Full Coverage / Maximum) with the price
already set for that home. No size word appears anywhere in the copy.

### Fork 2B: Investment Property
Same invisible-input principle, but the size proxy is **unit count** instead of
square footage:
- Single-Family
- Duplex
- Triplex
- 4-Plex
- 5-Plus Units

Investment-specific outcomes and the band-to-price mapping are an open decision
(see below). The duplex client already active gets a grandfather policy (open).

## Size bands (INTERNAL ONLY — never shown to the client)

Anchored so the typical single-family home lands in the **Estate** band at today's
prices. Names use a flattering "Estate ladder" so that if a band ever has to be
named on an internal quote or agreement, no home reads as diminished.

**Updated 2026-06-04 (floor model):** the standard rate ($59/$99/$149) is the
**floor** — small and average homes all start there (no discount below it). Larger
homes build up via a multiplication markup.

| Internal band | Square footage | Multiplier | Typical home |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Standard (floor)** | **under 2,000** | **1.00x** | small homes (median ~1,800 sq ft) = base price |
| Large | 2,000–3,500 | 1.30x | average single-family |
| Estate | 3,500–5,000 | 1.60x | larger single-family |
| Grand | 5,000+ | 1.90x | the largest properties |

Why these cutoffs:
- US existing-home median is ~1,792 sq ft (FRED, Jan 2025) and new single-family
  median is ~2,150 sq ft (NAHB, 2025), so a typical single-family sits in the
  Estate band. That is the "medium" anchor.
- 5,000 sq ft is the industry's standard premium threshold: home-warranty firms
  (NerdWallet/AHS) add a surcharge or require a separate quote above it, so it is
  the natural top band.

## Internal price grid (monthly)

**Updated 2026-06-04 (floor model).** Standard row = today's published prices and is
the floor (no discount below it). Larger rows build up via the band multiplier,
rounded to the house "$9" convention. The client only ever sees the single column
for their own home; the band, the multiplier, and the math stay internal.

| Band | Essential | Full Coverage | Maximum |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Standard (floor)** | **$59** | **$99** | **$149** |
| Large | $79 | $129 | $199 |
| Estate | $99 | $159 | $239 |
| Grand | $119 | $189 | $289 |

- **Quarterly / annual:** apply the same band multiplier to the established
  quarterly and annual anchors (Essential $169/qtr, $588/yr · Full Coverage
  $279/qtr, $948/yr · Maximum $419/qtr, $1,428/yr).
- **Per-job member discounts are unchanged and stay scope-based, not size-based:**
  Essential 5% / 3% / 1.5%, Full Coverage 8% / 5% / 2.5%, Maximum 12% / 8% / 4%
  (by job value <$1k / $1k–$5k / >$5k).

## Build Priority
1. Personal Home path — ship by 2026-06-15
2. Investment path — ship immediately after (duplex client is active)

## Funnel UX
- Quiz-style single page (preferred over multi-page navigation):
  1. "Tell us about your home" → sq ft (bedrooms + property type as proxy).
  2. Show the three scope tiers, price already tuned to the home, no size label.
  3. CTA → consultation / Stripe, consistent with the current `enroll.html` flow
     (which routes to a baseline consult before payment).

## Build status
The live site is a **React/Vite app**, not static HTML — repo
`360Method/handy-pioneers-manus` (front end), `360Method/HP-Estimator-app`
(backend), deployed on Railway with a `staging` → `main` flow. The funnel was
built **natively into that repo**, not as standalone HTML.

- **Built on the `staging` branch (commit 4e802fa), 2026-06-04**, previewable at
  **staging.handypioneers.com** (Railway auto-deploys the branch). Promote
  `staging → main` to ship.
- **Where:** `client/src/lib/tiers.ts` (added the size-band model: `bandForSqft`,
  band-aware `getPrice` / `getMonthlyEquivalent` / `getSavingsVsMonthly`, anchor =
  estate = today's prices), `client/src/pages/Membership.tsx` (the home-size quiz:
  sq ft slider + bedroom fallback → band), `client/src/components/membership/TierCard.tsx`
  (shows the tuned price). Size band/multiplier never render in the DOM.
- **CTA is consult-first (interim):** the enroll button calls the existing
  `openInquiry()` consultation modal instead of `/membership/checkout`. Typecheck
  + production build both pass.
- **Funnel simplified to one path (commit 9cffcba):** `/360-method` lost the 3-path
  "Choose Your Starting Point" chooser; its CTAs now lead into the `/membership`
  funnel ("Schedule Your Baseline Walkthrough"). `/360-method/walkthrough` redirects
  to `/membership`. The **Roadmap Generator** is kept separate (navbar only) as its
  own future project. The size personalizer was made prominent (amber callout +
  brand-amber, larger-thumb slider via scoped `.hp-size-slider` CSS).
- **3-step baseline funnel built (frontend `3037829`, backend `4415ed6`),
  2026-06-04:** the tier CTAs now open a purpose-built funnel instead of the generic
  consult popup:
  1. Step 1 popup (`BaselineInquiryForm`) — basics only → `/api/public/inquiry`
     (`funnel=baseline_walkthrough`), stashes lead ids + tier + size.
  2. Step 2 `/baseline/details` — address + home details → new backend
     `/api/public/inquiry/details` enriches the **same** lead.
  3. Step 3 `/baseline/offer` — buy-now upsell: annual at **30% off month-to-month**
     ($496 / $832 / $1,252) via `offer:"buynow"` checkout; decline → confirm.
  4. Step 4 `/baseline/confirmed` — "we'll reach out to schedule."
  `getApiBase()` routes the staging site to the staging backend. Both repos
  typecheck + build clean.
  - **SHIPPED LIVE 2026-06-04.** Both repos promoted `staging → main` (frontend
    `9c6637f`, backend `4415ed6`). handypioneers.com/membership = 200; prod backend
    `/api/health` = 200 and `/api/public/inquiry/details` preflight = 204. The whole
    funnel is live on handypioneers.com.
  - **Pricing floor (live):** under 2,000 = $59/$99/$149 · 2,000–3,500 = $79/$129/$199
    · 3,500–5,000 = $99/$159/$239 · 5,000+ = $119/$189/$289. Slider opens at 1,800 sq ft.
  - **Buy-now Stripe (live prices created):** 3 LIVE annual prices on the existing
    products — Essential `price_1Tek3kLvoQGv2QYvITMs4XBV` ($496), Full Coverage
    `price_1Tek3mLvoQGv2QYvN2hnTNib` ($832), Maximum `price_1Tek3mLvoQGv2QYvtn1rlTKs`
    ($1,252). **One step left (Marcin):** set env vars on the prod **HP-Estimator-app**
    Railway service — `STRIPE_PRICE_BRONZE_ANNUAL_BUYNOW` / `_SILVER_` / `_GOLD_` to
    those IDs; the service redeploys and the buy-now button works. Until then it
    degrades gracefully (lead still captured).
  - **Note:** the funnel's backend integration shipped without a live end-to-end
    test (staging-pro was down); prod health + CORS preflight verified, code mirrors
    the existing working inquiry path. A controlled live test lead is advisable.
- **Earlier "direct checkout (fast follow)" note** is superseded by the funnel
  above — buy-now uses flat per-tier annual prices, not per-band; per-size Stripe
  prices remain the deferred work.
- **`enroll-v2.html`** in this folder is now a **superseded reference prototype**
  (it proved the quiz + pricing); it does not match the real site and is not shipped.
- **Manus API path parked:** the API returns a 403 at its edge even with a valid
  key (likely API access not enabled on the account); not needed since the source
  is in GitHub. Helper/guide stay in the OS for later (`references/manus-api.md`).

## Phase 1 live test — verified end to end (2026-06-04)

First real end-to-end purchase run through the live funnel (test identity
`help+360test@handypioneers.com`, Essential, address 3402 NE 98th Ave Vancouver WA,
1,800 sq ft, built 1998, `founder100` coupon).

- **Funnel → Stripe: correct.** New customer `cus_Ue85ao8Wsy0T8O`, subscription on the
  Essential buy-now price (`price_1Tek3k…`, $496/yr, annual interval). Invoice $0.00
  paid — coupon zeroed it, no real charge. No cadence bug (annual confirmed).
- **Webhook → internal: fired.** Three emails landed (inquiry confirmation, membership
  welcome, portal-ready magic link), proving lead capture + membership creation + CRM
  tag + portal provisioning all ran.
- **Portal: tier renders, home is absent.** Logged in as the member: tier reads
  Essential, but the home (address / 1,800 sq ft / 1998) appears **nowhere**. This is
  the confirmed gap — the home the member describes in Step 2 never reaches a
  structured record and is never reflected back.

**Confirmed punch list → Phase 2:** (A) carry sqft/yearBuilt + address into a
structured `properties` row linked to the membership; (B) render the home + a "what you
bought" summary on the portal membership card; (C) cheap hardening (explicit
hpCustomerId link, assert annual on buy-now). Test subscription `sub_1Teppm…` cancelled;
test CRM/portal records to be deleted.

## Phase 2 — SHIPPED + VERIFIED LIVE (2026-06-05)

All three parts shipped to prod (HP-Estimator-app `main`) and verified with a live
buy-now purchase: the portal home block now renders the member's address + sq ft +
year built (confirmed live: "3402 NE 98th Ave · 2,600 sq ft · Built 1960").

- **Structured home capture.** `properties` gained `sqft`/`yearBuilt` columns
  (migration `0005`). The home is captured **structurally at funnel Step 2**
  (`/api/public/inquiry/details`) straight from the form — the authoritative source —
  so it no longer depends on the value surviving the browser's sessionStorage relay
  into checkout (the original test showed street saved but `sqft: null`, the relay was
  dropping it). The 360 webhook links that property to the membership by address match
  on purchase; `getMembership360` returns the linked home; `PortalHome` +
  `Portal360Membership` render it.
- **Cadence + linkage hardening.** Buy-now forces `cadence: "annual"` server-side;
  `hpCustomerId` is passed so the portal↔CRM link is explicit, not email-only.
- **Customer tier names everywhere.** Fixed a portal-wide leak of the internal
  Bronze/Silver/Gold codenames (membership page, welcome page, property card, estimate
  hints, enroll/manage dialogs) → Essential / Full Coverage / Maximum. Also corrected
  wrong hardcoded upgrade values ("$250 / 3 visits", "$200/$500 labor bank") to derive
  from the canonical tier definitions ($300/$600, +2 visits).
- **Follow-on direction:** the member portal as an always-on educate/serve/retain
  surface — tracked as the "Member Portal as a Value Engine" workstream in the cockpit.

## Value Engine slice 1 — Recommended work → request (SHIPPED 2026-06-05)

First slice of the "Member Portal as a Value Engine" workstream. Deployed to prod
(HP-Estimator-app `main` @ `b5de911`, Railway deploy `379f11ab` SUCCESS, migration
0006 applied). The existing portal estimate-approval flow (e-signature → opportunity
won) closes the loop unchanged; this slice wires scan recommendations into it.

- **Per-recommendation request.** Each recommendation on the 360° report page got a
  "Request this work" button. One tap creates a service request + CRM lead pre-filled
  with the finding (system, priority, scan-time cost range, technician notes), linked
  to the membership and property. Critical items route high-priority with an owner
  SMS. Idempotent — the button flips to "Requested."
- **Member pricing badge.** Each recommendation shows the member's tier label +
  headline discount rate ("Full Coverage member pricing applies — up to 8% off").
  Deliberately no dollar math: the real price comes from the internal estimate, which
  protects the margin floor and the no-cost-math rule.
- **Home card.** Portal home gained a "Recommended for your home" card aggregating
  open items from the latest scan (critical/high counts + top item + review link).
- **Plumbing.** Migration 0006 added `reportId`/`recommendationIndex` to
  `portalServiceRequests`; the portal→CRM lead bridge was extracted into a shared
  helper (`createCrmLeadFromPortalRequest`), reused by the standard service-request
  flow with behavior unchanged.
- **Copy fix.** "Pre-negotiated sub-contractor rates" → "Pre-negotiated rates with
  vetted trade partners" in the Maximum tier features (closes the client-facing subs
  leak flagged 2026-06-04).
- **Deliberately out of slice 1:** one-tap auto-priced approval (would bypass the
  internal estimate + margin floor), multi-item bundles, auto-scheduling.
- **Note:** the card and buttons only render for members with a delivered scan
  report, so nothing visible changes for members until their first report lands.

## Step-1 drop-off drip (LIVE IN PROD 2026-06-05)

Leads who complete the Step 1 popup but never finish enrollment used to sit
silently at "New Lead" in the CRM. An hourly backend job now sends them a
3-email recovery sequence:

- **Email 1 (+24h):** "Pick up where you left off" — resume link, soft tone.
- **Email 2 (+72h):** value angle (small fixes stay small), Proactive Path framing.
- **Email 3 (+7d):** low-pressure close, door stays open.

Mechanics (`HP-Estimator-app server/baselineDrip.ts`, wired in `_core/index.ts`):
auto-send via the existing email sender, CTA to handypioneers.com/membership,
no dollar figures or internal terms anywhere. Sent-state tracked with
`[Baseline-Drip-N sent]` markers on the lead notes. The drip stops when any of
these happen: the lead's stage moves past "New Lead" (a human took over), the
lead is archived, the customer purchases (the 360 webhook now also auto-archives
open Step-1 leads on checkout), the email is internal (@handypioneers.com), or
an open "Cart Abandoned" lead exists for the same customer (that drip already
owns the conversation). The pre-existing cart-abandonment drip is untouched.

18 unit tests cover eligibility, the 24h/72h/7d buckets, marker dedupe, and a
guard that the copy never leaks pricing or internal tier names. Decision logged
2026-06-05 (auto-send, cadence, stop-on-takeover).

**Deployed to prod 2026-06-05** (commit `de44a9e`, Railway deploy `32c56c34`
SUCCESS; boot log confirms "[Baseline Drip] Step-1 drop-off drip scheduler
started"). Shipped alongside the roadmap-generator funnel + its approval-gated
dropout drip (commit `6d9bd11`, built in a parallel session) — that one stays
dormant until `AGENTS_ENABLED=true` is set on the prod service. Sister doc for
the roadmap funnel lives with the roadmap workstream. Staging note: the
hp-estimator-staging service can't deploy because MySQL-Staging is stopped;
prod verification was done directly.

## Open Decisions
- **Investment band → price mapping** — how unit count maps to a price (per-unit
  add-on vs. its own multiplier ladder), and the investment-specific scope.
- **Duplex client grandfather policy** — the active duplex client's pricing.
- **Scope-tier names:** resolved on the live React site — it already uses the
  canonical **Essential / Full Coverage / Maximum Protection** (in
  `client/src/lib/tiers.ts`); the Silver/Gold wording only existed in the stale
  standalone `enroll.html`. No change needed. (Optional: shorten "Maximum
  Protection" to "Maximum" if desired.)
- **Hero copy:** the `/membership` hero still reads "from $588/year ($49/mo
  Annual)"; with size-aware pricing the true floor is the Residence band (~$500/yr,
  ~$42/mo). Decide whether to lower the "from" figure. (Left unchanged this pass.)

## Notes
- Do not expose cost, markup, margin, or the size multiplier to clients in any
  funnel copy. Size is an internal pricing input only.
- Quiz-style single-page UI preferred over multi-page navigation.

---
*Source: Notion cockpit Idea (auto-queued by the cockpit worker). Sits alongside
the live funnel assets in this folder (`enroll.html`, `thankyou.html`). Canonical
scope-tier pricing: `Pioneers-Compass/HP-Context.md`.*
docs.title: Membership Funnel Redesign Plan

docs.body:
---
id: HP-DOC-003
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: Roadmap Generator Funnel
status: final
initiative_status: live
created: 2026-06-05
updated: 2026-06-06
---

# Roadmap Generator Funnel — the marketable free front door

The 360° Roadmap Generator (handypioneers.com/roadmap-generator) is HP's
complimentary lead magnet for the affluent homeowner who already holds an
inspection report and wants guidance. Rebuilt 2026-06-05 from a single long
form into a 3-step funnel with a one-time offer, ready to market immediately.

## The funnel (SHIPPED 2026-06-05)

1. **Step 1 — popup.** One CTA on the marketing page opens a modal: first
   name, last name, phone, email, SMS consent. Lead lands in the CRM
   immediately (leadSource "Website — Roadmap Generator") — this is the
   dropout-drip anchor. Funnel-specific ack email/SMS ("finish your upload"),
   portal magic-link email suppressed (the roadmap delivery email carries its
   own).
2. **Step 2 — /roadmap/details.** Property-type toggle (**Personal home /
   Investment property** — investment swaps sqft for a unit-count picker:
   SFR / duplex / triplex / 4-plex / 5+). Address with the Clark County ZIP
   gate (out of area → waitlist branch, drip cancelled), year built, PDF
   drag-drop or Spectora/web-report URL, the "not a legal inspection"
   acknowledgment. Submit starts roadmap processing in the background and
   reuses the step-1 customer + lead (no duplicates) — the home is upserted
   as a structured CRM property row (sqft / yearBuilt / propertyKind /
   unitCount), the server-side source of truth.
3. **Step 3 — /roadmap/offer (the OTO).** While the roadmap generates:
   - **Personal home:** Maximum plan only, annual buy-now, priced by the
     home's size band — **$1,249 / $1,669 / $2,009 / $2,429** (band internal,
     re-resolved server-side at checkout via `offer: "buynow_sized"`;
     missing config falls back to the flat buy-now price, never blocks).
     Strike-through math: 30% off the published month-to-month grid.
   - **Investment property:** consultation pitch (portfolio walkthrough CTA
     that flags intent on the lead) instead of self-serve checkout — the
     unit-count pricing ladder is an open decision.
   - Small decline link: "No thanks, just send my free roadmap."
4. **Decline or purchase → live processing page**
   (`pro.handypioneers.com/portal/roadmap/submitted/{id}`). Buyers also get
   the membership welcome email + portal magic link via the existing webhook;
   the confirmation page notes the free roadmap is still on its way.
5. **Roadmap delivers free either way** (Claude reads the report → branded
   NOW/SOON/WAIT PDF → email + portal). Delivery now also schedules the
   post-delivery `roadmap_followup` nurture cadence on the marketing-site
   path (previously only the legacy path did).

## Generation pipeline — WORKING (verified live 2026-06-06)

The pipeline produced its first product on 2026-06-06: a live smoke test
against prod reached `completed` in 100 seconds — 13-page branded PDF, all 16
fixture findings extracted and triaged into NOW/SOON/WAIT, delivery email
sent. Three bugs had each killed it in turn, all fixed and deployed:

1. Oversized PDFs (Spectora exports) hit Claude's 18MB/95-page API limits →
   text-extraction fallback (`f6f73ea`).
2. `max_tokens: 4096` truncated real 20-finding roadmaps mid-JSON → raised to
   32,000 (`0c1bb8e`).
3. That raise tripped the SDK's 10-minute non-streaming ceiling, failing every
   call instantly → the call now streams (`3810ec3`, the final unblock).

**Roadmaps auto-send to the customer on completion** (decision 2026-06-06):
volume is ~0 and every delivery CCs sales@; a human review gate is required
before paid traffic (see Open below).

**Photo placement vision-verified (2026-06-07):** page-number mapping put
neighbors' photos under findings (Marcin's bug report). A Sonnet vision pass
(`photoMatcher.ts`) now assigns only photos that clearly show each finding's
specific component, justification logged per photo; tool/meter shots and other
systems rejected; matcher failure ships the roadmap photo-less. Verified on
the real 66-page report (21 photos, 11 findings, all correct). Cost: cents per
roadmap.

**Incident + fix (2026-06-07):** the guardrail deploy took the funnel down
overnight; migration 0008 silently never applied (journal timestamps on
0006/0007 are hand-stamped June 19, 2026 — the future — and drizzle skips
"older" entries; any migration generated before June 19 needs its journal
`when` hand-bumped above 1781920000001). Fixed and verified: columns live,
guardrails now FAIL OPEN (a broken protection layer logs loudly and lets the
homeowner through), both routes smoke-tested (personal completed in 100s;
investment completed in 6.7s — the dedupe reused the stored response, proving
that guardrail live). The investment route works as designed: same roadmap,
consultation-pitch offer instead of self-serve checkout.

**Out-of-area transparency + Home Score clarity (2026-06-07):** the details
form states the service area upfront (Clark County WA, each roadmap produced
at our expense); an outside ZIP switches the form to a short join-the-list ask
("Keep Me On The List", report/acknowledgment not required) and the waitlist
confirmation explains the why. $10 paid out-of-area roadmap DEFERRED (ranges
are Clark County-anchored; selling directional numbers elsewhere fails the
honest-pricing rule; revisit when one metro stacks waitlist leads). The Home
Score method is now explained on the roadmap PDF (under the At-a-Glance
strip), in the funnel FAQ, and in the portal (report detail, reports list,
home tile). Sample assets now `alder4-*` (9 pages, 5 verified findings).

**Em-dash purge (fourth deploy, 2026-06-06):** no em or en dashes on any
customer surface, Marcin's explicit standard. Enforced in the roadmap prompt
(every field, colon category labels), the PDF sanitizer (legacy content can't
render one), the Nurturer draft prompt, the whole manus site (749 swept), and
63 live email/SMS template rows (`scripts/strip-dashes-templates.ts`). Sample
regenerated v3 under `alder2-*` names.

**Guardrails shipped 2026-06-06 (third deploy):** three spend protections on
the pipeline — same-report dedupe (sha256 of the submitted report; identical
bytes within 30 days reuse the stored Claude response, render/photos/email
still run fresh), per-IP cap (5 per 24h; the per-email cap of 2 is
alias-dodgeable), and a global daily cap (`ROADMAP_DAILY_CAP`, default 25 —
overflow parks as "submitted" with an owner alert, releasable via reprocess).
Migration 0008 (`pdfSha256`, `submitIp`). Service area stays the existing ZIP
gate: Clark County WA constants, overridable per-market via the
`serviceZipCodes` table (no deploy needed when Portland opens).

**Sample + mobile fix shipped 2026-06-06 (manus site):** the marketing sample
is now a fictionalized real pipeline roadmap ("Daniel", fictional address, 12
findings, 6 anonymous inspector photos, 14 pages; assets `alder-*`,
cache-busted). The sample viewer scrolls pre-rendered page images instead of
iframing the PDF — mobile browsers froze the iframe on the cover page. Known
issue: staging.handypioneers.com custom DNS 404s (preview via
www-staging-production.up.railway.app).

**Quality pass shipped 2026-06-06 (same day, second deploy):**
- **Inspector photos in the roadmap.** Claude cites each finding's report
  pages (`source_pages`); the pipeline pulls the inspector's JPEGs off those
  pages and places up to 3 under the finding, labeled "FROM YOUR INSPECTION
  REPORT" (8MB document cap). Works on oversized reports too — the text
  fallback now keeps `[Page N]` markers. Verified on Marcin's real 66-page,
  42MB report: 36 photos across 12 of 18 findings.
- **Plain-prose rules in the generation prompt** (the humanizer standard:
  no em dashes in prose, no puffery, no rule-of-three, no -ing filler).
  Verified: 21k chars of generated prose, zero tells.

Ops tooling (run under `railway run -s HP-Estimator-app`):
- `scripts/test-roadmap-generator.mjs` — live e2e smoke test (no railway
  needed; synthetic fixture via `scripts/make-roadmap-fixture.mjs`).
- `scripts/roadmap-snapshot.mjs` — recent submissions + failure reasons.
- `scripts/retry-roadmap.mjs <id>` / `scripts/reprocess-local.ts <id> <pdf>` —
  re-run a failed submission (server copy vs rescued local copy).
- `scripts/render-roadmap-from-row.mjs <id> <out.pdf>` /
  `scripts/dump-roadmap-row.mjs <id>` — QA a delivered roadmap.

## Dropout drip — `roadmap_dropout` playbook

Step-1-only leads (gave contact, never uploaded) get a 4-step cadence, all
drafts approval-gated in the admin drafts inbox (/admin/agents/drafts),
operator-editable at /admin/agents/playbooks:

- T+45 min — email: "your roadmap is one step away"
- T+24 h — concierge SMS (skipped without SMS consent)
- T+3 d — value email (what NOW/SOON/WAIT gives a homeowner)
- T+7 d — graceful close, door stays open

Auto-cancelled on: report submitted, membership purchased, appointment
booked, inbound reply, or the out-of-area waitlist branch.

## Stripe + config

- 4 live prices on the Maximum product (`prod_UcyDwD7LBgRu0G`), lookup keys
  `max_annual_buynow_{standard|large|estate|grand}` — customer-facing tier
  vocabulary on all new artifacts, never the internal metal codes.
- Railway env (prod + staging backends):
  `STRIPE_PRICE_MAX_ANNUAL_BUYNOW_{STANDARD|LARGE|ESTATE|GRAND}`.
- Band helpers live in `HP-Estimator-app/shared/threeSixtyContract.ts`
  (keep-in-sync contract file); display grid in
  `handy-pioneers-manus/client/src/lib/tiers.ts` (`GOLD_BUYNOW_ANNUAL`).

## Key code (for the next session)

- Marketing site: `client/src/pages/RoadmapGenerator.tsx` (marketing page),
  `RoadmapDetails.tsx`, `RoadmapOffer.tsx`,
  `components/RoadmapInquiryForm.tsx`.
- Backend: `server/routers/publicInquiry.ts` (step-1 branch + waitlist),
  `server/lib/priorityTranslation/orchestrator.ts` (linkage + property
  upsert + drip cancel + followup scheduling),
  `server/routers/threeSixty.ts` (`buynow_sized`),
  `server/lib/leadNurturer/playbook.ts` + `roadmapFollowup.ts`
  (per-playbook engine + dropout cadence).
- Migration 0007: `properties.propertyKind` + `unitCount` (additive).

## Open / deferred

- **Review gate before paid traffic** — `draft_awaiting_review` status + an
  /admin/roadmap-reviews approval queue so Marcin approves each AI-composed
  roadmap before it emails. Auto-send is a temporary exception while volume
  is ~0 (decisions log 2026-06-06).
- **Upload persistence** — uploaded report PDFs land in `/tmp` and die on
  every redeploy (this is why Marcin's 2026-06-06 test couldn't be retried
  server-side). Attach a Railway volume or store the PDF in object storage.
- **Delivery visibility** — the customer email CCs sales@, but when the From
  is help@ itself, Google's self-send dedupe can hide the copy from the
  help@ inbox. Consider a distinct from-address or an explicit internal
  notification on success.
- **Investment pricing ladder** (per-unit add-on vs multiplier) — decide
  before giving investors self-serve checkout.
- Resume-token deep links in drip emails (drip links to /roadmap-generator;
  a returning lead re-runs the popup — email-deduped, but creates a second
  opportunity; acceptable at current volume).
- "Membership confirmed" state on the processing page.
- A/B tests, monthly/quarterly OTO variants, downsells; roadmap deliverable
  quality pass (separate session).

## Rules honored

- Size bands, multipliers, cost math: internal only, never client-facing.
- Tier names customer-facing everywhere (Maximum, never gold).
- The roadmap stays free regardless of the OTO answer.
docs.title: Roadmap Generator Funnel — the marketable free front door

docs.body:
---
id: HP-DOC-007
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: Email Funnel Audit — every automated email, fixed
status: final
created: 2026-06-08
updated: 2026-06-08
---

# Email Funnel Audit (every automated email Handy Pioneers sends)

Full sweep of every automated email and SMS that goes out across the funnel,
done before driving paid traffic to affluent buyers. The brief: find and fix the
things a sharp buyer notices and reads as machine-written. Three problems were
found and fixed everywhere. Everything below is **live on production**.

## What was wrong, and what was fixed

1. **Five different phone numbers.** The website publishes **(360) 838-6731**
   everywhere, but the app's emails had drifted to four others: `241-5718`
   (every database template + the support-phone setting), `544-9858` (estimate,
   360 welcome, the branded email shell, portal notices), `334-4428` (consultation
   ack, roadmap ack, baseline drip), and `519-9618` (baseline-walkthrough confirm).
   **Fix:** one number everywhere now, **(360) 838-6731**. 110 replacements across
   35 code files + 54 database templates + the support-phone setting.
2. **Em-dashes (the classic AI tell).** None remained in the database (a cleanup
   ran 2026-06-06), but they were all through the **hardcoded** funnel emails:
   consultation ack, roadmap ack, baseline drip, 360 welcome, booking ack,
   appointment confirm, baseline-walkthrough confirm, the estimate email, the
   overdue-invoice email, password reset, and several portal notices. **Fix:**
   replaced with plain punctuation (period, comma, colon, parentheses).
3. **AI-sounding word.** "we're thrilled to be the long-term home-care team" in the
   360 membership welcome. **Fix:** "we're glad to be...".

Beyond those, the copy is in good shape. The library is written in Handy Pioneers'
plain, warm "stewardship" voice and mostly reads human already, so this was a
correction pass, not a rewrite.

## The one true contact (use this everywhere)

- Phone: **(360) 838-6731**
- Email: **help@handypioneers.com** (replies to every customer email land here)
- From addresses: `help@handypioneers.com` (customer-facing), `noreply@handypioneers.com`
  (system/transactional only, still replies to help@)

## Where the emails live (two systems)

- **Database templates** — `emailTemplates` + `smsTemplates` tables in the
  production Postgres DB. 54 email + 9 SMS templates, edited in the admin UI and
  read at send time. This is the source of truth for lifecycle/nurture mail. (A
  legacy MySQL seed file, `scripts/seed-email-templates.mjs`, predates the
  Postgres migration and no longer feeds production — superseded.)
- **Hardcoded in code** — the funnel-entry emails (consultation, roadmap, baseline,
  360 welcome) and a few transactional ones are written inline in the app code.

---

## The inventory, by funnel stage

Status key: **FIXED** = had a problem (phone/dash/word), now corrected. **clean** =
reviewed, no change needed.

### 1. Consultation booking (the "in our care" path)

| Email | Trigger | Subject (now) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Project inquiry ack (inline, `messaging.ts`) | ProjectInquiryForm submit (T+0) | Your project request is in our care | FIXED (dashes, ambiguous "whichever you prefer" line) |
| Booking wizard ack (inline, `booking.ts` + DB `booking_inquiry_received`) | /book submit | Your inquiry is in our care, {name} | FIXED (phone, dashes) |
| Website inquiry ack (inline, `publicInquiry.ts`) | Public inquiry submit | Your inquiry is in our care, {name} | FIXED (phone, dashes, dropped "— Handy Pioneers") |
| Portal magic-link (inline, `publicInquiry.ts`) | Inquiry → portal account | Your Handy Pioneers portal is ready, {name} | FIXED (dashes) |
| Appointment confirmed (inline `leadRouting.ts` + DB `appt_consultation_scheduled`) | Consultation booked | Your visit on {date} is confirmed | FIXED (phone, dashes) |
| Concierge follow-up / estimate-view / long-term nurture drafts (`messaging.ts`) | Cadence, human-approved | (various) | FIXED (dashes) |

### 2. Membership signup (Proactive Path / 360° Method)

| Email | Trigger | Subject (now) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 360 membership welcome (inline, `threeSixtyWebhook.ts`) | Stripe checkout completed | Welcome to the 360° Method: {tier} membership confirmed | FIXED (phone, dashes, "thrilled") |
| `mem_welcome` (DB) | membership active | Welcome to the 360° Method - {tier} tier | FIXED (phone, "thrilled") |
| `mem_seasonal_scheduled`, `mem_tier_upgraded`, `mem_tier_downgraded`, `mem_paused`, `mem_cancelled`, `mem_annual_valuation` (DB) | lifecycle events | (see DB) | FIXED (phone) |
| Membership intro draft (`messaging.ts`) | T+5d after project, human-approved | An invitation to ongoing 360° stewardship | FIXED (dashes) |

### 3. Baseline assessment signup

| Email | Trigger | Subject (now) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Baseline dropout drip 1/2/3 (inline, `baselineDrip.ts`) | Step-1 lead stalls (24h/72h/7d) | Pick up where you left off / Small fixes stay small / We'll leave the door open | FIXED (phone) |
| Baseline walkthrough confirm (inline, `portalRoadmap.ts`) | Baseline booked from portal | Your Baseline Walkthrough is set, {name} | FIXED (phone 519-9618, dash) |
| `appt_baseline_scheduled` (DB) | Baseline scheduled | Your 360° Baseline Walkthrough is scheduled | FIXED (phone) |
| Post-baseline nurture `pb_day0/2/5/9/14` + `pb_sms_day1/4/12` (DB) | After baseline visit | (5-touch + 3 SMS) | FIXED (phone) — copy clean |

### 4. Roadmap generator

| Email | Trigger | Subject (now) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Roadmap "one step away" ack (inline, `publicInquiry.ts`) | Email captured, report not yet uploaded | Your 360° Roadmap is one step away, {name} | FIXED (phone, dashes) |
| `pt_received` (DB) | Report uploaded | We got your inspection report - translation started | FIXED (phone) — copy clean |
| Roadmap delivery (inline, `priorityTranslation/email.ts`) | Roadmap PDF ready | Your 360° Roadmap is ready | clean (already voice-scrubbed; no phone/dash) |
| `pt_ready` (DB) | translation ready | Your priority translation is ready | FIXED (phone) |

> **Roadmap "gap" checked and cleared.** In production the form posts to the
> HP-Estimator backend (`handleRoadmapSubmit`), which sends the customer the
> "one step away" ack and then the roadmap delivery. The manus site's
> team-only intake email is a *fallback* used only when the backend is
> disconnected, so no customer is left without an acknowledgment. No new email
> needed.

### 5. Estimates, invoices, payments, portal, account (lifecycle)

All carried `241-5718` (DB) or `544-9858`/`241-5718` (inline) and several inline
ones had em-dashes. All now on **838-6731** with dashes removed.

- **Estimates:** `scope_delivered`, `scope_accepted`, `scope_declined`,
  `scope_revised` (DB); estimate send email + SMS (inline `estimate.ts`, FIXED
  dashes); estimate-approved + job-sign-off + change-order fallbacks (inline
  `appSettings.ts`, FIXED dashes).
- **Invoices/payments:** `invoice_delivered`, `payment_received`, `payment_failed`,
  `payment_reminder_14`, `payment_reminder_30` (DB); payment receipt + overdue
  reminder (inline `_core/index.ts`, `gmail.ts`, FIXED).
- **Portal/account:** `account_welcome`, `account_magic_link`, `account_magic_expired`,
  `account_password_reset`, `account_email_changed`, `account_profile_updated` (DB);
  portal invite / estimate-approved / job-complete / referral / review-request
  notices (inline `portal.ts`, FIXED dashes); staff password reset (`passwordReset.ts`, FIXED dashes).
- **Appointments:** `appt_reminder_48h`, `appt_reminder_day_of`, `appt_tech_late`,
  `appt_visit_cancelled`, `appt_visit_completed` (DB) — FIXED phone, copy clean.

### Drip / nurture sequences (DB, copy reviewed — clean, phone fixed)

- Cold lead `nurt_cold_1/2/3`; no-show `nurt_noshow_1/2`; estimate-silent
  `nurt_est_silent_1/2/3`; declined `nurt_declined_1/2`; past-customer
  `nurt_past_6mo_1/2`, `nurt_anniv_1/2`; post-translation `nurt_pt_to_baseline_1/2`;
  referral `nurt_referral_1/2`.
- Nurture SMS: `nurt_sms_new_booking`, `nurt_sms_appt_reminder`,
  `nurt_sms_estimate_silent`, `nurt_sms_payment_reminder`, `nurt_sms_review_request`,
  `nurt_sms_seasonal_nudge`.

---

## What changed (summary)

- **Phone → (360) 838-6731:** 110 hits across 35 code files (server + customer-facing
  client/portal pages), 54 DB email templates, and the `supportPhone` setting.
- **Em-dashes removed** from every customer-facing inline email (consultation,
  roadmap, baseline, 360 welcome, booking, appointment, estimate, invoice, portal,
  password reset). DB templates were already dash-free.
- **"thrilled" removed** from the 360 welcome.
- Two reusable ops scripts left in the app repo: `scripts/dump-templates.ts`
  (read-only audit dump) and `scripts/fix-email-phone-and-voice.ts` (the DB change record).

## Notes / deferred (low priority)

- **Staging DB** templates weren't updated by the fix script (its DB is on a
  Railway-internal host not reachable from a laptop). Prod is correct; staging
  resyncs from prod. Customer-facing sends are all on prod.
- **Legacy MySQL seed file** still has old dashes/"thrilled" in its text. It's dead
  against production (Postgres) so customers never see it; left as-is, superseded.
- **Estimate PDF** uses an em-dash as an empty-cell marker and in "Tax (WA — client
  to verify)." That's a separate surface (PDF, not email) and standard table
  typography; left for a future PDF pass.
- **Internal staff tools** (GBP post suggestion, Meta reply suggestion) still use a
  dash in their composer drafts. Staff-facing, not auto-sent to customers; left.
- **Centralization:** all code now uses one literal number, but DB templates still
  bake the number into copy rather than pulling a `{{supportPhone}}` merge var.
  True single-source templating is a later refactor.
