### 3. Communication
Score: ___
Notes: [e.g., "Responds to texts within 30 minutes. Proactively flagged the subfloor issue on the Olson job before proceeding." / "Hard to reach — often takes 2–3 texts before a response."]

---

### 4. Site Cleanliness
Score: ___
Notes: [e.g., "Leaves clean — one issue in March where tile debris was left in the driveway but was corrected when flagged." / "Consistently leaves sites in excellent condition."]

---

### 5. Client Interaction
Score: ___
Notes: [e.g., "Had a side conversation with the Henderson client about pricing — was corrected. No repeat of this." / "Professional, pleasant — clients often mention him by name in reviews."]

---

## Any Incidents or Complaints Involving This Sub This Period:
[e.g., "None." / "One client complaint (Torres job) — client felt he was dismissive of her questions. We addressed it directly with the sub at the time."]

---

## Insurance Expiry Date:
[e.g., November 30, 2025]

---

## Reviewer Name:
[e.g., Marci — Owner, Handy Pioneers]

---

## Prompt for AI:
Copy and paste the following into the AI chat window, then paste your filled data below it:

> Using the 17_Sub_Performance_Score workflow, produce a subcontractor performance review including the score table, written recommendation, and verbal feedback notes per criterion. Apply the KEEP/COACH/REMOVE threshold from Instructions.md. Here is the data:
>
> [PASTE YOUR FILLED DATA HERE]

docs.body:
# 17_Sub_Performance_Score — Instructions

## AI Role
Score a subcontractor across 5 criteria based on input provided by the owner or PM, then produce a written recommendation and specific feedback notes per criterion. This is an internal document used to maintain a high-performing sub roster.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## Review Schedule
Sub reviews happen twice per year:
- **January** — covers July–December of prior year
- **July** — covers January–June of current year

## Scoring System

### 5 Criteria — Score Each 1 to 5

| Criterion | Description |
|---|---|
| 1. Work Quality | Is the finished product acceptable? Does it require callbacks? Does it meet HP's standard? |
| 2. Reliability / Punctuality | Do they show up when scheduled? Do they communicate if there's a delay? Do they complete on the agreed timeline? |
| 3. Communication | Do they respond to calls/texts promptly? Do they ask the right questions upfront? Do they flag issues proactively? |
| 4. Site Cleanliness | Do they leave the site clean? Do they protect the client's property? Do they take materials when they leave? |
| 5. Client Interaction | When they interact with the client directly, do they represent HP well? Professional demeanor, respectful, no side conversations about price or scope? |

### Scoring Guide
- 5 = Excellent — consistently above expectation
- 4 = Good — meets expectation with minor room for improvement
- 3 = Acceptable — mostly meets expectation, but there's a pattern worth noting
- 2 = Below expectation — recurring issue in this area
- 1 = Poor — this criterion is a problem

---

## Scoring Thresholds

| Total Score | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| 20–25 | KEEP — Active roster, no action required |
| 13–19 | COACH — Address gaps, set expectations, re-evaluate in 90 days |
| Below 13 | REMOVE — Off-roster; do not dispatch to HP jobs |

---

## Output: Score Table + Written Recommendation

### Score Table
Format as a clean table:

| Criterion | Score (1–5) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Work Quality | X | [1–2 sentence summary from data provided] |
| Reliability / Punctuality | X | [...] |
| Communication | X | [...] |
| Site Cleanliness | X | [...] |
| Client Interaction | X | [...] |
| **TOTAL** | **X / 25** | **[KEEP / COACH / REMOVE]** |

---

### Written Recommendation
One paragraph (5–8 sentences) covering:
- Overall assessment
- What this sub does well
- What needs to change (if COACH or REMOVE)
- Specific feedback the owner can deliver verbally
- If REMOVE: state it plainly — no hedging

---

### Feedback Notes Per Criterion
For each criterion, one brief bullet summarizing what the sub should hear (if applicable). Written in plain language the owner can use verbally. Not a formal review form — talking points.

---

## Tone Notes
- Factual, not punitive
- For KEEP: affirm specifically, not generically ("Jason is reliable — has not missed a scheduled date in 6 jobs" not "He does a good job")
- For COACH: specific and constructive — "The pattern is communication on day-of delays. Set the expectation that a text by 7 AM is required if he's going to be late."
- For REMOVE: Direct. No hedging. "Based on this review, we recommend removing this sub from the active roster."

## Linked Process Map
HP_Sub_Performance_Review

# 17_Sub_Performance_Score — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Pull job history for the sub | PM or owner | CRM / job tracker |
| Score each criterion | Owner | This file — Data_Template |
| Generate review document | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Review output | Owner | — |
| Deliver verbal feedback | Owner | In person (call or meeting) |
| Update sub roster | Owner or PM | 02_Subcontractors/ roster |
| File review document | PM | 02_Subcontractors/Performance_Reviews/ |

---

## Review Schedule

| Review Window | Period Covered |
|---|---|
| January | July–December (prior year) |
| July | January–June (current year) |

Set a recurring calendar reminder: January 5 and July 5 — "Run sub performance reviews."

---

## File Storage
Save each completed review as:
`02_Subcontractors/Performance_Reviews/[YYYY-MM]_[SubName]_PerformanceReview.pdf`

---

## Roster Actions by Recommendation

| Recommendation | Action |
|---|---|
| KEEP | No roster change — sub remains active |
| COACH | Verbal feedback delivered by owner. Set a 90-day check-in reminder. Sub remains active. If no improvement in 90 days → Re-score and consider REMOVE. |
| REMOVE | Remove from active roster. Do not dispatch on future jobs. If re-engagement is ever considered, re-evaluate from scratch — no automatic reinstatement. |

---

## Insurance Tracking
Review every sub's insurance expiry date during the review. If a sub's insurance expires within 90 days:
- Flag in CRM
- Request updated certificate before dispatching again

If insurance is expired and the sub is active — suspend from roster immediately until updated.

---

## Verbal Feedback Delivery
The review document is internal. Do not email the scored review to the sub. Deliver feedback verbally:
- Positive points first
- Specific areas for improvement
- Clear expectation if COACH
- Firm and respectful if REMOVE

---

- 23_Sub_Onboarding_Email — for new subs coming onto the roster

docs.body:
# 18_Annual_Planning_Facilitator — Instructions

## AI Role
Structure and write the Annual Planning session outputs. Takes raw notes and numbers from the planning session and produces a clean, actionable planning document. This document gets shared with the team at the January kickoff meeting.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## When This Runs
Once per year — the planning session happens in December. The document is finalized in early January and presented at the team kickoff meeting.

## Output: 4-Section Annual Planning Document

This is the document that goes to the team. It must be clear, motivating, and specific. Not a spreadsheet — a narrative plan.

---

### Section 1 — Year-in-Review Summary

Write a brief, honest summary of the year that just closed. This is not a highlight reel — acknowledge both wins and problems. The team lived through this year too.

Structure:
- Revenue performance (actual vs. goal — brief)
- 2–3 specific wins (what worked)
- 2–3 specific problems or patterns that need fixing
- One sentence: what kind of year was it overall?

Tone: Direct, grounded, no spin. Acknowledge hard things, celebrate real wins.

Length: 3–4 paragraphs

---

### Section 2 — 3-Year Picture

A short, compelling statement of where HP is headed in 3 years. Based on the owner's vision statement from the planning session.

Structure:
- What HP looks like in 3 years (revenue target, team size, service mix, market position)
- 2–3 sentences that feel aspirational but specific — not corporate-speak
- Close with one sentence about why this matters (to the team, the clients, the owner)

Tone: Motivating but grounded. This is a compass, not a dream board.

Length: 1–2 paragraphs

---

### Section 3 — 1-Year Goals

Specific, measurable goals for the coming year. Based on what was decided in the planning session. These are not aspirations — they are commitments.

Format each goal as:
- Goal: [What we will achieve]
- Measure: [How we know we achieved it]
- Owner: [Who is accountable]

At minimum, include:
- Revenue goal
- PP client count goal
- Review count goal
- Any other goals set in the session

---

### Section 4 — Q1 Rocks

3–5 specific projects or initiatives that are the top priority for Q1. These are the things that get done before anything else. Each Rock has one owner and a due date.

Format:
```
ROCK 1: [Title — short, action-oriented]
Owner: [Name]
Due: [Date — within Q1]
What done looks like: [One sentence]

ROCK 2: ...
```

---

## Writing Instructions
- Write in a voice that sounds like HP — not like a McKinsey strategy document
- The 3-Year Picture should give the team something to work toward
- The 1-Year Goals should be specific enough that there's no debate at year-end about whether you hit them
- The Q1 Rocks should be discrete enough to complete in 90 days — if a Rock is too vague or too large, say so and ask for clarification
- This document gets read aloud at the kickoff — keep each section scannable

## Output Format

```
HANDY PIONEERS — [Year] ANNUAL PLAN
Prepared: [Date]
(Internal — Team Kickoff Document)

═══════════════════════════════════════

SECTION 1 — YEAR-IN-REVIEW
[3–4 paragraphs]

═══════════════════════════════════════

SECTION 2 — 3-YEAR PICTURE
[1–2 paragraphs]

═══════════════════════════════════════

SECTION 3 — 1-YEAR GOALS

[Goal 1]
[Goal 2]
[Goal 3]
[Additional goals]

═══════════════════════════════════════

SECTION 4 — Q1 ROCKS

[Rock 1]
[Rock 2]
[Rock 3]
[Rock 4 — optional]
[Rock 5 — optional]

═══════════════════════════════════════
```

## Linked Process Map
HP_Annual_Planning

docs.body:
# 19_Hiring_Post_Generator — Instructions

## AI Role
Write a job posting and a phone screen question set for a new HP hire. HP hires for character first, skills second. The posting must reflect the brand and attract the right kind of person — reliable, professional, community-minded, and comfortable working in people's homes.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## HP Hiring Philosophy
HP hires character first. The right person is:
- Reliable — shows up, on time, every time
- Professional in appearance and manner in clients' homes
- A communicator — not a mind-reader; asks questions, updates proactively
- Takes pride in their work — cares about the finished product
- Community-rooted — is part of Clark County, not just passing through

Skills matter, but a skilled person with bad character is a liability. A solid person with trainable gaps is an asset.

## Output: Two Deliverables

---

### Deliverable 1 — Job Posting

Structure:
1. **Role Title** — clear and searchable (e.g., "Handyman / Remodeling Technician — Clark County, WA")
2. **What the Job Actually Looks Like Day-to-Day** — be honest and specific. What does a typical week look like? Where are they working? With whom? What kinds of jobs?
3. **What HP Looks For** — go beyond the job description. What makes someone succeed here? Character traits, not just credentials.
4. **Why Work at HP** — what's the genuine appeal? Not generic "great team culture" language. What is actually true about working at Handy Pioneers?
5. **Role Details** — full-time / part-time, pay range (if authorized), start date
6. **How to Apply** — specific instructions

Tone: Reads like it was written by a real person who runs a real company, not an HR department. Confident, warm, specific. No corporate filler.

Length: 350–500 words

---

### Deliverable 2 — Phone Screen Question Set

5–7 questions that surface character, reliability, and culture fit — not just technical skill. Technical skills are tested on the job or in the interview. Phone screen is for: Are they who they say they are? Do I want to spend more time with this person?

For each question:
- Write the question
- Write a brief note on what a GOOD answer looks like and what a RED FLAG answer looks like

Questions should cover:
- Reliability and track record
- How they handle difficult situations (clients, coworkers, mistakes)
- What they're actually looking for in a job
- Their experience working in residential settings
- Any role-specific requirements (license, tools, etc.)

Format:
```
Q1: [Question]
Good answer: [What to listen for]
Red flag: [What raises concern]
```

---

## Writing Instructions

### For the Posting:
- Do not use "rockstar," "ninja," "guru," or similar clichés
- Do use specific, realistic language about the work — don't over-promise
- The "What HP Looks For" section should read like the owner wrote it personally
- If pay range is authorized to share, include it — it filters for serious applicants and is increasingly expected
- Make the application method clear and friction-free

### For the Phone Screen:
- Questions should be open-ended — not yes/no
- Avoid hypotheticals in favor of behavioral questions ("Tell me about a time when...")
- The notes on good vs. red flag answers should be actionable, not generic

## Linked Process Map
HP_Hiring_Flow
docs.title: 19_Hiring_Post_Generator — Instructions

docs.body:
# 19_Hiring_Post_Generator — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Decide to hire | Owner | — |
| Fill in Data_Template | Owner | This file |
| Write posting and phone screen | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Review and approve posting | Owner | — |
| Post on job boards | Owner or PM | Indeed, Craigslist, Facebook Jobs |
| Conduct phone screens | PM or Owner | Phone — using AI-generated questions |
| Advance candidates to in-person | Owner | Owner decision |
| Extend offer | Owner | Owner decision |

---

## Posting Platforms

| Platform | Notes |
|---|---|
| Indeed (indeed.com) | Paid promotion optional — free posts get results for trade roles |
| Craigslist (vancouver.craigslist.org) | Still active for trade jobs in Clark County — use the "Skilled Trade" category |
| Facebook Jobs | Free — reaches local Clark County audience. Post on HP's business page. |
| Word of mouth | Ask current crew and subs — best candidates often come through referrals |

---

## Phone Screen Process
1. Candidate applies / reaches out
2. PM or owner sends a brief "Thanks, we'd like to do a quick 15-minute phone call" response
3. Schedule a time (don't do unscheduled screens — they're too rushed)
4. Use the AI-generated question set during the call
5. After the call: score the candidate (1–10 gut instinct), note any red flags
6. Owner decides: advance to in-person or pass

---

## In-Person Interview
Phone screen is a filter, not a full evaluation. If the candidate passes:
- In-person or job-site interview
- Show them what the work looks like
- Ask 2–3 deeper questions based on phone screen
- If applicable: a brief skills demonstration (simple repair task or walkthrough)

---

## Offer and Onboarding
After hire decision:
- Verbal offer by owner
- Written offer letter (see HR files)
- Background check if required by HP policy
- Onboarding orientation before first day on site

---

## Linked Process Map
HP_Hiring_Flow

docs.body:
# 20_PP_Upsell_Script — Instructions

## AI Role
Write a personalized Proactive Path (360 Plan) pitch for a specific client based on their job history and property situation. The pitch is delivered at job closeout — in person or via text follow-up. It should feel like a natural next step in a conversation, not a sales pitch.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## What the Proactive Path Is
The Proactive Path 360 Plan is HP's ongoing maintenance membership. Members get:
- Proactive, scheduled seasonal visits (HP reaches out — the client doesn't have to remember)
- Priority scheduling when they need work done
- A dedicated PM who knows their home
- Peace of mind that their home isn't quietly falling apart

The PP is not a subscription they forget — it's a relationship that saves them time, money, and the mental load of home maintenance management.

## When to Deliver This Pitch
At job closeout — when the work is done, the client is happy, and there's natural goodwill in the room. This is the best moment. Strike while the relationship is warm.

If the client isn't present at closeout, or the timing is off, use the follow-up text version.

---

## Output: Two Deliverables

---

### Deliverable 1 — In-Person Talking Points (Closeout)
Not a script — talking points. The PM speaks naturally. These are prompts to guide the conversation.

Structure:
1. **Transition line** — move from "job is done" to "there's something else I want to mention"
2. **Reference the home** — acknowledge what you saw during the job that's relevant to ongoing maintenance (personalized, not generic)
3. **Describe the PP** — what it is, how it works, what the client gets. One paragraph max. Plain language.
4. **The ask** — soft, open-ended. "Would that be something worth learning more about?" or "It might be worth a 5-minute conversation if you're open to it."
5. **If they want more info** — have the PP packet ready (digital or physical)

Tone: Conversational, not salesy. This is one neighbor talking to another about something that could genuinely help.

---

### Deliverable 2 — Follow-Up Text (If No Decision at Closeout)
Used when:
- The client wasn't home at closeout
- They were non-committal ("I'll think about it")
- PM decided the timing wasn't right in person

Format:
- Under 300 characters (text-friendly)
- Reference the job just completed
- Brief mention of the PP
- Soft close — no pressure
- No links or long URLs in the text itself

---

## Writing Instructions
- Reference the client's specific property, job type, and what maintenance needs were visible — this is what makes it feel personal
- The pitch should feel earned — they just saw HP do good work; the PM is extending an offer to make their life easier
- Do not use the word "upsell" anywhere in the client-facing content
- Do not list pricing in either deliverable — the PP packet handles pricing
- If the client is "reserved" or "busy" per the engagement level input, keep the talking points shorter and less detailed
- If the client was "chatty," the talking points can be more conversational and expansive

## Linked Process Map
HP_PP_Upsell | HP_PP_Client_Welcome
docs.title: 20_PP_Upsell_Script — Instructions

docs.body:
# 20_PP_Upsell_Script — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Flag job as PP candidate (pre-job) | PM | 16_Job_Dispatch_Brief (PP flag) |
| Fill in Data_Template at closeout | PM | This file — on-site or right after |
| Generate talking points and follow-up text | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Deliver pitch in person | PM | Talking points from AI output |
| Send follow-up text (if needed) | PM | Phone / SMS |
| Send PP packet if interested | PM or owner | Digital or physical PP packet |
| Log in CRM | PM | Tag: "PP Prospect" |
| Route to onboarding if they sign | PM | 07_PP_Welcome_Packet workflow |

---

## PP Packet
Have the PP packet ready at closeout — digital or printed. If the client says "tell me more," you hand it to them.

PP packet should include:
- What the 360 Plan covers
- How HP manages their home proactively
- Pricing tiers (if applicable)
- Signature page or link to agreement

If HP doesn't have a PP packet, create one. This workflow assumes it exists.

---

## CRM Actions

| Event | CRM Action |
|---|---|
| PP pitch delivered | Tag "PP Prospect" — log date |
| Client takes the packet | Log "PP Packet Delivered" |
| Client says "not interested" | Log "PP Declined — [date]" — do not pursue further |
| Client signs | Route to 07_PP_Welcome_Packet — update status to "Active PP Client" |

---

## Timing

| Action | When |
|---|---|
| Generate talking points | Day before or morning of closeout |
| Deliver pitch | At job closeout (in person) |
| Send follow-up text | Same day as closeout if no decision, or next morning |
| Follow up again | One more touch in 7 days if no response — then let it rest |

---

## What Not to Do
- Do not call and pitch PP out of the blue — this pitch only works when it follows a completed job
- Do not leave the PP pitch until after the invoice is paid — do it at closeout while the relationship is warm
- Do not pressure the client if they decline — log it and move on. They may say yes after the next job.

---

## Linked Process Map
HP_PP_Upsell | HP_PP_Client_Welcome

## Linked Workflows
- 07_PP_Welcome_Packet — triggered if the client signs
- 16_Job_Dispatch_Brief — the PP flag in this brief is the trigger for this workflow
- 11_Past_Client_Win_Back — if the client was a past one-time client being reactivated

docs.body:
# 21_Incident_Report_Writer — Instructions

## AI Role
Produce a formal incident report document after a safety incident on a job site. This is an internal record — not a client communication, not a legal filing, not an insurance claim form. It must be factual, specific, and written without any admission of liability or causal conclusions.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## When This Is Used
Any time an incident occurs on a job site that involves:
- Personal injury (crew, client, or third party) — any severity
- Property damage (client's property or third-party)
- Near-miss events that could have caused injury or damage
- Equipment failure or tool-related incidents

Document immediately. Do not wait until the end of the day. Memory fades and details matter.

## Critical Rules for the Report

1. **Factual only** — describe what happened, not why or who is at fault
2. **No admission of liability** — do not write "we caused" or "our negligence" — describe events neutrally
3. **No interpretation** — "The subfloor was wet" not "the subfloor was wet because the client failed to disclose a leak"
4. **Specific** — times, names, measurements, descriptions — not vague ("around noon," "some damage")
5. **Root cause is preliminary** — label it "preliminary root cause" — it is not a finding

---

## Output: Formal Incident Report

```
INCIDENT REPORT
Handy Pioneers — Internal Document
(Not for public distribution without owner authorization)

Report Prepared By: [Name and role]
Report Date: [Today's date]
Report Number: IR-[YYYY]-[sequential number]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 1 — INCIDENT SUMMARY

Date of Incident: [Date]
Time of Incident: [Approximate time — e.g., "Approximately 10:30 AM"]
Location / Job Site: [Full address]
Brief Description: [2–3 sentences — who, what, when, where. Factual only.]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 2 — IMMEDIATE ACTIONS TAKEN

[List the actions taken immediately after the incident — in chronological order]
- e.g., Work stopped immediately
- e.g., First aid administered by [name]
- e.g., 911 called at [time] / Not called
- e.g., Client notified at [time]
- e.g., Owner notified at [time]
- e.g., Site secured

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 3 — PEOPLE INVOLVED

[List each person involved — name, role, whether they were injured]

Name: [Full name]
Role: [e.g., HP Crew Member / Subcontractor / Client / Third Party]
Injury: [Yes — describe / No]
Medical attention required: [Yes — describe / No]

[Repeat for each person]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 4 — PROPERTY / EQUIPMENT INVOLVED

[Describe any property or equipment involved in the incident]

Client property affected: [Yes — describe / No]
Equipment involved: [Yes — describe / No]
Estimated extent of damage: [Brief factual description — do not speculate on dollar amount]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 5 — PRELIMINARY ROOT CAUSE

Note: This is a preliminary assessment based on available information. It is not a finding or determination of fault.

[Brief factual description of what conditions or sequence of events preceded the incident, based on what is currently known]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 6 — CORRECTIVE ACTIONS REQUIRED

[List specific actions that need to happen as a result of this incident]
- e.g., Review tool maintenance procedures with crew
- e.g., Establish new protocol for [specific situation]
- e.g., Inspect all similar equipment before next use
- e.g., Review site assessment process for similar job types

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 7 — FOLLOW-UP ITEMS

[List outstanding items — who owns them and by when]

Item: [Description]
Owner: [Name]
Due: [Date]

[Repeat as needed]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SIGNATURES

Prepared by: _____________________________ Date: ___________
Owner Review: _____________________________ Date: ___________

─────────────────────────────────────────
```

---

## Writing Instructions
- Use passive voice when describing the incident to avoid implying fault ("the tool fell" not "Jason dropped the tool")
- Do not name third-party witnesses or include their contact info in the report body — maintain privacy
- If emergency services were called, note the call time and who called, but do not quote what was said
- Corrective actions should be specific and actionable — not "be more careful"

## Linked Process Map
HP_Safety_Incident
docs.title: 21_Incident_Report_Writer — Instructions

# 21_Incident_Report_Writer — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Fill in Data_Template immediately after incident | Owner or PM on site | This file (phone or paper) |
| Generate formal report | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Review for accuracy | Owner | — |
| Sign report | Owner | Physical or digital signature |
| File report | Owner or PM | 07_Legal_HR/Company_Policies/ or job folder |
| Notify insurance (if claim) | Owner | Insurance carrier contact |
| Complete corrective actions | Owner or PM | Per report Section 6 |
| Follow up on outstanding items | Owner or PM | Per report Section 7 |

---

## Timing

| Action | When |
|---|---|
| Fill Data_Template | Immediately — same day as incident |
| Generate report | Same day |
| Owner review | Same day |
| Insurance notification (if applicable) | Within 24 hours of incident |
| Corrective actions completed | Per due dates in report |

---

## File Storage

Save completed report as:
`07_Legal_HR/Company_Policies/Incident_Reports/IR-[YYYY]-[###]_[JobSite].pdf`

Also file a copy in the relevant job folder if the incident occurred on a client job.

Keep all incident reports indefinitely — do not delete.

---

## Insurance Protocol

If the incident involves any of the following, notify HP's insurance carrier immediately:
- Any crew member injury (regardless of severity)
- Any client property damage
- Any emergency services call
- Any near-miss involving a client or third party

Contact information for HP's insurance carrier:
[Insert carrier name, phone, and policy number here — owner to fill in]

Do NOT make any written or verbal commitments to the client about damage repair or compensation before speaking with insurance.

---

## If a Crew Member Was Injured

Washington State requirements:
- Workers' compensation claim may be required — file with L&I (lni.wa.gov) if the employee seeks medical treatment
- Employee may self-report to L&I — employer must not discourage this
- Retain the incident report as part of the claim record

For subcontractors: the sub's own workers' comp and liability insurance apply. Confirm their coverage is current.

---

docs.body:
# 22_Project_Closeout_Checklist — Instructions

## AI Role
Generate a job-specific closeout checklist for the crew to use before leaving a job site. The checklist combines standard items with job-specific items based on the scope provided. The output is a printable, one-page checklist the crew physically completes and signs off before departing.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## When This Is Generated
The PM or lead tech generates this checklist before the job starts or morning of. The crew uses it on-site and completes it before leaving. It is a checklist — not a punch list. It ensures nothing is left undone or forgotten.

---

## Output: Project Closeout Checklist

Generate a checklist under 6 standard sections, with job-specific items woven into the relevant sections based on the scope and special notes provided.

---

### Section 1 — Site Cleanup

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] All debris removed from work area
- [ ] All materials and packaging disposed of or taken off-site
- [ ] Drop cloths or floor protection removed
- [ ] Tools and equipment removed from site
- [ ] Any borrowed or client-provided items returned to their original location
- [ ] Dumpster or haul-away arranged if needed

Job-specific items (insert based on scope — examples):
- [ ] Tile dust vacuumed and mopped from bathroom floor (tile job)
- [ ] Paint drips cleaned from adjacent surfaces (painting job)
- [ ] Wood chips and cut material cleared from deck (deck job)

---

### Section 2 — Work Quality Verification

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] Scope of work completed as described in the contract
- [ ] No visible defects in the finished work
- [ ] All fasteners, fixtures, and finishes are secure
- [ ] No damage to adjacent surfaces or areas caused during the job

Job-specific items (insert based on scope — examples):
- [ ] Grout lines are consistent and fully filled (tile job)
- [ ] Paint coverage is even — no visible roller marks, missed spots (painting job)
- [ ] Deck boards are level and secure — no raised fasteners (deck job)
- [ ] Drywall texture matches surrounding wall (drywall job)

---

### Section 3 — Photo Documentation

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] "After" photos taken of the completed work
- [ ] Photos show the full scope (multiple angles)
- [ ] Photos uploaded to job folder or sent to PM

Job-specific items (based on before/after documentation needs):
- [ ] Before and after photos taken (if before photos were captured at start)

---

### Section 4 — Client Walk-Through

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] Client notified that work is complete
- [ ] Walk-through completed with client (if client is present)
- [ ] Client questions or concerns noted and addressed
- [ ] Client satisfaction confirmed before crew departs
- [ ] If client is not present: PM notified, walk-through to be scheduled

---

### Section 5 — Invoice and CRM

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] Invoice generated or confirmed as sent
- [ ] Invoice amount matches the signed contract (plus any signed COs)
- [ ] PM notified of job completion for CRM update
- [ ] Job status updated to "Complete — Pending Payment" in CRM

---

### Section 6 — PP Flag

Standard items (always include):
- [ ] Is this client a PP (Proactive Path) candidate? [Yes / No / Already a member]
- [ ] If Yes: PP pitch delivered at closeout OR follow-up text to be sent today
- [ ] If Already a member: PP visit notes logged for PM review

---

## Formatting Rules
- Format as a printable checklist with checkboxes
- One item per line
- Keep the document to one page when printed
- Include job header at the top (client, address, date)
- Include a crew sign-off line at the bottom

## Output Format

```
PROJECT CLOSEOUT CHECKLIST
Handy Pioneers

Client: [Name]
Address: [Address]
Job Type: [Scope summary]
Date: [Date]
Crew: [Names]

─────────────────────────────────────────

SECTION 1 — SITE CLEANUP
[Standard + job-specific items]

SECTION 2 — WORK QUALITY VERIFICATION
[Standard + job-specific items]

SECTION 3 — PHOTO DOCUMENTATION
[Standard + job-specific items]

SECTION 4 — CLIENT WALK-THROUGH
[Standard items]

SECTION 5 — INVOICE AND CRM
[Standard items]

SECTION 6 — PP FLAG
[Standard items]

─────────────────────────────────────────

CREW SIGN-OFF
Lead Tech: _____________________________ Date: ___________
PM Notified: [ ] Yes — Time: ___________
```

## Linked Process Map
HP_Project_Closeout
docs.title: 22_Project_Closeout_Checklist — Instructions

docs.body:
# 22_Project_Closeout_Checklist — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Fill in Data_Template | PM | This file |
| Generate checklist | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Print or send to crew | PM | Print / Text |
| Complete checklist on-site | Lead tech | Physical checklist |
| Sign off and note PM notification | Lead tech | Checklist sign-off block |
| Update CRM | PM | After receiving crew sign-off |
| Deliver invoice | PM or crew | Email or in-person |

---

## Timing

| Action | When |
|---|---|
| Generate checklist | Day before job or morning of — must be before crew leaves |
| Crew completes checklist | On-site, before departing |
| Crew signs off and texts PM | Before leaving the job site |
| PM updates CRM | Same day as job completion |
| Invoice sent | Same day as job completion (or within 24 hours) |

---

## Delivery Options for the Checklist
1. **Print:** Print the checklist and send with the crew — they complete it on-site
2. **Text/email:** Send as a text attachment or email to the lead tech's phone — they reference it digitally
3. **Notes app:** Paste into a shared notes app if HP uses one for crew communication

---

## After the Job

### CRM Update Required
- Update job status to: "Complete — Pending Payment"
- Log job completion date
- Log whether client walk-through was completed
- Log PP flag status and whether pitch was delivered

### Invoice
- Confirm the invoice was sent same day
- Log invoice number and amount in CRM
- Set a 7-day follow-up reminder if payment is not received

### Photos
- Confirm "after" photos were taken
- Upload to the job folder (or Google Drive / Dropbox if HP uses cloud storage)
- Tag photos with client name and date

---

## Checklist Archive
The closeout checklist is a disposable field document — it doesn't need to be filed long-term. However:
- If the client later disputes the quality of work, the completed checklist (with crew sign-off) is valuable documentation
- If there was a complaint or callback, retain the checklist with the incident record

---

## Linked Process Map
HP_Project_Closeout

## Linked Workflows
- 16_Job_Dispatch_Brief — the brief is the pre-job counterpart to this checklist
- 20_PP_Upsell_Script — PP pitch happens at this closeout
- 21_Incident_Report_Writer — if anything went wrong during the job

docs.title: 23_Sub_Onboarding_Email — Data Template

docs.body:
# 23_Sub_Onboarding_Email — Instructions

## AI Role
Write the welcome and next-steps email sent to a newly approved subcontractor after their trial job is complete and they are added to the HP active roster. This email is professional, clear, and welcoming — it sets expectations without overwhelming the new sub with information.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## When This Is Sent
After a subcontractor completes a trial job and is approved for the active roster. The PM or owner sends this email — it's the official "you're in" communication.

## What This Email Is — and Is Not
- IS: A professional welcome, a clear overview of how things work, and an expression of genuine appreciation for the relationship
- IS NOT: A legal agreement (that's handled separately), a complete vendor manual, or a list of every rule

The sub agreement covers legal and liability. This email covers the working relationship.

---

## Output: Onboarding Email

### Email Structure

1. **Welcome** — Acknowledge they've been added to the HP roster. Make it feel like recognition, not just a notification. Reference the trial job briefly (that they did well, that's why they're here).

2. **How Jobs Are Dispatched** — Explain exactly how they'll receive job invitations: by text, call, or app (based on the data provided). What information they'll receive. What the expected response window is. (HP's standard: respond within [2 hours / same business day — owner to confirm and insert]).

3. **What HP Expects** — Keep this to 3–4 bullet points. Not an exhaustive list — just the things that matter most:
   - Insurance must remain current (they cannot be dispatched if expired)
   - Communication SLA — respond to texts/calls within [X hours]
   - Site standards — leave the site the way you found it or better; protect the client's property
   - Any specific expectation to emphasize based on their trial (pulled from data)

4. **Who to Contact** — Name and preferred contact method for their day-to-day HP point of contact (PM). For questions about pay or agreements, who to contact.

5. **Close** — Brief, genuine. HP is selective about who's on the roster — express that this sub earned their spot.

---

## Tone Notes
- Professional but not cold — this is a working relationship, not a vendor contract
- No legal language in the email body — that's in the agreement
- Don't list every possible scenario — keep it brief and actionable
- The email should take under 3 minutes to read

## Output Format

```
SUBJECT LINE:
[e.g., "Welcome to the HP Roster, [Sub Name]"]

EMAIL BODY:
[Structured as per the 5-section outline above]
[200–300 words]

SIGNATURE:
[PM or owner name]
Handy Pioneers
help@handypioneers.com
handypioneers.com
Clark County, WA
```

## Linked Process Map
HP_Sub_Onboarding
docs.title: 23_Sub_Onboarding_Email — Instructions

---

## CRM / Roster Actions

- [ ] Update sub record status → **Active Roster**

## File Storage for Sub Documents

All new sub documents filed in:
`02_Subcontractors/Sub_Onboarding/[SubName]/`

Required documents in this folder:
- [ ] Signed sub agreement
- [ ] Current certificate of insurance (COI)
- [ ] W-9 (for 1099 filing)
- [ ] Any trade license if applicable

If any of these are missing, do not activate the sub on the roster until they are received.

---

## Insurance Tracking

HP cannot dispatch a sub without current insurance. Process:
1. Log expiry date in CRM at onboarding
2. Set a 60-day advance reminder
3. At 60 days: request updated COI from sub
4. If not received before expiry: suspend from dispatch until updated
5. Never accept a verbal "it's renewed" — require the actual COI

---

## Sub Trial Process (Before This Workflow)
This email is sent AFTER the trial is complete. The trial process:
1. Sub submits to HP (referral, Indeed, etc.)
2. Owner or PM screens (phone call)
3. Sub reviews and signs the sub agreement before first job
4. HP assigns a trial job (lower-risk, owner or PM oversees more closely)
5. PM evaluates trial job performance
6. Owner decides: add to roster or pass
7. If added → send this email

---

## Linked Process Map
HP_Sub_Onboarding

## Linked Workflows
- 17_Sub_Performance_Score — first formal review happens 6 months after onboarding

docs.body:
# 24_Seasonal_Schedule_Planner — Instructions

## AI Role
Build a quarterly outreach and scheduling plan for a seasonal push. Takes current capacity, backlog, and campaign targets and produces a one-page, actionable plan the owner can work from. Format as a simple calendar/table — not a narrative.

## Global HP Rules
- Never expose hard costs, markup multipliers, or internal labor rates
- Minimum GM: 30% standard, 40% if hard cost under $2,000
- Brand voice: professional, reliable, straightforward — confident but approachable
- Soft CTAs only, never high-pressure
- Service area: Clark County, WA
- handypioneers.com | help@handypioneers.com

## When This Is Run
At the start of each quarter, or 6–8 weeks before a seasonal campaign launches. This plan drives the 12_Seasonal_Promotion_Copy campaign.

## Output: 3-Part Seasonal Plan

---

### Part 1 — Outreach Target Summary

Who to contact and what to offer them, by segment:

| Segment | Outreach Type | Services to Offer | Volume (approx.) |
|---|---|---|---|
| PP clients needing seasonal service | Proactive outreach (call or text) | [Services based on data] | [PP count from data] |
| Past one-time clients (12+ months inactive) | Win-back campaign | [Seasonal services] | [Estimate based on data] |
| New leads (campaign-driven) | GBP + Facebook + Email campaign | [Services from data] | [Target from data] |
| Geographic focus (if specified) | Local outreach / GBP geo targeting | [Services] | — |

Brief written note (2–3 sentences) on the priority segment for this season and why.

---

### Part 2 — Week-by-Week Scheduling Recommendation

Based on current capacity (jobs per week) and backlog, lay out a 13-week quarterly calendar that shows:
- Which weeks are reserved for campaign-driven bookings
- Which weeks are pre-filled with PP client seasonal visits
- Where buffer weeks should be (every 4–5 weeks recommended)
- Campaign launch week and mid-season check week

Format:

```
WEEK  |  DATES          |  CAPACITY (jobs)  |  PRIORITY / NOTES
------|-----------------|-------------------|-----------------------------
  1   |  Feb 3 – Feb 7  |  X jobs available |  Campaign launch week — hold 2 slots for new leads
  2   |  Feb 10 – Feb 14|  X jobs available |  PP seasonal visits — [PP client list here]
  3   |  Feb 17 – Feb 21|  X jobs available |  Open for new bookings
  ...
```

Adjust based on actual capacity and backlog provided. If backlog is heavy (2+ weeks), note that and recommend delaying the campaign launch.

---

### Part 3 — Key Dates

A simple reference list:

| Milestone | Date |
|---|---|
| Campaign launch (12_Seasonal_Promotion_Copy goes live) | [Date] |
| PP seasonal outreach begins | [Date] |
| Win-back campaign texts sent | [Date] |
| Mid-season capacity check | [Date — approx. 6 weeks in] |
| Season close / last booking date for this push | [Date] |
| Post-season review (how did we do?) | [Date] |

---

## Writing Instructions
- This is a planning tool — keep it scannable and actionable
- Do not pad with narrative — bullet points and tables only
- If backlog is more than 3 weeks, flag it prominently: "CAUTION: Current backlog may limit campaign intake — recommend soft launch or delayed start"
- The week-by-week plan should be realistic — don't schedule beyond actual capacity
- PP clients take scheduling priority over new leads — reflect this in the week-by-week plan

## Output Format

```
SEASONAL SCHEDULE PLAN — [Season / Quarter / Year]
Prepared: [Date]

═══════════════════════════════════════

PART 1 — OUTREACH TARGETS
[Table + brief note]

═══════════════════════════════════════

PART 2 — WEEK-BY-WEEK CAPACITY PLAN
[13-week table]

═══════════════════════════════════════

PART 3 — KEY DATES
[Table]

═══════════════════════════════════════
```

## Linked Process Map
HP_Seasonal_Scheduling

## Linked Workflow
12_Seasonal_Promotion_Copy — this plan drives the campaign content
docs.title: 24_Seasonal_Schedule_Planner — Instructions

# 24_Seasonal_Schedule_Planner — Tools

## Who Does What

| Task | Owner | Tool / Platform |
|---|---|---|
| Fill in Data_Template | Owner | This file |
| Generate seasonal plan | AI (Claude or ChatGPT) | Prompt from Data_Template |
| Review and adjust plan | Owner | — |
| Post plan in weekly huddle tracker | PM | 14_Weekly_Huddle_Prep |
| Launch PP seasonal outreach | PM | CRM — pull PP client list |
| Launch win-back campaign | Owner or PM | 11_Past_Client_Win_Back workflow |
| Launch marketing campaign | Owner | 12_Seasonal_Promotion_Copy workflow |
| Mid-season check | Owner | Halfway through the quarter — check capacity vs. bookings |

---

## Timing

| Action | When |
|---|---|
| Fill Data_Template | 6–8 weeks before campaign launch |
| AI generates plan | Same week as data fill |
| Owner reviews and approves | Within 3 days |
| Plan posted in huddle tracker | Before the quarter starts |
| PP outreach begins | Week 1 of the plan |
| Marketing campaign launches | Campaign launch date from Part 3 |
| Mid-season check | Week 6 or 7 |

---

## Coordination with Weekly Huddle
Post the Key Dates table (Part 3) in the weekly huddle document at the start of the quarter. Review progress against the plan every huddle.

---

## Capacity Check — Warning Thresholds

| Backlog | Recommended Action |
|---|---|
| Under 1 week | Aggressive campaign — hold plenty of slots for new leads |
| 1–2 weeks | Standard campaign — balance PP visits and new leads |
| 2–3 weeks | Soft launch — PP clients first, limit new-lead intake |
| 3+ weeks | Delay campaign launch — focus on clearing backlog first |

This plan assumes backlog data is accurate. If crew capacity changes mid-season, re-run the plan with updated numbers.

---

## Linked Workflows
- 12_Seasonal_Promotion_Copy — the marketing campaign content that this plan drives
- 11_Past_Client_Win_Back — the win-back campaign that runs alongside seasonal outreach
- 07_PP_Welcome_Packet — if seasonal outreach converts a prospect to PP
- 14_Weekly_Huddle_Prep — Key Dates live here throughout the quarter
- 18_Annual_Planning_Facilitator — seasonal marketing calendar dates go into the annual plan

# Handy Pioneers — AI Workflows
**Handy Pioneers · Clark County, WA · Internal Use Only · May 2026**

---

## What This Folder Is

This is the **portable operating layer** for Handy Pioneers AI workflows.

Every task file here is plain markdown — no proprietary format, no vendor lock-in.
Paste any `task_[name].md` into Claude, ChatGPT, or any AI tool and it runs identically.

The **live engine** is inside the HP Estimator app (pro.handypioneers.com → Agent section).
The app reads these same workflows and executes them via the built-in agent runtime.
This folder is the source of truth. The app is the execution layer.

---

## Folder Structure

```
AI_Workflows/
├── README.md                          ← This file
├── 01_Estimating/                     ← Live agent: Project Estimator sub-team
├── 02_Field_Operations/               ← Live agent: AI Dispatch + AI QA
├── 03_Finance/                        ← Live agent: AI Bookkeeping + Margin Monitor
└── 04_Marketing/                      ← Live agent: AI Content/SEO + Community/Reviews
```

Each domain folder follows the same pattern:
```
[Domain]/
├── task_[name].md        ← Paste into any AI to run the task
├── data_refs.md          ← Pointers to live data files and folders
├── outputs/              ← Saved outputs (auto-saved by app, or save manually)
└── skills/
    ├── prompts/          ← System context this domain uses (internal rules, rates)
    └── subtasks/         ← Edge-case or nested task prompts
```

---

## Role → Seat Mapping (Accountability Chart · May 2026)

| Role | Person / AI | Seat ID | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| VISIONARY | Marcin | visionary | Vision · Strategy · Spend approval · Hard stops |
| INTEGRATOR | AI Integrator | `integrator` | Holds P&L · Runs daily brief · Cross-dept coordination |
| SALES / ESTIMATING | AI Project Estimator | `ai_project_estimator_*` | Lead → estimate → signed agreement |
| OPERATIONS | AI Dispatch | `ai_dispatch` | Job scheduling · Crew assignment · Closeout |
| OPERATIONS QA | AI QA | `ai_qa` | Sign-off · Punch list · Invoice gate |
| FINANCE | AI Bookkeeping | `ai_bookkeeping` | Invoicing · Reconciliation · Margin monitoring |
| MARGIN | AI Margin Monitor | `ai_margin_monitor` | Hard stop < 30% GM · Weekly margin report |
| MARKETING | AI Content/SEO | `ai_content_seo` | Organic content · Blog · SEO |
| MARKETING | AI Community/Reviews | `ai_community_reviews` | Reviews · Reputation · Referrals |
| CUSTOMER | Customer Experience Lead | `cx_lead` | Human gate for all customer-facing comms |
| FIELD | Internal Tradesmen | `internal_tradesmen` | Field execution · Photos · Sign-off |
| FIELD | External Contractor Network | `external_contractor_network` | Specialty subs · Electrical · Plumbing · HVAC |

**Communication Rule:** All customer-facing output is DRAFT ONLY until Customer Experience Lead approves.
All spend > $500 routes to Marcin.

---

## Escalation Logic (used in all task prompts)

| Trigger | Escalate To |
|---------|-------------|
| Estimate gross margin < 30% | HARD STOP — blocked from sending |
| Estimate gross margin 30–40% | Soft flag → Marcin reviews |
| Job actuals trending below 30% GM | Alert PM + Marcin immediately |
| Change order > 10% of original job value | PM written approval required |
| Sub contract > $1,000 | PM approval before sending |
| Invoice overdue > 21 days | Marcin — collections risk |
| 30-day projected cash < $10,000 | Immediate alert to Marcin |
| Negative review (1–2 stars) | Immediate alert to Marcin + CX Lead — NEVER auto-post |
| Customer complaint | CX Lead responds within 1 business hour |
| Safety incident on job | Marcin same day |
| New market / expansion decision | Marcin — strategic fork |

---

## Margin Rules (enforced by AI Margin Monitor — INTERNAL ONLY)

| Rule | Value |
|------|-------|
| Minimum gross margin — standard jobs | 30% |
| Minimum gross margin — small jobs (< $2,000 hard cost) | 40% |
| Internal labor rate (cost) | $100/hr |
| Customer billing rate (labor) | $150/hr |
| Subcontractor markup | cost × 1.5 |
| Materials markup | cost × 1.4 (default) |

**NEVER expose these rates or multipliers to customers.**

---

## Workflows

| # | Folder | Primary Agent Seat | Trigger Cadence | App Page |
|---|--------|--------------------|-----------------|----------|
| 01 | Estimating | `ai_project_estimator_*` | Per estimate | Agent → Project Estimator |
| 02 | Field Operations | `ai_dispatch`, `ai_qa` | Per job / daily cron | Agent → Dispatch |
| 03 | Finance | `ai_bookkeeping`, `ai_margin_monitor` | Per invoice / weekly | Agent → Finance |
| 04 | Marketing | `ai_content_seo`, `ai_community_reviews` | Per trigger / weekly cron | Agent → Marketing |

---

## How to Run a Task Manually (any AI tool)

1. Open the relevant `task_[name].md`
2. Copy the prompt block
3. Fill in all `[BRACKET]` fields with real data
4. Attach any referenced files (sub quote PDF, invoice, photos)
5. Paste into Claude / ChatGPT / any AI
6. Save output to `outputs/YYYY-MM-DD_[customer]_[workflow].md`

---

## How the App Runs It (HP Estimator)

1. Open pro.handypioneers.com → Agent section
2. Select workflow from dropdown
3. App pre-fills context from active customer / estimate / job
4. Upload any attachments
5. Click Run Agent
6. Output saved to Supabase `agent_outputs` table AND to `outputs/` folder here

---

## GitHub Repos

| Repo | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| github.com/360Method/HP-Estimator-app | Staff portal + agent runtime |
| github.com/360Method/handy-pioneers-website | Marketing site (handypioneers.com) |
| github.com/360Method/hp-360-funnel | 360° enrollment funnel |

---

*Handy Pioneers · AI Workflows · Internal Use Only · May 2026*
docs.title: Handy Pioneers — AI Workflows

docs.body: # Twilio A2P 10DLC Remediation — Handy Pioneers **Campaign SID:** CM9480badec1b0bdb3749061bb5eb5879fc **Use case:** Low Volume Mixed **Rejection reason:** "Issues verifying the Call to Action (CTA)" **Date prepared:** April 24, 2026 > **Note on verification:** The Cowork sandbox cannot reach handypioneers.com (egress-blocked). I could not inspect your live Privacy Policy, Terms, or booking forms directly. The paste-ready copy below follows the current TCR/CTIA template that campaign reviewers require — it will pass regardless of what's on the pages today, as long as you paste it in and show the evidence screenshots. If you want me to actually read your live pages, add `handypioneers.com` to Settings → Capabilities → Network allowlist and I'll verify what's missing. --- ## Why TCR rejected TCR (The Campaign Registry) reviewers reject A2P 10DLC campaigns when they cannot independently verify — by visiting your public URLs — that customers are giving **explicit, informed consent** to receive SMS from you. "Issues verifying the CTA" almost always means one or more of these are missing from your public-facing pages: 1. No dedicated **SMS consent** section in the Privacy Policy (phone number collection is mentioned, but not text messaging specifically). 2. No disclosure on the **booking/contact form** that submitting a phone number opts the customer into SMS. 3. Missing required language: message types, frequency, "msg & data rates may apply," STOP/HELP instructions. 4. No visible **checkbox or affirmative consent language** tied to the phone number field. 5. Privacy Policy and Terms links not visible on the form itself. Reviewers screenshot your pages. If any of the five above are missing, it's an auto-reject. --- ## Privacy Policy additions **Paste this as a new section inside `https://handypioneers.com/privacy-policy`. Add it as its own H2 heading near the top, not buried at the bottom.** ``` ## SMS / Text Messaging Communications By providing your mobile phone number to Handy Pioneers — whether through our website booking form, contact form, baseline walkthrough request, membership signup, or by giving it to our team directly — you expressly consent to receive text messages (SMS) from Handy Pioneers at that number. **What messages you will receive:** appointment confirmations and reminders, estimate delivery and follow-up, scheduling coordination, project updates, invoice and payment notifications, customer service replies, and occasional service-related announcements. **Message frequency:** Message frequency varies based on your project activity and interactions with us. **Message and data rates:** Message and data rates may apply. Check with your mobile carrier for details. **Opt-out:** You can opt out of SMS messages at any time by replying **STOP** to any message you receive from us. After replying STOP, you will receive one final confirmation message and no further SMS messages will be sent. **Help:** Reply **HELP** to any message for assistance, or contact us at help@handypioneers.com. **Carriers:** Supported carriers include AT&T, T-Mobile, Verizon, Sprint, U.S. Cellular, and all major U.S. mobile carriers. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. **No sharing with third parties for marketing:** Handy Pioneers will not share, sell, or transfer your mobile phone number or SMS opt-in data to any third party or affiliate for their marketing or promotional purposes. Phone numbers collected for SMS consent are used solely by Handy Pioneers to communicate with you. **Privacy:** For details on how we handle your information, see this Privacy Policy. For terms governing use of our services, see our [Terms & Conditions](https://handypioneers.com/terms-and-conditions). ``` --- ## Terms & Conditions additions **Paste this as a new section inside `https://handypioneers.com/terms-and-conditions`. Add it as its own H2 heading.** ``` ## SMS / Text Messaging Terms By providing your mobile phone number to Handy Pioneers, you agree to receive text messages (SMS) from us related to your service, including appointment reminders, estimates, scheduling, project updates, and customer service replies. - **Consent:** Providing your phone number through our booking form, contact form, or to our staff constitutes your express written consent to receive SMS messages from Handy Pioneers. Consent is not a condition of purchase. - **Frequency:** Message frequency varies. - **Cost:** Message and data rates may apply. Contact your mobile carrier for specifics on your plan. - **Opt-out:** Reply **STOP** at any time to unsubscribe. You will receive one confirmation message and no further texts. - **Help:** Reply **HELP** for support, or email help@handypioneers.com. - **Data handling:** Mobile phone numbers and SMS opt-in information are never shared, sold, or transferred to third parties for marketing purposes. See our [Privacy Policy](https://handypioneers.com/privacy-policy) for full details. - **Service availability:** SMS service is available on all major U.S. carriers. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. - **Changes:** Handy Pioneers reserves the right to modify these SMS terms at any time. Continued use after modifications constitutes acceptance of the updated terms. ``` --- ## Booking / contact form additions **Add this directly below every phone number field on every form that collects a phone number** — booking page, contact page, baseline walkthrough request, membership signup, estimator intake, any embedded forms. ### Disclosure text (place immediately under the phone input) ``` By providing your phone number, you agree to receive text messages from Handy Pioneers including appointment reminders, estimates, scheduling updates, and follow-ups. Message frequency varies. Msg & data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out, HELP for help. View our Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions. ``` Both "Privacy Policy" and "Terms & Conditions" must be clickable hyperlinks pointing to: - `https://handypioneers.com/privacy-policy` - `https://handypioneers.com/terms-and-conditions` ### Checkbox (required — must be UNCHECKED by default) ```html ``` **Critical rules reviewers enforce:** - Checkbox must NOT be pre-checked. Pre-checked boxes fail TCR review. - The checkbox and disclosure must be visible **above the submit button**, not hidden in a collapsed section, tooltip, or modal. - If you use Jotform, Typeform, HubSpot, Calendly, or a similar embed, add the checkbox as a required field inside that form's builder. --- ## Screenshots Twilio will want Capture these URLs and attach them to the resubmission. Each screenshot must show the consent language clearly — zoom to 100%, include browser URL bar if possible. 1. **Privacy Policy — SMS section visible** `https://handypioneers.com/privacy-policy` Screenshot must show the full "SMS / Text Messaging Communications" heading and all required language (STOP/HELP, msg frequency, rates, no third-party sharing). 2. **Terms & Conditions — SMS section visible** `https://handypioneers.com/terms-and-conditions` Screenshot must show the full "SMS / Text Messaging Terms" heading and body. 3. **Booking form — phone field + disclosure + checkbox + submit button, all in one frame** `https://handypioneers.com/book` (or whichever URL is your primary booking page) The screenshot must show, in a single image: the phone input, the disclosure text, the unchecked consent checkbox, and the submit button. This is the most important screenshot — it's the CTA itself. 4. **Contact form — same as above** `https://handypioneers.com/contact` 5. **Any additional intake forms** — baseline walkthrough, membership, estimator intake. One screenshot each. Name files: `HP_PrivacyPolicy_SMS.png`, `HP_Terms_SMS.png`, `HP_BookingForm_Consent.png`, `HP_ContactForm_Consent.png`, etc. Upload to Twilio Console when resubmitting. --- ## Click-by-click resubmit steps 1. Log in: https://console.twilio.com 2. Left nav → **Messaging** → **Regulatory Compliance** → **A2P 10DLC** → **Campaigns** 3. Find the rejected campaign (SID `CM9480badec1b0bdb3749061bb5eb5879fc`) in the list. Status will show **Failed** or **Rejected**. 4. Click the campaign row → look for **Fix Campaign** or **Resubmit** button in the top-right of the detail view. - If no "Fix" option appears, click the campaign → **Delete Campaign** (top-right kebab menu) → confirm. Then click **Create New Campaign** and recreate with the same Use Case (Low Volume Mixed) and Brand. 5. In the **Call to Action / Message Flow** field, paste this exact text: ``` Customers opt in to SMS from Handy Pioneers in one of three ways: (1) WEBSITE BOOKING FORM at https://handypioneers.com/book — customers enter their phone number, read the SMS disclosure placed directly below the phone field, and check a required, unchecked-by-default consent checkbox labeled "I agree to receive SMS text messages from Handy Pioneers..." before submitting. (2) WEBSITE CONTACT FORM at https://handypioneers.com/contact — same flow as the booking form. (3) IN-PERSON / PHONE during a baseline walkthrough or estimate call, where our team verbally discloses that the phone number will be used for SMS appointment reminders, estimates, and follow-ups before collecting it. SMS consent language is published at: - Privacy Policy: https://handypioneers.com/privacy-policy (see "SMS / Text Messaging Communications" section) - Terms & Conditions: https://handypioneers.com/terms-and-conditions (see "SMS / Text Messaging Terms" section) Message types include appointment reminders, estimate delivery, scheduling updates, project updates, and customer service replies. Msg frequency varies. Msg & data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out, HELP for help. ``` 6. In the **Sample Messages** field, include at least 2 realistic samples. Each must include the brand name and opt-out instructions: ``` Sample 1: Hi {Name}, this is Handy Pioneers confirming your appointment on {Date} at {Time}. Reply STOP to opt out, HELP for help. Sample 2: Handy Pioneers: Your estimate for the {Project} is ready. View it here: {Link}. Reply STOP to unsubscribe. ``` 7. Confirm **Embedded Links** = Yes and **Embedded Phone Numbers** = Yes (you'll likely send both). 8. Confirm **Age-Gated Content** = No, **Direct Lending** = No, **Affiliate Marketing** = No. 9. Attach the screenshots from the section above if the UI offers a file upload field. If it doesn't, keep them on hand — TCR may request them via Twilio support ticket. 10. Click **Submit for Review**. 11. You will receive a Twilio email when the status changes from **In Review** to **Approved** or **Failed**. --- ## Approval ETA **1–3 business days** for TCR re-review. Most resubmissions with the above fixes in place clear within 24–48 hours. If rejected again, the rejection email will cite a specific reason — send it to me and I'll adjust. --- ## Pre-submit checklist (do this before clicking Submit) - [ ] Privacy Policy updated and published — visit the live URL and confirm the SMS section is visible - [ ] Terms & Conditions updated and published — visit the live URL and confirm - [ ] Every phone-collecting form has the disclosure text under the phone field - [ ] Every phone-collecting form has the required, unchecked-by-default consent checkbox above the submit button - [ ] Both Privacy Policy and Terms links on the forms are clickable and go to the right pages - [ ] Screenshots captured and named - [ ] Sample messages drafted with STOP language - [ ] CTA text (from step 5 above) ready to paste

docs.body:
# Agent Engine

The runtime that powers every AI seat in the org chart.

## Boot

On `node dist/index.js` start, the server brings up these workers in addition to Express + tRPC:

- **Email Manager AI** — outbound mail dispatch + Gmail ingest poll.
- **Lead Nurturer worker** — drafts roadmap follow-ups, missing-info questions, T+4h..T+10d cadence.
- **Review scheduler** — daily 06:00 quality scan.
- **360° Drip** — onboarding, renewal, nurture cadences.
- **Deferred Credit** — releases 90-day labor bank credits for monthly Silver/Gold members.
- **Auto-archive** — opportunity stage cleanup.
- **Agent runtime + KPI cron** — the seat scheduler (this file).

`Database unavailable` errors during local boot are expected (no DB); production has DB connectivity verified by `/api/health`.

## Tool-use loop

Every seat is a Claude session bound to:

1. **A charter** (`charters` table, seeded by `scripts/seed-charters.mjs`) — mission, decision matrix, KPIs, SOPs, escalation paths.
2. **A tool whitelist** — only the tRPC procedures the seat is authorized to call. See each charter's "Tool Authorization" matrix.
3. **A trigger** — `event` (e.g., `opportunities.signed`), `cron` (e.g., daily 06:00), or `event+cron`.

The loop:
- Trigger fires.
- Engine loads the charter prompt + the relevant context (customer record, opportunity, prior turns).
- Claude (Sonnet for reasoning, Haiku for high-volume) returns either a tool call or a draft.
- Tool calls execute against the tRPC layer with the seat's identity. Outputs return to the model.
- When the loop terminates, drafts go to `agentDrafts` with `status = ready`. Auto-send only when the charter explicitly authorizes (transactional only — never customer-facing first-touch).

## Drafts and approvals

Every customer-facing message originates as a draft. The Lead Nurturer's draft generator surfaces banned-vocab warnings before the operator taps Send. See [[Hard Stops + Guardrails]] and [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]].

Tier-by-tier auto-send rules (Customer Success):
- Bronze / Silver — auto-send on transactional templates after Department Head sign-off.
- Gold — Concierge reviews every message before send. No exceptions.

## Schedules

A small set of crons drive the org:

- **04:00 PT** — Snyk security scan.
- **06:00 PT** — AI Cash Flow snapshot, AI Annual Valuation anniversary scan, AI Vendor Outreach pipeline scan, AI Content/SEO opportunity scan, AI Margin Monitor.
- **07:00 PT** — AI Bookkeeping transaction sweep, AI SDR ad performance pull.
- **08:00 PT** — AI System Integrity weekly health digest (Mondays), AI Margin Monitor trend briefing (Mondays), AI Membership Success silent-member sweep (Mondays).
- **09:00 PT** — AI Cash Flow A/R aging sweep.
- **14:00 PT (Fri)** — AI Security weekly dependency review, AI Paid Ads creative slate.
- **16:00 PT (Fri)** — AI Bookkeeping reconciliation pass, AI Expansion Playbook readiness recompute.
- **18:00 PT** — AI Margin Monitor live-job margin check.
- **First Mon Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct** — AI Security key rotation calendar.
- **Quarterly** — AI Paid Ads PMax asset refresh, Customer Success retention review.

## Agent surfaces in the operator app

- `/admin/visionary` — Visionary Console (cockpit). See [[Visionary Console]].
- `/admin/agents/drafts` — pending drafts queue.
- `/admin/agents/tasks` — tasks needing approval.
- `/admin/ai-agents` — seat registry, charter viewer, KPI rollups.
- `/admin/agents/teams` — team configuration.
- `/settings → AI Agents → Charters & Playbooks` — verification surface after deploy.

## Cost & token observability

Every assistant turn reports tokens + dollars in the Visionary Console. Left pane shows 24-hour department-by-department spend. Cost gets attributed back to the seat that initiated the loop.

## Failure modes (system-enforced)

- **Drift toward sales voice.** Stewardship test before send.
- **Cadence overload.** Hard frequency cap at the dispatch layer.
- **Documentation rot.** QA bundle gate for baseline complete + freshness check on valuation source data.
- **Concierge bottleneck.** Documented backup contact per Gold member.
- **Premature tier pitch.** 30-day eligibility lock at the system level.
