docs.body:
# Agent Teams — The Three Rules

How the 8 departments coordinate without a human standing in the middle.

## Rule 1 — Own your territory

Every seat has a charter that defines what it decides alone, what it escalates, and what it never does. A seat that can decide does. It does not "loop in" peers for cover. It logs the decision in `pipelineEvents` (or the equivalent department log) and moves on.

The Department Head only enters the loop when:
- the matter crosses the seat's documented authority,
- the matter touches another department's territory,
- a hard-stop condition fires.

The Integrator only enters the loop when the Department Head escalates. The Visionary only enters the loop when the Integrator escalates per the [[Hard Stops + Guardrails|hard stops list]].

## Rule 2 — Direct messages

When a signal touches another seat, the originating seat **pings the owning seat directly** via `hierarchy.ping`. No daisy-chain ("Sales tells the PM to tell Finance"). No status standups. The Integrator routes only when the right owner is ambiguous.

Example (from the Integrator charter):
- Sales surfaces a hot $40k commercial lead.
- Sales `hierarchy.ping`s **Operations** for crew capacity and **Finance** for margin pre-check (against the [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]] floor).
- Customer Success preps onboarding in parallel.
- Brand Guardian confirms the proposal language is on-voice.
- All four threads run concurrently. The Integrator sees the rolled-up result, not the choreography.

This is the "minutes, not days" rule.

## Rule 3 — Parallel start

Independent work begins in parallel by default. Sequential only when there is a real data dependency. If two work items can both start now, they both start now.

The runtime enforces this: agents are stateless between turns and the engine fires triggers concurrently when they don't share a write target. Where there is shared state (e.g., two seats want to update the same customer profile), the engine uses optimistic-locking with a "second writer reads, retries" pattern.

---

## Why this works

The three rules collapse coordination overhead. A 70-seat org with daily standups would die of meetings; an org operating on direct messages + own-your-territory + parallel-start moves like a small team because there is no waiting.

It also creates a clean failure signal. If the Integrator finds itself routinely routing decisions, that's a charter problem (a seat doesn't have the authority it needs) — not a coordination problem.

## Cross-references
- [[Agent Engine]] — runtime & schedules
- [[Visionary Console]] — Marcin's cockpit on top of the team layer
- [[../Charters/Integrator Visionary]] — how the Integrator coordinates the 8 departments
- [[Hard Stops + Guardrails]] — when no rule overrides escalation
docs.title: Agent Teams — The Three Rules

docs.body:
# API Surface — tRPC Routers

> Source: `HP-Estimator-app/server/routers/*.ts`. Composition root: `server/routers.ts`.
> All routers run on tRPC v11.6.0 with Zod 4 validation and superjson serialization.

## Root composition

The root router (`server/routers.ts`) mounts 27 sub-routers. Each is auth-gated by default (operator JWT cookie); customer portal routes use a separate magic-link cookie.

## Router Inventory

### Customer & Pipeline
- **`customers`** — find-by-email-or-phone, find-or-create-from-call, list, get, update, mergeStub, listWithActivity (unread-aware).
- **`opportunities`** — CRUD, importCsv (HousecallPro mapping), kanban moves, listByStage, importBatch, archive.
- **`pipelineEvents`** — append-only event reads.
- **`properties`** — CRUD, healthScore, enroll/upgrade/cancel membership, listByCustomer.
- **`priorityTranslation`** — Roadmap Generator (NOW/SOON/WAIT) — `getCtaContext`, `assertVoice`, `cancelPendingFollowupsForCustomer`.

### Money path
- **`estimate`** — calc engine (17 phases), rewriteCustomItem, addCustomItem, present view; margin enforcement is server-side via `enforceMarginFloor()`.
- **`invoices`** — list, get, create, send, markPaid, aging.
- **`payments`** — Stripe payment intents, deposit auto-redirect.
- **`financials`** — P&L, expense breakdown by `scope`, sendReminder (gated through automation engine).
- **`expenses`** — CRUD, scope = `job | business`.
- **`reporting`** — exports, KPI rollups.

### 360° Method
- **`threeSixty`** — getMembership360, list memberships, listByCustomer, listByProperty, listJobsByMembership, adjustLaborBank, updateMembershipNotes, pauseMembership, resumeMembership, getMembershipStripeStatus.
- **`workOrders`** — listGlobal (filter by type/status), listJobsByMembership, completion (writes back hpOpportunityId).
- **`schedule`** — booking, reschedule, baseline visit, calendar feed; calls onAppointmentBooked when Baseline/Consultation created (commit `2d63e34`).

### Portal
- **`portal`** — getDashboard, getMembership360, getTeamInfo, getRecentProjectCompletion, sendMagicLink, verifyToken, autoLoginFromStripeSession, approveEstimate, getCustomerJobProgress, submitServiceRequest, uploadPhoto, createSetupIntent, confirmCardSetup, request*.
- **`booking`** — `/book` ingest, deriveSource, sendBookingInquiryAck.
- **`inbox`** — unifiedFeed.getByCustomer, customerList.listWithActivity, linkToCustomer, conversation thread, send message.
- **`phone`** — getSettings, updateSettings, testCall, voicemail handling, recording proxy URL.
- **`gmail`** — OAuth connect, ingest poll, label management.

### Marketing & Communications
- **`emailTemplates`** — CRUD; pre-filled with HP defaults (magic link, estimate approval, job sign-off, change order, magic link).
- **`campaigns`** — recipients, send batches.
- **`automationRules`** — rule builder UI, log viewer (25 pre-seeded rules).
- **`notificationPreferences`** — per-staff toggle map.
- **`notifications`** — bell.

### Integrations
- **`quickbooks`** — OAuth connect, draftInvoice, syncCustomer, fetchPaymentStatus, bulk sync.
- **`uploads`** — S3 presign for photos / docs.

### Settings
- **`appSettings`** — single-row settings reader / writer.

## Cross-router Patterns

### Server-side margin enforcement
`enforceMarginFloor()` (in `server/lib/projectEstimator/`) runs after Claude returns an estimate proposal. Floor: 30% on $2k+ hard cost, 40% under. Confidence gate routes to `delivered | needs_review | needs_info` per [[Customer Lifecycle]].

### Approval gates everywhere a customer message originates
The Lead Nurturer and every agent draft land in `/admin/agents/drafts` with status `ready` and wait for the operator's tap. No autosend to customers without human approval.

### Cadence pause hooks
`cancelPendingFollowupsForCustomer()` and `pauseCadenceForCustomer()` flip matching `agentDrafts` rows to `cancelled` when:
- `appointment.scheduled`
- `customer.replied`
- `subscription.created`
- `estimate.approved`
- `customer.declined`

### Auto-link inbound to customer
Every inbound call/SMS routes through `findOrCreateCustomerFromCall` — normalized phone match, stub creation if missed, conversation linkage.

### Visionary Console
`/admin/visionary` cockpit calls `agentTeams.assignTask` and `agentTeams.broadcast`. Tool calls render inline; cost and tokens shown per turn; mobile-first layout. See [[Visionary Console]].

## REST Webhook Surface

Outside tRPC, the server exposes:
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/inbound` → TwiML route.
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/voicemail` → voicemail capture + owner notify.
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/fallback` → 2-stage fall-through to AI service / system voicemail.
- `POST /api/twilio/recording/:sid` → S3-proxied recording stream with Basic Auth.
- `POST /api/stripe/webhook` (primary on `pro.handypioneers.com`) + fallback (`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET_FALLBACK`) for the Manus-space funnel.
- `POST /api/360/checkout` + `/api/360/portfolio-checkout` + `/api/360/event` — 360° funnel endpoints.
- `GET /api/health` — DB connection, email sender readiness, Gmail OAuth status.
- `GET /api/integrations/google-business/callback` and `/api/integrations/qbo/callback` — OAuth returns.
- `GET /360-welcome` — confirmation session endpoint for funnel.
docs.title: API Surface — tRPC Routers

docs.body:
# Customer Lifecycle — Path A → Path B

The money path. Two doors, one destination.

## Two paths

### Path A — Project work
A homeowner contacts HP about a specific project. We deliver a walkthrough, a Roadmap (NOW/SOON/WAIT), a written scope of work, and the work itself. This is the door, not the destination.

### Path B — 360° Method membership
After a Path A engagement (or directly from the funnel), the homeowner enrolls in the 360° Method. Bronze / Silver / Gold tier × monthly / quarterly / annual cadence. This is where the relationship economics, the brand, and the customer outcome all win.

Every Path A engagement is engineered to qualify and convert into Path B within 90 days.

## Money path diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Inbound — web form, GBP, Meta DM, referral, /book, missed call] --> B[AI SDR triage + ICP score]
  B --> C{ICP fit?}
  C -- No --> D[Out-of-area decline / referral]
  C -- Yes --> E[Customer Experience Lead — in-person walkthrough]
  E --> F[Roadmap — NOW/SOON/WAIT]
  F --> G[Project Estimator — confidence gate]
  G -- High confidence --> H[Estimate delivered]
  G -- Medium --> I[needs_review — Marcin notified]
  G -- Low --> J[needs_info — questions to customer]
  I --> H
  J --> H
  H --> K{Customer choice}
  K -- Approve --> L[Stripe deposit invoice]
  K -- Schedule walkthrough --> M[In-person scope confirm]
  M --> H
  L --> N[Operations dispatch — internal-first]
  N --> O[Job complete + photo QA + sign-off]
  O --> P[Final invoice]
  P --> Q[NPS survey T+48h]
  Q --> R{Path B offer in portal}
  R -- Enroll --> S[Stripe subscription]
  S --> T[Baseline visit ≤14 days]
  T --> U[Member portal active — 360° cadence]
  U --> V[Annual valuation report — anniversary month]
  V --> U
  R -- Not yet --> W[Nurture cadence — 90-day window]
  W --> R
```

## Stage mechanics

### Inbound capture (one inbox per role)
Every source funnels into a single Leads inbox. Source tagged via `deriveSource()` in `server/routers/leads.ts`. Sources currently funneled in: Online Request, Roadmap Generator, Inbound Call, Missed Call, Voicemail, Membership Intent, Baseline Walkthrough, Manual entry, Contact Form / Referral.

Adding a new source = a row in `deriveSource()` and a `SOURCE_META` entry in `LeadsPage.tsx`. **Never** a new top-level page.

### AI SDR triage
- Look up `customers.findByEmailOrPhone`; create-or-attach.
- `opportunities.create` with stage `new_inquiry`, source channel, raw message.
- Run ICP enrichment (occupation, neighborhood, advisor signals).
- Draft first-touch reply (templates L-1 through L-4 in [[../Charters/Sales|Sales charter]]).
- Queue task for Customer Experience Lead.
- TTFC target: < 15 min business hours, < 12 hr overnight.

### Confidence gate (estimate)
Every estimate ships through one of three paths:
- **High confidence** → status `delivered`, customer sees range immediately, `concierge_estimate_ready` draft queues for approval.
- **Medium confidence** → status `needs_review`, Marcin notified via `notifyOwner`. Customer sees "your range is being finalized" copy.
- **Low confidence** → status `needs_info`. Customer sees "your Concierge will be in touch shortly".

Customer never sees a sub-floor estimate. `enforceMarginFloor()` validates server-side regardless of what Claude returned.

### Customer-facing pricing rules
- Always a range — `low — high`. Never "starting at."
- ±25% discovery buffer baked in.
- Never expose internal margin, markup multipliers, or hourly rates.
- Always show what's included AND what's not.

### Two-path CTA on every project page
1. **Proceed with this project** — opens scheduling for project commencement.
2. **Request a walkthrough first** — opens scheduling for an in-person scope confirmation.

Either is "the right answer." Never pressure the customer toward Proceed.

### Cadence pause hooks
The Project Estimator's T+4h..T+10d cadence cancels automatically on any of:
- `appointment.scheduled`
- `customer.replied` (inbound SMS or email)
- `subscription.created` (joined 360° Method)
- `estimate.approved` (clicked Proceed)
- `customer.declined` (operator-set)

Pause is triggered via `pauseCadenceForCustomer()` which flips matching `agentDrafts` rows to `cancelled` with the reason recorded.

### Path A → Path B handoff (Customer Success)
On `opportunities.statusChanged → won`:
1. `customers.update` — provision portal account, tier assignment, anniversary date.
2. `tasks.create` — schedule baseline visit within 14 days.
3. Welcome email + portal credentials within 60 minutes of signature.
4. Day-30 NPS survey scheduled.

90-day post-completion conversion sequence anchors on the home's specific record, never on a calendar promotion.

### Stewardship pacing (customer surfaces)
- No "next available in 2 hours" offers.
- Default appointment offers start **5–10 days out**, weekdays only.
- Mornings (10am) and afternoons (2pm). Four windows is the cap.
- Pre-arrival reminders: 24h before, 1h before. No marketing follow-ups about the same walkthrough.

### Single primary CTA on Roadmap (server-resolved)
| Customer state | Variant | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Default | `baseline_walkthrough` | Schedule walkthrough |
| Estimate sent or viewed | `approve_estimate` | Review now |
| Estimate approved + linked HP opportunity | `track_project` | Open project |
| 360° member, no upcoming visit | `schedule_member_visit` | Request a visit |

No upsell stack below it. No countdowns.

## Cross-references
- [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]
- [[../Library/360° Method Pricing|360° Method Pricing]]
- [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]]
- [[../Charters/Sales|Sales charter]]
- [[../Charters/Customer Success|Customer Success charter]]
docs.title: Customer Lifecycle — Path A → Path B

docs.body:
# Hard Stops + Guardrails

What no AI seat ever does without Marcin's call.

## The four global hard stops

1. **Spend money or commit to spend.** No vendor contracts, no compensation, no capital outlay. Pre-model the math and present one recommendation.
2. **Make external commitments in Marcin's name.** Press, legal signatures, contracts, public statements, partnership MOUs, regulatory filings, customer commitments. Drafts only.
3. **Modify a Charter file.** Charters change only by explicit Marcin instruction.
4. **Activate a new agent or seat.** All AI seats start `draft_queue`. Activation is sequenced and approved.

Anything that touches one of those four lines is a hard stop. Surface it as one sentence with one recommendation.

## Per-domain hard stops

### Customer-facing copy
- Never auto-send a first-touch reply to an inbound lead.
- Never use forbidden vocab (handyman, estimate, free, cheap, affordable, easy, fix, repair, best, save, discount, limited time). The Lead Nurturer surfaces violations as advisory warnings.
- Never auto-send to a member with an open complaint flag.
- Never pitch a tier upgrade in the first 30 days.
- Never send the same customer >2 messages in 7 days outside an active project.
- Never use exclamation marks in stewardship copy.

### Pricing
- Never approve an estimate below 30% gross margin (40% if hard cost < $2k).
- Never re-rate internal labor below $150/hr.
- Never approve a margin override autonomously — always escalate to Marcin.
- Never expose internal margin, markup multipliers, or hourly rates to customers.

### Operations
- Never mark a baseline complete without QA photos and signed checklist.
- Never dispatch a vendor whose insurance has lapsed.
- Never activate a vendor without PM sign-off.

### Finance
- Never invent a category — post to `Uncategorized–Review` and ping Margin Monitor.
- Never recategorize a prior period without CPA approval.
- Never write off bad debt — escalate to Integrator.
- Never initiate a wire / ACH / transfer.

### Technology
- AI never executes deploys, restarts, or config changes on production. Drafts and recommendations only.
- AI never auto-rotates secrets — drafts the rotation plan; Marcin executes.
- AI never autonomously approves a "won't fix" on a security finding.

### Marketing
- Never launch, edit live budgets, or pause campaigns autonomously.
- Never bid on competitor brand terms without explicit approval.
- Never run anything outside Clark County, WA in v1.
- Never publish content; brief and draft only — Marcin publishes.

### Vendor / Trades
- Never commit work, rates, or volume in an outreach message.
- Never onboard a vendor without verified license + COI + W-9 + signed agreement + scope sheet.
- Never substitute external for internal labor without a logged reason code.

### Strategy / Expansion
- Never contact anyone in a candidate market.
- Never declare HP "ready for outbound" without 2 consecutive quarters above all readiness thresholds.

## Approval gates everywhere a customer message originates

The Lead Nurturer never auto-sends. Drafts land in `/admin/agents/drafts` with status `ready` and wait for the operator's tap. This bar applies to every future agent — translation, scheduling reminders, billing chase, anything that speaks in the brand's voice.

## Guardrails (system-enforced, not policy-enforced)

- **Margin floor** — `enforceMarginFloor()` runs server-side regardless of what Claude returned.
- **Frequency cap** — dispatch layer enforces ≤2 messages per customer per 7 days outside an active project.
- **Onboarding lock** — tier-upgrade sequences gated behind 30-day eligibility lock.
- **QA bundle gate** — baseline cannot be marked complete without verified photos + checklist + technician notes.
- **Valuation freshness** — annual valuation cannot ship without a freshness check on source data.
- **Stewardship test** — every outbound template includes a "would a chief of staff send this to a principal?" check before ship.

## Cross-references
- [[../Memory|Memory § Hard Stops]]
- [[../Charters/Integrator Visionary|Integrator + Visionary charter — decision matrix]]
- [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]
- [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]]
docs.title: Hard Stops + Guardrails

docs.body:
# Cloudflare

## What it does
DNS, edge cache, WAF, rate limiting, DDoS for all HP domains.

## Domains under management
- `handypioneers.com` (apex + `www`)
- `pro.handypioneers.com`
- `client.handypioneers.com`
- `360.handypioneers.com`

## DNS records
- `pro` and `client` → CNAME to Railway public domain.
- Apex → Railway or hosted marketing site.
- DKIM / SPF / DMARC → [[Resend]] (hand-rotated).

## API access
- Token rotated last session (per HANDOFF_CHECKLIST "Existing Keys to Rotate").
- Old DNS-only token (`cfut_Rl4tz…`) **still to revoke** in Cloudflare Dashboard → My Profile → API Tokens.

## WAF posture
- Allow + log mode for the operator app; block on bot signatures and geographic anomalies for the customer portal.
- Rate limiting wraps `/api/twilio/*` and `/api/stripe/webhook` — burst protection only; do not block legitimate webhooks.

## Agent dependencies
- AI System Integrity (Technology) — reads health signals; never edits Cloudflare config.
- AI Security (Technology) — flags Cloudflare events that look like enumeration / abuse.
docs.title: Cloudflare

## What it does
Read and reply to GBP reviews; pull profile insights (calls, direction requests, profile views) into Marketing dashboards.

1. `business.google.com` → sign in with HP Google account → location → Settings → Manage users → add `reviews@handypioneers.com` as Owner.
2. `console.cloud.google.com` → project "Handy Pioneers Prod" → APIs & Services → Library → enable **Google Business Profile API**.
3. Credentials → Create OAuth client → Web application.
4. Authorized redirect URI: `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/integrations/google-business/callback`.
5. Paste back: `GBP_CLIENT_ID`, `GBP_CLIENT_SECRET`.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Community / Reviews (Marketing) — drafts review replies, never sends.
- AI QA (Operations) — pulls reputation signals into per-job scorecards.

## Voice rules
Review replies follow the same banned-word list. Per [[../../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]: no "thanks for the review!" cliché, no exclamation marks, identity-first, specific to the customer's home if known.

# QuickBooks Online

## What it does
Sync invoices, payments, customers, expenses; provide the source of truth for monthly close, tax, and audit.

## Status
**OAuth scaffold shipped** (commit `f13be41`) — bulk sync stubbed. Production credentials and `QBO_REALM_ID` not yet pasted.

## Setup
1. **QBO Simple Start** at `quickbooks.intuit.com` — $30/mo.
2. `developer.intuit.com` → My Apps → Create app → "QuickBooks Online and Payments" → "Handy Pioneers".
3. Scope: `com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting`.
4. **Switch to Production Settings** before issuing keys.
5. Redirect URI: `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/integrations/qbo/callback`.

## Env Vars
- `QBO_CLIENT_ID`
- `QBO_CLIENT_SECRET`
- `QBO_REALM_ID` (from the connect-flow handshake)

## Database
`qbTokens` table holds OAuth access + refresh tokens per environment.

## Server tools (per Finance charter)
- `qbo.draftInvoice` — autonomous draft from approved estimate.
- `qbo.syncCustomer` — autonomous, with merge.
- `qbo.categorizeExpense` — autonomous when vendor + memo match prior pattern.
- `qbo.fetchPaymentStatus` — read-only.

## Sandbox → Prod path
1. Connect sandbox first; verify draft invoice flow on a fixture customer.
2. Run a 7-day reconciliation pass against a sandbox bank feed.
3. Switch app to Production keys; re-OAuth.
4. CPA fractional (HUMAN seat) signs off on first month-end close before AI Bookkeeping operates without supervision.

## Hard rules
- Never recategorize a prior-period transaction without CPA approval.
- Never write off bad debt — escalate to Integrator.
- Never invent a category — post to `Uncategorized–Review` and ping Margin Monitor.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Bookkeeping (Finance) — primary operator.
- AI Margin Monitor (Finance) — gates invoice draft.
- AI Cash Flow (Finance) — DSO + payable aging.
- CPA / Tax (HUMAN, fractional) — period close + tax position.
docs.title: QuickBooks Online

# Resend

## What it does
All transactional + customer-facing email. Replaced Gmail-as-sender (PR #48 merged 2026-04-27).

## Library
- Direct `fetch` calls in `server/lib/email/resend.ts`.
- `nodemailer` v8.0.5 retained only for legacy SMTP fallback paths and Gmail OAuth ingest on `help@`.

## Env Vars
- `RESEND_API_KEY` — required. `/api/health` reports `email.sender: resend_ready` when set.

## Sender Discipline
- `help@handypioneers.com` — default. Customer-facing emails that should be replyable: estimates, invoices, magic links, Roadmap delivery (`server/lib/priorityTranslation/email.ts:38`), Project Estimator notifications (`server/lib/projectEstimator/messaging.ts:216`).
- `noreply@handypioneers.com` — RESERVED. Used only when reply doesn't make sense. The Resend strategy doc enforces this at the lib level.

Critical fix landed 2026-04-28 in consolidation: defaults were `noreply@`, flipped to `help@` on the two customer-facing senders above.

## Domain Setup
- Sender domain: `handypioneers.com`.
- DKIM, SPF, DMARC configured via Cloudflare DNS.
- DNS owner: Marcin (single account).

## Templates
Pre-filled in `appSettings`:
- Magic link
- Estimate approval
- Job sign-off
- Change order approval
+ four more transactional bodies (commit `75b5e5f`).

All templates use `{{variable}}` interpolation; the automation engine injects `companyName`, `supportPhone`, `supportEmail`, `websiteUrl`, `portalUrl`, `addressLine1/2`, `googleReviewLink` automatically (commit `1e944ef`).

## Agent dependencies
- Lead Nurturer — drafts go to `/admin/agents/drafts` first, never autosent.
- AI Onboarding (Customer Success) — welcome + day-30 cadence.
- AI Annual Valuation — anniversary cover note.
- AI Bookkeeping — invoice send (gated through Margin Monitor approval).

## Known limitations
- Inbound on `help@` still polls via Gmail OAuth; not yet ingested through Resend's inbound webhook. Migration not prioritized.

# Snyk

## What it does
Daily vulnerability scan of the HP Estimator monorepo. Surfaces Critical / High / Medium / Low findings to AI Security.

## Status
**Not yet wired.** P1 #8 in [[../../HANDOFF_CHECKLIST|HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md]].
GitHub-side check is "known-flaky" — referenced in the 2026-04-28 marketing readiness audit; admin-merged PR #17 with Snyk failing.

## Setup
1. `snyk.io` → Sign up with GitHub → authorize the Handy Pioneers GitHub org.
2. Settings → Service accounts → Create → "HP Estimator CI" → copy token.

## Env Vars
- `SNYK_TOKEN`

## Server tool
- `security.runScan` — daily 04:00 cron.
- `security.flagFinding` — append to `tasks.create` queue when severity ≥ High.

## SLAs (per Technology charter)
- Critical: triage within 24h, close within 7 days. Target: zero open Critical at any time.
- High: triage within 24h, close within 30 days. Target: < 3 open at any time.
- Medium / Low: weekly batch review.

## 90-day token window
Refresh draft fires day 75; hard fail at day 88. Drafts a refresh task for Marcin — never auto-rotates.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Security (Technology).

# Stripe

## What it does
Subscriptions for the 360° Method (Bronze / Silver / Gold × monthly / quarterly / annual cadences), one-shot deposits for Path A projects, and SetupIntent for portal Wallet card-on-file.

## Library
- `stripe` v22.0.0 (server)
- `@stripe/stripe-js` v9.0.1 + `@stripe/react-stripe-js` v6.1.0 (client)

## Env Vars
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` — server-side restricted key.
- `STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` — client.
- `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — primary webhook (events to `pro.handypioneers.com`).
- `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET_FALLBACK` — secondary webhook (legacy `manus.space` events; both secrets tried in handler — commit `7668642`).
- `STRIPE_PRICE_HOMEOWNER_BRONZE_*`, `_SILVER_*`, `_GOLD_*` for each cadence; portfolio variants too. 9 homeowner price IDs in total.

## Webhooks
- Primary: `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/stripe/webhook`
- Fallback: legacy Manus space (kept until traffic fully off the funnel space).

Handler sequence on `checkout.session.completed`:
1. Resolve tier via `HOMEOWNER_TIER_MAP` (max → GOLD, full_coverage → SILVER, exterior_shield → BRONZE — commit `a50f572`).
2. Create `threeSixtyMembership` row + `customers` upsert.
3. Issue magic-link token (7-day window) + `welcome` email via Resend.
4. Schedule baseline visit work order (48h SLA), notify owner via SMS if `OWNER_PHONE` set.
5. For monthly Silver/Gold, queue deferred 90-day labor bank credit (cron every 6h).

## Customer flow
1. `/portal/360-membership` → `EnrollModal` → `/api/360/checkout`.
2. Success URL: `/portal/360-confirmation?session_id=...` → `portal.autoLoginFromStripeSession` polls `getMembership360` until materialized.
3. Cancel URL: `/portal/360-membership`.

## Known limitations
- Tier price IDs are env-only; CMS surface for tier mgmt is post-launch.
- Failure mode: webhook secret mismatch silently no-ops; logged via Email Manager AI.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Bookkeeping (Finance) — read invoices and payments via QBO sync chain.
- AI Membership Success (Sales) — observe subscription state for retention triggers.

docs.body:
# Twilio

## What it does
Inbound and outbound voice + SMS for `OWNER_PHONE` and the operator pool. Voicemail capture, recording proxy, two-stage call routing.

## Library
- `twilio` v5.13.1 (server)
- `@twilio/voice-sdk` v2.18.1 (in-app web caller)

## Env Vars
- `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`
- `TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER` — default outbound caller ID.
- `OWNER_PHONE` — Marcin's personal cell for stage-1 forwarding.
- `FORWARD_TO_NUMBER` — env-only fallback when `phoneSettings` table is missing (`429431a`, `2b337d5`).
- `TWILIO_MESSAGING_SERVICE_SID` — populated after A2P 10DLC approval.

## Inbound voice flow
1. Carrier hits `POST /api/twilio/voice/inbound`.
2. Server reads `phoneSettings` from DB (or env fallback).
3. TwiML returns: forward → `OWNER_PHONE` for ~10 seconds (2 rings).
4. On no-answer: `POST /api/twilio/voice/fallback` → AI service or system voicemail (commit `e12b58d`).
5. On voicemail recording: `POST /api/twilio/voice/voicemail` → `notifyOwner()` + SMS to `OWNER_PHONE` if recording present.

## Recording proxy
- Recordings download server-side from Twilio CDN, re-upload to app S3 → `recordingAppUrl` written to `callLogs`.
- Public route: `/api/twilio/recording/:sid` streams audio with Basic Auth — no Twilio login needed in browser (commit `cac1988`).
- Inbox All tab and Calls tab both render inline `<audio>` players.

## Inbound SMS auto-link
`findOrCreateCustomerFromCall` (commit `d383588`):
1. Normalize phone.
2. Look up `customers` by phone.
3. If miss: create stub customer with `leadSource = "inbound_call"`.
4. Link the conversation, backfill any prior unlinked rows.

## A2P 10DLC
Required to deliver SMS reliably to US carriers. Filing today.
- Brand: Handy Pioneers LLC; EIN on file; industry: Home Services; private (no ticker).
- Campaign: Customer Care use case; sample messages drafted by Sales lead, approved by Marcin.
- ETA: 3–7 business days.
- Until approved: outbound SMS may be filtered. Voice path is unaffected.

## Settings UI
Settings → Integrations → Phone tab: mode radio (forward/AI/voicemail), call recording toggle, voicemail transcription toggle, test call, Twilio webhook instructions copied with one click. PhoneSettings UI carries call greeting + voicemail prompt as separate editors with 5 example templates each (`4171a5e`, `e685244`).

## Agent dependencies
- AI Dispatch (Operations) — outbound arrival pings.
- Customer Success AI — confirmation + reminder cadences.
- AI SDR (Sales) — never sends; drafts only.

# Repository Structure

## Repos

### `HP-Estimator-app` (the nucleus)
Path: `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\Handy Pioneers\HP Estimator\HP-Estimator-app`

Single mono-repo. Frontend + backend + agent runtime + drizzle migrations.

```
HP-Estimator-app/
├── client/                 # React 19 + Vite app (operator + customer portal)
│   ├── src/pages/          # Top-level routes (Estimator, CRM, Portal*, Settings)
│   ├── src/components/     # Feature components + shadcn/ui primitives
│   └── src/lib/            # Hooks, contexts, utilities
├── server/
│   ├── _core/              # Express + tRPC scaffold, env, llm, voice, sdk
│   │   ├── index.ts        # Server entry
│   │   ├── trpc.ts         # tRPC instance
│   │   ├── auth.ts         # JWT cookie auth
│   │   ├── llm.ts          # Anthropic Claude wrapper
│   │   ├── voiceTranscription.ts
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── routers/            # 27 tRPC routers (see [[API Surface]])
│   ├── lib/                # leadNurturer, projectEstimator, email, etc.
│   └── routers.ts          # Root router composition
├── shared/                 # Types, const, error helpers, tax rates, 360 tiers
├── drizzle/
│   ├── schema.ts           # 56 tables (see [[Schema Overview]])
│   └── 0000–0064_*.sql     # 65 migrations
├── docs/
│   ├── agents/             # 9 department charters → Charters/* in this vault
│   └── pro-portal-sync-plan.md
├── ARCHITECTURE.md         # 17-phase estimator data model
├── HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md    # API keys + decisions blocking agent activation
├── README.md
├── STAGING_WORKFLOW.md
├── TOOLS_GAP.md
├── PDF_LAYOUT_NOTES.md
├── invoice-design-notes.md
├── portland-labor-rates-research.md
├── ideas.md / todo.md
├── package.json
├── drizzle.config.ts
├── nixpacks.toml + railway.json
├── vite.config.ts + vitest.config.ts
└── tsconfig*.json
```

### `360Method/hp-360-funnel` (public)
GitHub: `https://github.com/360Method/hp-360-funnel`
Stack: React/TypeScript, originally Manus-built. Pages: `FunnelPage`, `CheckoutPage`, `ConfirmationPage`. ~73 commits.

Hosts the 360° funnel at `https://360.handypioneers.com` (homeowner + multifamily versions). Routes checkout to HP Estimator's Stripe webhooks (`/api/360/checkout`, portfolio variant, then back to `/portal/360-confirmation`).

### `handy-pioneers-website` (marketing site)
Hardcoded copy today. Tagged for migration into the [[../Memory|nucleus CMS]] post-launch.

## Worktree convention

`HP-Estimator-app/.claude/worktrees/<adjective-name>` — one git worktree per feature branch / Cowork session. Audit docs (`PORTAL_AUDIT_*`, `MORNING_BRIEF_*`, `MARKETING_READINESS_*`, `EXPERIENCE_STANDARDS.md`, `SECURITY_AUDIT.md`, `SNYK_BASELINE.md`, `CONSULTANT_BASELINE_TOOL_PLAN.md`) live in the worktree where they were generated and migrate to `main` when promoted. Latest set as of 2026-04-28 sits in `cool-faraday-626f76`.

## Build pipeline

1. `pnpm install` (uses `pnpm-lock.yaml` + patches).
2. `pnpm check` → `tsc --noEmit` clean.
3. `pnpm build` → vite build (~4.3 MB chunk) + esbuild server (~1.2 MB dist).
4. Smoke import: `node -e "import('./dist/index.js')"` boots Email Manager, Lead Nurturer worker, Review scheduler, 360 Drip, Deferred Credit, Auto-archive, agent runtime, KPI cron.
5. `pnpm start` → `node dist/index.js`.

# Schema Overview

> Source: `HP-Estimator-app/drizzle/schema.ts` (1,536 lines, 56 tables) + `drizzle/0000–0064_*.sql` (65 migrations).
> ORM: Drizzle 0.44.5 against MySQL on Railway.

## Architectural Principle — Customer-Centric SSOT

Every customer-related entity FKs back to `customers.id`. No FK = "Unknown Customer" + admin queue. Cross-customer data (templates, automations, settings) lives at the system level under `appSettings`. See [[../Memory|Memory § Customer-Centric Architecture]].

## Tables by Cluster

### Identity & Auth (5)
- `users` — staff users (operator login).
- `staffUsers` — extended staff metadata.
- `userRoles` — role-based access (consultant / project_manager / admin).
- `adminAllowlist` — superuser email list.
- `gmailTokens` — OAuth tokens for inbound Gmail polling on `help@`.

### Customer Domain (4) — the SSOT
- `customers` — the root entity. Bypass-auto-nurture flag, leadSource, lifecycle stage, ICP score, anniversary date.
- `customerAddresses` — 1:N service addresses per customer.
- `properties` — 1:N stewarded properties per customer; carries health score and 360° eligibility.
- `serviceZipCodes` — geofence for inbound qualification.

### Opportunity / Pipeline (3)
- `opportunities` — Lead → Estimate → Job → Archive (`area` enum). Carries `membershipId` FK to bridge Path A and Path B.
- `pipelineEvents` — append-only event log for stage changes.
- `onlineRequests` — `/book` form submissions, source-tagged.

### Communications (5)
- `conversations` — 1:1 with customer (or stub) keyed by phone/email.
- `messages` — SMS, email, note, portal message rows in a single timeline.
- `callLogs` — every Twilio call; carries `recordingAppUrl` (S3-proxied), `twilioSid`, `voicemail` text.
- `phoneSettings` — voicemail prompt, call greeting, business hours, voicemail mode.
- `notifications` + `notificationPreferences` — operator notification bell.

### 360° Method (8)
- `threeSixtyMemberships` — Bronze / Silver / Gold tier, cadence (monthly / quarterly / annual), Stripe subscription ID, anniversary, labor bank balance.
- `threeSixtyVisits` — seasonal visits scheduled and completed.
- `threeSixtyChecklist` — per-system inspection checklist row.
- `threeSixtyLaborBankTransactions` — credit + debit ledger; deferred 90-day credit release.
- `threeSixtyScans` — annual home scans, scored.
- `threeSixtyPropertySystems` — per-property system inventory (HVAC, roof, plumbing, electrical, etc.).
- `threeSixtyWorkOrders` — work orders linked back to `hpOpportunityId` for cross-system bridge.
- `portalReports` — published Roadmap reports for portal viewing.

### Customer Portal (8)
- `portalCustomers` — portal identity (subset of `customers`).
- `portalTokens` — magic link tokens (7-day window).
- `portalSessions` — active sessions.
- `portalEstimates`, `portalInvoices`, `portalAppointments`, `portalMessages`, `portalGallery`, `portalReferrals`, `portalServiceRequests`, `portalJobMilestones`, `portalJobUpdates`, `portalJobSignOffs`, `portalChangeOrders`, `portalDocuments` — portal-side projections.

### Estimator / Jobs (4)
- `invoices`, `invoiceLineItems`, `invoicePayments` — billing.
- `expenses` — `scope` ∈ `job | business`; tied to opportunity for job costing.
- `scheduleEvents` — calendar / appointments / portfolios.

### Snapshots (2)
- `snapshotOpportunities`, `snapshotInvoices` — point-in-time captures for valuation reports.

### Settings & Config (4)
- `appSettings` — single-row operator SSOT (company info, sender email, support phone, document footer, terms, email templates ×4, pricing defaults, SMS settings, Google review link).
- `emailTemplates` — editable transactional templates.
- `automationRules`, `automationRuleLogs` — rule builder + execution log.

### Marketing Automation (3)
- `campaigns`, `campaignRecipients`, `campaignSends` — outbound campaign engine.

### Integrations (1)
- `qbTokens` — QuickBooks OAuth.

### Legacy / Internal (1)
- `priorityTranslation` (mention) — Roadmap Generator scaffolding ported into `priorityTranslation.ts` router.

## Key Relationships

- `customers (1) → (N) opportunities`
- `customers (1) → (N) properties`; properties carry the `membershipId`
- `opportunities.membershipId → threeSixtyMemberships.id` (Path A ↔ Path B bridge, migration 0037)
- `threeSixtyWorkOrders.hpOpportunityId → opportunities.id` (work order completion writes back the linked CRM job)
- `conversations.customerId → customers.id` (every inbound call/SMS finds-or-creates a stub)
- All `portal*` tables FK to `customers` via `portalCustomers`

## Migration discipline

- Boot-time `ensurePhoneTables` and `ensureCharterTables` patch missing tables when drizzle-kit diverges (commit `b60ec4c`).
- Migrations 0054 / 0055 are no-op safe on missing tables (`c5a6b17`).
- 0037 added the Path A ↔ Path B bridge.
- 0038 onboarding modal; 0039 off-cycle visit; 0040 portal photo URLs; 0041 phone settings; 0049 second daimon_hellstrom; 0065 / 0066 charter tables (deployment script `seed-charters.mjs`).

See [[../Migration History]] for the full ledger with rationale.

# Tech Stack — Pinned Versions and Rationale

> Source: `HP-Estimator-app/package.json` as of 2026-04-28.
> Stack name in package.json: `hp-field-estimator` v1.0.0, ESM, MIT.

## Frontend

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| React | 19.2.1 | Latest concurrent renderer; matches React DOM. |
| Vite | 7.1.7 | Build speed; supports React 19 plugin v5. |
| TypeScript | 5.9.3 | Strict mode across the monorepo. |
| Tailwind CSS | 4.1.14 | Atomic styling; oxide engine. |
| Radix UI primitives | 1.x – 2.x | shadcn/ui foundation across 30+ components. |
| Lucide React | 0.453.0 | Icon set. |
| Wouter | 3.3.5 (patched 3.7.1) | Lightweight router; nested anchor patches applied. |
| TanStack React Query | 5.90.2 | Pairs with tRPC v11. |
| React Hook Form | 7.64.0 | Form state. |
| Recharts | 2.15.4 | Financial charts. |
| Framer Motion | 12.23.22 | Portal animations. |
| date-fns | 4.1.0 | Date arithmetic. |

## API & Server

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| tRPC server / client / react-query | 11.6.0 | End-to-end typed API; 27 routers. |
| Express | 4.21.2 | Web server inside `server/_core/index.ts`. |
| Zod | 4.1.12 | Request validation, schema contracts. |
| Helmet | 8.1.0 | Security headers. |
| express-rate-limit | 8.4.0 | Rate limiting. |
| Drizzle ORM | 0.44.5 | MySQL-flavored ORM. |
| drizzle-kit | 0.31.4 | Migration tooling. |
| mysql2 | 3.15.0 | MySQL driver. |
| tsx | 4.19.1 | Dev server runner. |
| esbuild | 0.25.0 | Production bundle. |
| jose | 6.1.0 | JWT signing. |
| superjson | 1.13.3 | Date / Map / Set serialization for tRPC. |

## AI

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| @anthropic-ai/sdk | 0.90.0 | Claude is the only LLM in production. |

Models in use:
- **Claude Sonnet** — primary reasoning, agent orchestration, customer-facing draft generation.
- **Claude Haiku** — high-volume / low-latency tool calls (lead scoring, entity extraction).

## Comms

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Twilio (SMS + Voice) | 5.13.1 | Inbound/outbound SMS, voice webhook routing, recordings. |
| @twilio/voice-sdk | 2.18.1 | In-app web caller (operator side). |
| Resend (via fetch) | n/a | Transactional + customer-facing email. Replaced Gmail-as-sender. |
| nodemailer | 8.0.5 | Fallback / Gmail OAuth ingest only. |
| googleapis | 171.4.0 | Gmail OAuth ingest for help@ inbox. |

## Payments

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Stripe (server) | 22.0.0 | Subscriptions (360° tiers), one-shot deposits, payment intents. |
| @stripe/stripe-js + react-stripe-js | 9 / 6 | Customer-facing checkout. |
| @paypal/react-paypal-js | 9.1.1 | Optional PayPal flow on customer portal. |

## Storage / Files

| Component | Version | Why |
|---|---|---|
| @aws-sdk/client-s3 + presigner | 3.693.0 | Photos, recordings, documents. App proxies all Twilio recordings into our own S3. |
| Cloudinary | 2.9.0 | Replaced Forge file storage; image transforms. |
| Puppeteer | 24.41.0 | PDF generation (replaced manus-md-to-pdf). |
| pdf-lib | 1.17.1 | PDF assembly for invoices. |
| jspdf + html2canvas | 4.2.1 / 1.4.1 | Client-side PDF previews. |

## Hosting

| Service | Role |
|---|---|
| Railway | Application + MySQL + cron. nixpacks build (`nodejs_22 + pnpm`). |
| Cloudflare | DNS, WAF, edge cache for `pro`, `client`, `360`, root domain. |

## Dev tooling

- pnpm 10.4.1 (workspace root). `onlyBuiltDependencies` allow-list: puppeteer, @tailwindcss/oxide, core-js, esbuild.
- Vitest 2.1.4 — 86+ test suites at last clean state; current main carries 202 passing / 53 env-dependent (Stripe price IDs, Twilio TwiML SID, Gmail OAuth).
- Prettier 3.6.2 — write-only formatter.
- patch-package via pnpm — patches/wouter@3.7.1.patch.

## Migrated FROM (decision log)

- **Forge file storage → Cloudinary.** Commit `8006f9f` (2026-04-19). Forge was a vendor lock-in; Cloudinary is portable.
- **Manus OAuth → email + password.** Commit `8e9360a`. Removed dependency on Manus identity provider before pivot.
- **Manus-md-to-pdf binary → Puppeteer.** Commit `469e007`. Owns the rendering loop.
- **Manus dev tooling → vanilla Vite.** Commit `e7d6a1e`. Cleaned up Vite config.
- **Google OAuth login → email + password.** Commit `ee0ee7f`. Reduces external surface for login.
- **Manus platform → Supabase + Claude API → Railway + MySQL + Anthropic.** Commit `d75e9e3` then full migration through April 2026. Final state: no Manus, no Supabase.
- **Housecall Pro → HP Estimator.** In progress. 458 customers imported via `opportunities.importCsv` (commit `5dc9ac5`); HCP API key still to revoke once verified green.
- **Gmail-as-sender → Resend.** PR #48 merged 2026-04-27. `noreply@` reserved; customer-facing sends use `help@handypioneers.com`.

## Known Limitations

- **Stripe price IDs are env-only.** No CMS surface yet for tier pricing; changes require a Railway env edit + redeploy.
- **Booking unit tests pass; full suite carries 53 env-dependent failures locally.** Not a code regression — Stripe / Twilio / Gmail / DB env vars are not present without injection.
- **No structured complaint flag on customers.** Hard-stop logic depends on tag hygiene (freeform tag).
- **No vendor-coordination tool for the Concierge.** Email today.
- **No automated freshness check on annual valuation source data.** Manual QA today.
- **Snyk dev token has 90-day window.** Refresh task fires at day 75 cron; hard fail at day 88.
- **A2P 10DLC pending.** Until brand + campaign approved, outbound SMS is filtered or blocked on US carriers. ETA 3–7 business days from Twilio submission.

## Railway Service Config

- Build: `nixpacks.toml` declares `nodejs_22` and `pnpm` setup phases.
- Start: `pnpm build` (vite + esbuild) then `pnpm start` (`node dist/index.js`).
- Healthcheck: `GET /api/health` returns `{ db: connected, email.sender, gmail.configured/connected }`.
- Pre-start: `drizzle-kit migrate` runs before server boot.
- Environment: see [[Hard Stops + Guardrails]] for which envs are P0 / P1 / P2.

## Domains

| Domain | Tenant | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| `pro.handypioneers.com` | Operator app | Internal team |
| `client.handypioneers.com` | Customer portal | Customers |
| `handypioneers.com` | Public marketing site | Marketing |
| `360.handypioneers.com` | 360° Method funnel | Marketing → Sales |

docs.body:
# Visionary Console

`/admin/visionary` — Marcin's operating cockpit. From there he gives the Integrator a directive; the Integrator routes work to the right team via `agentTeams.assignTask` and broadcasts context via `agentTeams.broadcast`.

## Phase 1 — Cockpit shell (shipped)
- Streaming chat panel anchored to the Integrator role.
- Chat input stays usable while text is streaming; the next directive can queue.
- Tool calls render inline with input + output. No hidden side effects.
- If a tool requires approval, the bubble says so and links to `/admin/ai-agents/tasks`.

## Phase 2 — Team coordination (shipped)
- 8 teams = the org chart. Members are seats from `/admin/ai-agents`; team configuration lives in `/admin/agents/teams`.
- Adding a department-level surface that bypasses these teams is a smell.
- Customer-centric tasks. When the Integrator creates a team task that involves a specific customer, `customerId` is set so the task surfaces inside the customer profile alongside drafts and other agent work.

## Phase 3 — Cost & mobile (shipped)
- Every assistant turn shows token + dollar cost so Marcin can see runtime spend without leaving the cockpit.
- Left pane shows 24-hour department-by-department spend.
- Mobile primary viewport. Excellent at 390 px. Single column, sticky compose with `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)`, action queue collapses into a bottom drawer.

## Operating rules

- **Streaming, not blocking.** Long Integrator turns must stream so Marcin can read along and steer mid-thought.
- **Tool calls are visible.** Inline transparency of every tool invocation.
- **Customer-centric tasks.** Always set `customerId` when a task involves a customer.
- **Mobile is primary.** Marcin operates from his phone.
- **Cost on the surface.** Token and dollar attribution per turn.
- **The 8 teams are the org chart.** Don't bypass.

## Replaces

The "tied to Dispatch as the owner/Visionary" constraint. The full coordination layer now lives inside HP Estimator; the Integrator no longer rides on Dispatch.

## Next phase candidates (not yet shipped)
- **Phase 4 — Activation.** Move agents from `draft_queue` to `active` per the recommended sequence: AI SDR → AI Dispatch → AI Bookkeeping → AI System Integrity → remainder. See [[../Roadmap]].
- **Phase 5 — White-label / multi-tenant.** Tenant separation, branding switch, content CMS. See [[../Charters/Strategy Expansion|Strategy & Expansion charter]].

docs.body:
# Agent Engine

The runtime that powers every AI seat in the org chart.

## Boot

On `node dist/index.js` start, the server brings up these workers in addition to Express + tRPC:

- **Email Manager AI** — outbound mail dispatch + Gmail ingest poll.
- **Lead Nurturer worker** — drafts roadmap follow-ups, missing-info questions, T+4h..T+10d cadence.
- **Review scheduler** — daily 06:00 quality scan.
- **360° Drip** — onboarding, renewal, nurture cadences.
- **Deferred Credit** — releases 90-day labor bank credits for monthly Silver/Gold members.
- **Auto-archive** — opportunity stage cleanup.
- **Agent runtime + KPI cron** — the seat scheduler (this file).

`Database unavailable` errors during local boot are expected (no DB); production has DB connectivity verified by `/api/health`.

## Tool-use loop

Every seat is a Claude session bound to:

1. **A charter** (`charters` table, seeded by `scripts/seed-charters.mjs`) — mission, decision matrix, KPIs, SOPs, escalation paths.
2. **A tool whitelist** — only the tRPC procedures the seat is authorized to call. See each charter's "Tool Authorization" matrix.
3. **A trigger** — `event` (e.g., `opportunities.signed`), `cron` (e.g., daily 06:00), or `event+cron`.

The loop:
- Trigger fires.
- Engine loads the charter prompt + the relevant context (customer record, opportunity, prior turns).
- Claude (Sonnet for reasoning, Haiku for high-volume) returns either a tool call or a draft.
- Tool calls execute against the tRPC layer with the seat's identity. Outputs return to the model.
- When the loop terminates, drafts go to `agentDrafts` with `status = ready`. Auto-send only when the charter explicitly authorizes (transactional only — never customer-facing first-touch).

## Drafts and approvals

Every customer-facing message originates as a draft. The Lead Nurturer's draft generator surfaces banned-vocab warnings before the operator taps Send. See [[Hard Stops + Guardrails]] and [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]].

Tier-by-tier auto-send rules (Customer Success):
- Bronze / Silver — auto-send on transactional templates after Department Head sign-off.
- Gold — Concierge reviews every message before send. No exceptions.

## Schedules

A small set of crons drive the org:

- **04:00 PT** — Snyk security scan.
- **06:00 PT** — AI Cash Flow snapshot, AI Annual Valuation anniversary scan, AI Vendor Outreach pipeline scan, AI Content/SEO opportunity scan, AI Margin Monitor.
- **07:00 PT** — AI Bookkeeping transaction sweep, AI SDR ad performance pull.
- **08:00 PT** — AI System Integrity weekly health digest (Mondays), AI Margin Monitor trend briefing (Mondays), AI Membership Success silent-member sweep (Mondays).
- **09:00 PT** — AI Cash Flow A/R aging sweep.
- **14:00 PT (Fri)** — AI Security weekly dependency review, AI Paid Ads creative slate.
- **16:00 PT (Fri)** — AI Bookkeeping reconciliation pass, AI Expansion Playbook readiness recompute.
- **18:00 PT** — AI Margin Monitor live-job margin check.
- **First Mon Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct** — AI Security key rotation calendar.
- **Quarterly** — AI Paid Ads PMax asset refresh, Customer Success retention review.

## Agent surfaces in the operator app

- `/admin/visionary` — Visionary Console (cockpit). See [[Visionary Console]].
- `/admin/agents/drafts` — pending drafts queue.
- `/admin/agents/tasks` — tasks needing approval.
- `/admin/ai-agents` — seat registry, charter viewer, KPI rollups.
- `/admin/agents/teams` — team configuration.
- `/settings → AI Agents → Charters & Playbooks` — verification surface after deploy.

## Cost & token observability

Every assistant turn reports tokens + dollars in the Visionary Console. Left pane shows 24-hour department-by-department spend. Cost gets attributed back to the seat that initiated the loop.

## Failure modes (system-enforced)

- **Drift toward sales voice.** Stewardship test before send.
- **Cadence overload.** Hard frequency cap at the dispatch layer.
- **Documentation rot.** QA bundle gate for baseline complete + freshness check on valuation source data.
- **Concierge bottleneck.** Documented backup contact per Gold member.
- **Premature tier pitch.** 30-day eligibility lock at the system level.
docs.title: Agent Engine

docs.body:
# Agent Teams — The Three Rules

How the 8 departments coordinate without a human standing in the middle.

## Rule 1 — Own your territory

Every seat has a charter that defines what it decides alone, what it escalates, and what it never does. A seat that can decide does. It does not "loop in" peers for cover. It logs the decision in `pipelineEvents` (or the equivalent department log) and moves on.

The Department Head only enters the loop when:
- the matter crosses the seat's documented authority,
- the matter touches another department's territory,
- a hard-stop condition fires.

The Integrator only enters the loop when the Department Head escalates. The Visionary only enters the loop when the Integrator escalates per the [[Hard Stops + Guardrails|hard stops list]].

## Rule 2 — Direct messages

When a signal touches another seat, the originating seat **pings the owning seat directly** via `hierarchy.ping`. No daisy-chain ("Sales tells the PM to tell Finance"). No status standups. The Integrator routes only when the right owner is ambiguous.

Example (from the Integrator charter):
- Sales surfaces a hot $40k commercial lead.
- Sales `hierarchy.ping`s **Operations** for crew capacity and **Finance** for margin pre-check (against the [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]] floor).
- Customer Success preps onboarding in parallel.
- Brand Guardian confirms the proposal language is on-voice.
- All four threads run concurrently. The Integrator sees the rolled-up result, not the choreography.

This is the "minutes, not days" rule.

## Rule 3 — Parallel start

Independent work begins in parallel by default. Sequential only when there is a real data dependency. If two work items can both start now, they both start now.

The runtime enforces this: agents are stateless between turns and the engine fires triggers concurrently when they don't share a write target. Where there is shared state (e.g., two seats want to update the same customer profile), the engine uses optimistic-locking with a "second writer reads, retries" pattern.

---

## Why this works

The three rules collapse coordination overhead. A 70-seat org with daily standups would die of meetings; an org operating on direct messages + own-your-territory + parallel-start moves like a small team because there is no waiting.

It also creates a clean failure signal. If the Integrator finds itself routinely routing decisions, that's a charter problem (a seat doesn't have the authority it needs) — not a coordination problem.

## Cross-references
- [[Agent Engine]] — runtime & schedules
- [[Visionary Console]] — Marcin's cockpit on top of the team layer
- [[../Charters/Integrator Visionary]] — how the Integrator coordinates the 8 departments
- [[Hard Stops + Guardrails]] — when no rule overrides escalation
docs.title: Agent Teams — The Three Rules

docs.body:
# API Surface — tRPC Routers

> Source: `HP-Estimator-app/server/routers/*.ts`. Composition root: `server/routers.ts`.
> All routers run on tRPC v11.6.0 with Zod 4 validation and superjson serialization.

## Root composition

The root router (`server/routers.ts`) mounts 27 sub-routers. Each is auth-gated by default (operator JWT cookie); customer portal routes use a separate magic-link cookie.

## Router Inventory

### Customer & Pipeline
- **`customers`** — find-by-email-or-phone, find-or-create-from-call, list, get, update, mergeStub, listWithActivity (unread-aware).
- **`opportunities`** — CRUD, importCsv (HousecallPro mapping), kanban moves, listByStage, importBatch, archive.
- **`pipelineEvents`** — append-only event reads.
- **`properties`** — CRUD, healthScore, enroll/upgrade/cancel membership, listByCustomer.
- **`priorityTranslation`** — Roadmap Generator (NOW/SOON/WAIT) — `getCtaContext`, `assertVoice`, `cancelPendingFollowupsForCustomer`.

### Money path
- **`estimate`** — calc engine (17 phases), rewriteCustomItem, addCustomItem, present view; margin enforcement is server-side via `enforceMarginFloor()`.
- **`invoices`** — list, get, create, send, markPaid, aging.
- **`payments`** — Stripe payment intents, deposit auto-redirect.
- **`financials`** — P&L, expense breakdown by `scope`, sendReminder (gated through automation engine).
- **`expenses`** — CRUD, scope = `job | business`.
- **`reporting`** — exports, KPI rollups.

### 360° Method
- **`threeSixty`** — getMembership360, list memberships, listByCustomer, listByProperty, listJobsByMembership, adjustLaborBank, updateMembershipNotes, pauseMembership, resumeMembership, getMembershipStripeStatus.
- **`workOrders`** — listGlobal (filter by type/status), listJobsByMembership, completion (writes back hpOpportunityId).
- **`schedule`** — booking, reschedule, baseline visit, calendar feed; calls onAppointmentBooked when Baseline/Consultation created (commit `2d63e34`).

### Portal
- **`portal`** — getDashboard, getMembership360, getTeamInfo, getRecentProjectCompletion, sendMagicLink, verifyToken, autoLoginFromStripeSession, approveEstimate, getCustomerJobProgress, submitServiceRequest, uploadPhoto, createSetupIntent, confirmCardSetup, request*.
- **`booking`** — `/book` ingest, deriveSource, sendBookingInquiryAck.
- **`inbox`** — unifiedFeed.getByCustomer, customerList.listWithActivity, linkToCustomer, conversation thread, send message.
- **`phone`** — getSettings, updateSettings, testCall, voicemail handling, recording proxy URL.
- **`gmail`** — OAuth connect, ingest poll, label management.

### Marketing & Communications
- **`emailTemplates`** — CRUD; pre-filled with HP defaults (magic link, estimate approval, job sign-off, change order, magic link).
- **`campaigns`** — recipients, send batches.
- **`automationRules`** — rule builder UI, log viewer (25 pre-seeded rules).
- **`notificationPreferences`** — per-staff toggle map.
- **`notifications`** — bell.

### Integrations
- **`quickbooks`** — OAuth connect, draftInvoice, syncCustomer, fetchPaymentStatus, bulk sync.
- **`uploads`** — S3 presign for photos / docs.

### Settings
- **`appSettings`** — single-row settings reader / writer.

## Cross-router Patterns

### Server-side margin enforcement
`enforceMarginFloor()` (in `server/lib/projectEstimator/`) runs after Claude returns an estimate proposal. Floor: 30% on $2k+ hard cost, 40% under. Confidence gate routes to `delivered | needs_review | needs_info` per [[Customer Lifecycle]].

### Approval gates everywhere a customer message originates
The Lead Nurturer and every agent draft land in `/admin/agents/drafts` with status `ready` and wait for the operator's tap. No autosend to customers without human approval.

### Cadence pause hooks
`cancelPendingFollowupsForCustomer()` and `pauseCadenceForCustomer()` flip matching `agentDrafts` rows to `cancelled` when:
- `appointment.scheduled`
- `customer.replied`
- `subscription.created`
- `estimate.approved`
- `customer.declined`

### Auto-link inbound to customer
Every inbound call/SMS routes through `findOrCreateCustomerFromCall` — normalized phone match, stub creation if missed, conversation linkage.

### Visionary Console
`/admin/visionary` cockpit calls `agentTeams.assignTask` and `agentTeams.broadcast`. Tool calls render inline; cost and tokens shown per turn; mobile-first layout. See [[Visionary Console]].

## REST Webhook Surface

Outside tRPC, the server exposes:
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/inbound` → TwiML route.
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/voicemail` → voicemail capture + owner notify.
- `POST /api/twilio/voice/fallback` → 2-stage fall-through to AI service / system voicemail.
- `POST /api/twilio/recording/:sid` → S3-proxied recording stream with Basic Auth.
- `POST /api/stripe/webhook` (primary on `pro.handypioneers.com`) + fallback (`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET_FALLBACK`) for the Manus-space funnel.
- `POST /api/360/checkout` + `/api/360/portfolio-checkout` + `/api/360/event` — 360° funnel endpoints.
- `GET /api/health` — DB connection, email sender readiness, Gmail OAuth status.
- `GET /api/integrations/google-business/callback` and `/api/integrations/qbo/callback` — OAuth returns.
- `GET /360-welcome` — confirmation session endpoint for funnel.
docs.title: API Surface — tRPC Routers

docs.body:
# Customer Lifecycle — Path A → Path B

The money path. Two doors, one destination.

## Two paths

### Path A — Project work
A homeowner contacts HP about a specific project. We deliver a walkthrough, a Roadmap (NOW/SOON/WAIT), a written scope of work, and the work itself. This is the door, not the destination.

### Path B — 360° Method membership
After a Path A engagement (or directly from the funnel), the homeowner enrolls in the 360° Method. Bronze / Silver / Gold tier × monthly / quarterly / annual cadence. This is where the relationship economics, the brand, and the customer outcome all win.

Every Path A engagement is engineered to qualify and convert into Path B within 90 days.

## Money path diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Inbound — web form, GBP, Meta DM, referral, /book, missed call] --> B[AI SDR triage + ICP score]
  B --> C{ICP fit?}
  C -- No --> D[Out-of-area decline / referral]
  C -- Yes --> E[Customer Experience Lead — in-person walkthrough]
  E --> F[Roadmap — NOW/SOON/WAIT]
  F --> G[Project Estimator — confidence gate]
  G -- High confidence --> H[Estimate delivered]
  G -- Medium --> I[needs_review — Marcin notified]
  G -- Low --> J[needs_info — questions to customer]
  I --> H
  J --> H
  H --> K{Customer choice}
  K -- Approve --> L[Stripe deposit invoice]
  K -- Schedule walkthrough --> M[In-person scope confirm]
  M --> H
  L --> N[Operations dispatch — internal-first]
  N --> O[Job complete + photo QA + sign-off]
  O --> P[Final invoice]
  P --> Q[NPS survey T+48h]
  Q --> R{Path B offer in portal}
  R -- Enroll --> S[Stripe subscription]
  S --> T[Baseline visit ≤14 days]
  T --> U[Member portal active — 360° cadence]
  U --> V[Annual valuation report — anniversary month]
  V --> U
  R -- Not yet --> W[Nurture cadence — 90-day window]
  W --> R
```

## Stage mechanics

### Inbound capture (one inbox per role)
Every source funnels into a single Leads inbox. Source tagged via `deriveSource()` in `server/routers/leads.ts`. Sources currently funneled in: Online Request, Roadmap Generator, Inbound Call, Missed Call, Voicemail, Membership Intent, Baseline Walkthrough, Manual entry, Contact Form / Referral.

Adding a new source = a row in `deriveSource()` and a `SOURCE_META` entry in `LeadsPage.tsx`. **Never** a new top-level page.

### AI SDR triage
- Look up `customers.findByEmailOrPhone`; create-or-attach.
- `opportunities.create` with stage `new_inquiry`, source channel, raw message.
- Run ICP enrichment (occupation, neighborhood, advisor signals).
- Draft first-touch reply (templates L-1 through L-4 in [[../Charters/Sales|Sales charter]]).
- Queue task for Customer Experience Lead.
- TTFC target: < 15 min business hours, < 12 hr overnight.

### Confidence gate (estimate)
Every estimate ships through one of three paths:
- **High confidence** → status `delivered`, customer sees range immediately, `concierge_estimate_ready` draft queues for approval.
- **Medium confidence** → status `needs_review`, Marcin notified via `notifyOwner`. Customer sees "your range is being finalized" copy.
- **Low confidence** → status `needs_info`. Customer sees "your Concierge will be in touch shortly".

Customer never sees a sub-floor estimate. `enforceMarginFloor()` validates server-side regardless of what Claude returned.

### Customer-facing pricing rules
- Always a range — `low — high`. Never "starting at."
- ±25% discovery buffer baked in.
- Never expose internal margin, markup multipliers, or hourly rates.
- Always show what's included AND what's not.

### Two-path CTA on every project page
1. **Proceed with this project** — opens scheduling for project commencement.
2. **Request a walkthrough first** — opens scheduling for an in-person scope confirmation.

Either is "the right answer." Never pressure the customer toward Proceed.

### Cadence pause hooks
The Project Estimator's T+4h..T+10d cadence cancels automatically on any of:
- `appointment.scheduled`
- `customer.replied` (inbound SMS or email)
- `subscription.created` (joined 360° Method)
- `estimate.approved` (clicked Proceed)
- `customer.declined` (operator-set)

Pause is triggered via `pauseCadenceForCustomer()` which flips matching `agentDrafts` rows to `cancelled` with the reason recorded.

### Path A → Path B handoff (Customer Success)
On `opportunities.statusChanged → won`:
1. `customers.update` — provision portal account, tier assignment, anniversary date.
2. `tasks.create` — schedule baseline visit within 14 days.
3. Welcome email + portal credentials within 60 minutes of signature.
4. Day-30 NPS survey scheduled.

90-day post-completion conversion sequence anchors on the home's specific record, never on a calendar promotion.

### Stewardship pacing (customer surfaces)
- No "next available in 2 hours" offers.
- Default appointment offers start **5–10 days out**, weekdays only.
- Mornings (10am) and afternoons (2pm). Four windows is the cap.
- Pre-arrival reminders: 24h before, 1h before. No marketing follow-ups about the same walkthrough.

### Single primary CTA on Roadmap (server-resolved)
| Customer state | Variant | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Default | `baseline_walkthrough` | Schedule walkthrough |
| Estimate sent or viewed | `approve_estimate` | Review now |
| Estimate approved + linked HP opportunity | `track_project` | Open project |
| 360° member, no upcoming visit | `schedule_member_visit` | Request a visit |

No upsell stack below it. No countdowns.

## Cross-references
- [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]
- [[../Library/360° Method Pricing|360° Method Pricing]]
- [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]]
- [[../Charters/Sales|Sales charter]]
- [[../Charters/Customer Success|Customer Success charter]]
docs.title: Customer Lifecycle — Path A → Path B

docs.body:
# Hard Stops + Guardrails

What no AI seat ever does without Marcin's call.

## The four global hard stops

1. **Spend money or commit to spend.** No vendor contracts, no compensation, no capital outlay. Pre-model the math and present one recommendation.
2. **Make external commitments in Marcin's name.** Press, legal signatures, contracts, public statements, partnership MOUs, regulatory filings, customer commitments. Drafts only.
3. **Modify a Charter file.** Charters change only by explicit Marcin instruction.
4. **Activate a new agent or seat.** All AI seats start `draft_queue`. Activation is sequenced and approved.

Anything that touches one of those four lines is a hard stop. Surface it as one sentence with one recommendation.

## Per-domain hard stops

### Customer-facing copy
- Never auto-send a first-touch reply to an inbound lead.
- Never use forbidden vocab (handyman, estimate, free, cheap, affordable, easy, fix, repair, best, save, discount, limited time). The Lead Nurturer surfaces violations as advisory warnings.
- Never auto-send to a member with an open complaint flag.
- Never pitch a tier upgrade in the first 30 days.
- Never send the same customer >2 messages in 7 days outside an active project.
- Never use exclamation marks in stewardship copy.

### Pricing
- Never approve an estimate below 30% gross margin (40% if hard cost < $2k).
- Never re-rate internal labor below $150/hr.
- Never approve a margin override autonomously — always escalate to Marcin.
- Never expose internal margin, markup multipliers, or hourly rates to customers.

### Operations
- Never mark a baseline complete without QA photos and signed checklist.
- Never dispatch a vendor whose insurance has lapsed.
- Never activate a vendor without PM sign-off.

### Finance
- Never invent a category — post to `Uncategorized–Review` and ping Margin Monitor.
- Never recategorize a prior period without CPA approval.
- Never write off bad debt — escalate to Integrator.
- Never initiate a wire / ACH / transfer.

### Technology
- AI never executes deploys, restarts, or config changes on production. Drafts and recommendations only.
- AI never auto-rotates secrets — drafts the rotation plan; Marcin executes.
- AI never autonomously approves a "won't fix" on a security finding.

### Marketing
- Never launch, edit live budgets, or pause campaigns autonomously.
- Never bid on competitor brand terms without explicit approval.
- Never run anything outside Clark County, WA in v1.
- Never publish content; brief and draft only — Marcin publishes.

### Vendor / Trades
- Never commit work, rates, or volume in an outreach message.
- Never onboard a vendor without verified license + COI + W-9 + signed agreement + scope sheet.
- Never substitute external for internal labor without a logged reason code.

### Strategy / Expansion
- Never contact anyone in a candidate market.
- Never declare HP "ready for outbound" without 2 consecutive quarters above all readiness thresholds.

## Approval gates everywhere a customer message originates

The Lead Nurturer never auto-sends. Drafts land in `/admin/agents/drafts` with status `ready` and wait for the operator's tap. This bar applies to every future agent — translation, scheduling reminders, billing chase, anything that speaks in the brand's voice.

## Guardrails (system-enforced, not policy-enforced)

- **Margin floor** — `enforceMarginFloor()` runs server-side regardless of what Claude returned.
- **Frequency cap** — dispatch layer enforces ≤2 messages per customer per 7 days outside an active project.
- **Onboarding lock** — tier-upgrade sequences gated behind 30-day eligibility lock.
- **QA bundle gate** — baseline cannot be marked complete without verified photos + checklist + technician notes.
- **Valuation freshness** — annual valuation cannot ship without a freshness check on source data.
- **Stewardship test** — every outbound template includes a "would a chief of staff send this to a principal?" check before ship.

## Cross-references
- [[../Memory|Memory § Hard Stops]]
- [[../Charters/Integrator Visionary|Integrator + Visionary charter — decision matrix]]
- [[../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]
- [[../Library/Estimator Math|Estimator Math]]
docs.title: Hard Stops + Guardrails

docs.body:
# Cloudflare

## What it does
DNS, edge cache, WAF, rate limiting, DDoS for all HP domains.

## Domains under management
- `handypioneers.com` (apex + `www`)
- `pro.handypioneers.com`
- `client.handypioneers.com`
- `360.handypioneers.com`

## DNS records
- `pro` and `client` → CNAME to Railway public domain.
- Apex → Railway or hosted marketing site.
- DKIM / SPF / DMARC → [[Resend]] (hand-rotated).

## API access
- Token rotated last session (per HANDOFF_CHECKLIST "Existing Keys to Rotate").
- Old DNS-only token (`cfut_Rl4tz…`) **still to revoke** in Cloudflare Dashboard → My Profile → API Tokens.

## WAF posture
- Allow + log mode for the operator app; block on bot signatures and geographic anomalies for the customer portal.
- Rate limiting wraps `/api/twilio/*` and `/api/stripe/webhook` — burst protection only; do not block legitimate webhooks.

## Agent dependencies
- AI System Integrity (Technology) — reads health signals; never edits Cloudflare config.
- AI Security (Technology) — flags Cloudflare events that look like enumeration / abuse.

# Google Business Profile

## What it does
Read and reply to GBP reviews; pull profile insights (calls, direction requests, profile views) into Marketing dashboards.

## Status
**Not yet wired.** Blocking activation per [[../../HANDOFF_CHECKLIST|HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md]] P0 #1.

## Setup steps (Marcin action)
1. `business.google.com` → sign in with HP Google account → location → Settings → Manage users → add `reviews@handypioneers.com` as Owner.
2. `console.cloud.google.com` → project "Handy Pioneers Prod" → APIs & Services → Library → enable **Google Business Profile API**.
3. Credentials → Create OAuth client → Web application.
4. Authorized redirect URI: `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/integrations/google-business/callback`.
5. Paste back: `GBP_CLIENT_ID`, `GBP_CLIENT_SECRET`.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Community / Reviews (Marketing) — drafts review replies, never sends.
- AI QA (Operations) — pulls reputation signals into per-job scorecards.

## Voice rules
Review replies follow the same banned-word list. Per [[../../Library/Brand Voice Rules|Brand Voice Rules]]: no "thanks for the review!" cliché, no exclamation marks, identity-first, specific to the customer's home if known.

## Known limitations
- Same Google Cloud project is reused for [[Google Ads]] (P1 #5).

# Meta (Facebook + Instagram)

## What it does
Read paid-ad performance, propose budget reallocations, draft creative, monitor page DMs.

## Status
**Not yet wired.** P1 #4 in [[../../HANDOFF_CHECKLIST|HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md]].

## Setup
- App type: Business; name: "Handy Pioneers AI Assistant".
- Marketing API enabled.
- **System User token** (long-lived) — preferred over user tokens because it survives staff turnover and 2FA. Scopes: `ads_read`, `ads_management`, `business_management`.

## Env Vars
- `META_APP_ID`
- `META_APP_SECRET`
- `META_SYSTEM_USER_TOKEN`
- `META_AD_ACCOUNT_ID` (format `act_XXXXXXXXXX`)

## Server tools
- `meta.draftAdCreative` (Marketing → AI Paid Ads).
- `meta.draftPageReply` (Sales → AI SDR; never publishes).

## Hard rules
- No autonomous launch / pause / budget edit. Drafts only — Marcin approves.
- Never bid on competitor brand terms without explicit approval.
- Never run anything outside Clark County in v1.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Paid Ads (Marketing).
- AI SDR (Sales).
- Brand Guardian (Marketing) — pre-clears creative.

# QuickBooks Online

## What it does
Sync invoices, payments, customers, expenses; provide the source of truth for monthly close, tax, and audit.

## Status
**OAuth scaffold shipped** (commit `f13be41`) — bulk sync stubbed. Production credentials and `QBO_REALM_ID` not yet pasted.

## Setup
1. **QBO Simple Start** at `quickbooks.intuit.com` — $30/mo.
2. `developer.intuit.com` → My Apps → Create app → "QuickBooks Online and Payments" → "Handy Pioneers".
3. Scope: `com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting`.
4. **Switch to Production Settings** before issuing keys.
5. Redirect URI: `https://pro.handypioneers.com/api/integrations/qbo/callback`.

## Env Vars
- `QBO_CLIENT_ID`
- `QBO_CLIENT_SECRET`
- `QBO_REALM_ID` (from the connect-flow handshake)

## Database
`qbTokens` table holds OAuth access + refresh tokens per environment.

## Server tools (per Finance charter)
- `qbo.draftInvoice` — autonomous draft from approved estimate.
- `qbo.syncCustomer` — autonomous, with merge.
- `qbo.categorizeExpense` — autonomous when vendor + memo match prior pattern.
- `qbo.fetchPaymentStatus` — read-only.

## Sandbox → Prod path
1. Connect sandbox first; verify draft invoice flow on a fixture customer.
2. Run a 7-day reconciliation pass against a sandbox bank feed.
3. Switch app to Production keys; re-OAuth.
4. CPA fractional (HUMAN seat) signs off on first month-end close before AI Bookkeeping operates without supervision.

## Hard rules
- Never recategorize a prior-period transaction without CPA approval.
- Never write off bad debt — escalate to Integrator.
- Never invent a category — post to `Uncategorized–Review` and ping Margin Monitor.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Bookkeeping (Finance) — primary operator.
- AI Margin Monitor (Finance) — gates invoice draft.
- AI Cash Flow (Finance) — DSO + payable aging.
- CPA / Tax (HUMAN, fractional) — period close + tax position.
docs.title: QuickBooks Online

# Resend

## What it does
All transactional + customer-facing email. Replaced Gmail-as-sender (PR #48 merged 2026-04-27).

## Library
- Direct `fetch` calls in `server/lib/email/resend.ts`.
- `nodemailer` v8.0.5 retained only for legacy SMTP fallback paths and Gmail OAuth ingest on `help@`.

## Env Vars
- `RESEND_API_KEY` — required. `/api/health` reports `email.sender: resend_ready` when set.

## Sender Discipline
- `help@handypioneers.com` — default. Customer-facing emails that should be replyable: estimates, invoices, magic links, Roadmap delivery (`server/lib/priorityTranslation/email.ts:38`), Project Estimator notifications (`server/lib/projectEstimator/messaging.ts:216`).
- `noreply@handypioneers.com` — RESERVED. Used only when reply doesn't make sense. The Resend strategy doc enforces this at the lib level.

Critical fix landed 2026-04-28 in consolidation: defaults were `noreply@`, flipped to `help@` on the two customer-facing senders above.

## Domain Setup
- Sender domain: `handypioneers.com`.
- DKIM, SPF, DMARC configured via Cloudflare DNS.
- DNS owner: Marcin (single account).

## Templates
Pre-filled in `appSettings`:
- Magic link
- Estimate approval
- Job sign-off
- Change order approval
+ four more transactional bodies (commit `75b5e5f`).

All templates use `{{variable}}` interpolation; the automation engine injects `companyName`, `supportPhone`, `supportEmail`, `websiteUrl`, `portalUrl`, `addressLine1/2`, `googleReviewLink` automatically (commit `1e944ef`).

## Agent dependencies
- Lead Nurturer — drafts go to `/admin/agents/drafts` first, never autosent.
- AI Onboarding (Customer Success) — welcome + day-30 cadence.
- AI Annual Valuation — anniversary cover note.
- AI Bookkeeping — invoice send (gated through Margin Monitor approval).

## Known limitations
- Inbound on `help@` still polls via Gmail OAuth; not yet ingested through Resend's inbound webhook. Migration not prioritized.

# Snyk

## What it does
Daily vulnerability scan of the HP Estimator monorepo. Surfaces Critical / High / Medium / Low findings to AI Security.

## Status
**Not yet wired.** P1 #8 in [[../../HANDOFF_CHECKLIST|HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md]].
GitHub-side check is "known-flaky" — referenced in the 2026-04-28 marketing readiness audit; admin-merged PR #17 with Snyk failing.

## Setup
1. `snyk.io` → Sign up with GitHub → authorize the Handy Pioneers GitHub org.
2. Settings → Service accounts → Create → "HP Estimator CI" → copy token.

## Env Vars
- `SNYK_TOKEN`

## Server tool
- `security.runScan` — daily 04:00 cron.
- `security.flagFinding` — append to `tasks.create` queue when severity ≥ High.

## SLAs (per Technology charter)
- Critical: triage within 24h, close within 7 days. Target: zero open Critical at any time.
- High: triage within 24h, close within 30 days. Target: < 3 open at any time.
- Medium / Low: weekly batch review.

## 90-day token window
Refresh draft fires day 75; hard fail at day 88. Drafts a refresh task for Marcin — never auto-rotates.

## Agent dependencies
- AI Security (Technology).
