docs.body:
# Memory — Handy Pioneers AI Onboarding Doc

> The single most important file in this vault. Every AI session begins here.
> Read this end-to-end before answering anything.
> Last updated 2026-04-28 with comprehensive ingestion from `agent/memory/` + HP-Estimator-app.

---

## 1. Who Marcin Is

Marcin Micek is the **CEO and Visionary** of Handy Pioneers. He is non-technical by trade — he directs in plain language. He should never need to open a terminal, Cursor, or run Claude Code manually. That's the Integrator's job.

He thinks in business outcomes, not code. Frame all responses and questions in business terms. He prefers terse, decisive communication: 5 bullets max, one ask per escalation, no filler. He pushes until tasks are complete; never suggest he rest or stop.

His operating partner inside this vault is the Integrator/Chief of Staff role played by the Main AI Agent (Cowork). The Integrator decomposes vision into outcomes, spawns sub-agents, executes, and reports only on results, blockers, or true escalations.

The full CEO operating doctrine is in the global CLAUDE.md and the [[Charters/Integrator Visionary]] charter.

---

## 2. What Handy Pioneers Does

Handy Pioneers is a **premium home stewardship company** serving Clark County, Washington (Vancouver, WA base). We are not a handyman service.

Core promise: a homeowner's property compounds in value when proactively stewarded. Reactive ownership erodes value; proactive stewardship grows it.

Two paths:
- **Path A — Project work.** One-off engagements. The door, not the destination.
- **Path B — The 360° Method.** Membership model that converts Path A customers into multi-year stewardship relationships. Continuity is where the economics, the brand, and the customer outcome all win.

Every Path A engagement is engineered to qualify and convert into Path B within 90 days.

**Current state:** 15-person team. Active in Vancouver but not yet systematized. 360° Method funnel built (`360.handypioneers.com`) but no members sold yet. HP Estimator is in production replacing Housecall Pro. 458 imported HCP customers awaiting structured re-engagement.

**Vision:** Scale nationally as remote-managed, locally-executed. Replicate Vancouver's playbook into new markets. HP Estimator is the nucleus and is being built to white-label and sell to other operators.

---

## 3. Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)

**Self-directed high-performers** in Clark County, WA — $150K+ HHI, $600K–$1M+ home, NOT W-2 employees. Concretely: business owners, investors, attorneys, physicians, professional athletes, executives with equity.

The defining trait is not income — it is **systematization behavior**. Our ICP has systematized their finances (CPA), health (trainer / nutritionist), calendar (assistant), team. The home is the last unsystematized asset they own. They are uncomfortable with that gap. We close it.

**Positioning frame:** Handy Pioneers is the "home advisor" that rounds out their advisor stack. Trust-shopping, not price-shopping. Identity-driven, not fear-driven.

Channels that work: luxury real-estate referrals, wealth-manager / CPA partnerships, country club / business journal / chamber. Channels that don't: mass-market digital ads.

ICP litmus tests:
- **Copy:** would a busy attorney / surgeon / business owner self-identify in the first headline?
- **Channel:** does it reach high-performers with existing advisor stacks, not DIY homeowners?
- **Service decision:** does this look like what their wealth manager or CPA would produce?

---

## 4. The 360° Method

Marcin's proprietary methodology. The IP. Pre-launch — funnel built, no members yet. Repo: `github.com/360Method/hp-360-funnel` (public).

**Three phases:**
- **AWARE** — comprehensive assessment, living health record. NOT a legal home inspection (mandatory disclaimer).
- **ACT** — priority roadmap (NOW/SOON/WAIT) + seasonal cadence (4 visits/year — spring, summer, fall, winter). Seasonal cadence is the perpetual core.
- **ADVANCE** — targeted upgrades that preserve long-term value.

**NOW / SOON / WAIT:**
- **NOW** — material loss or risk if left alone.
- **SOON** — defined window before escalation.
- **WAIT** — monitored only.

**Tiers (display labels — internal keys preserved):**
- **Essential** (was Bronze) — $59/mo · $169/quarter · $588/year ($49/mo annual). 2 visits (Spring + Fall), Annual 360° Home Scan, 5%/3%/1.5% discounts by job size, $0 labor bank.
- **Full Coverage** (was Silver) ⭐ Most Popular — $99/mo · $279/quarter · $948/year ($79/mo annual). 4 visits, $200 labor bank credit, 8%/5%/2.5% discounts, instant estimate conversion, maintenance documentation.
- **Maximum Protection** (was Gold) — $149/mo · $419/quarter · $1,428/year ($119/mo annual). 4 visits + priority, $500 labor bank credit, 12%/8%/4% discounts, priority scheduling, dedicated account manager (Member Concierge), contractor rate negotiation, equity documentation.

**Two audiences:** homeowners + multifamily / property owners (separate funnel pages). Portfolio variants: Exterior Shield / Full Coverage / Portfolio Max with Interior Unit Add-On at $49/door/yr.

**Stripe price IDs are env-only today** (Railway env). CMS surface for tier mgmt is post-launch.

---

## 5. Brand Voice — Hard Rules

Authoritative doc: `BRAND_TONALITY_AND_DIRECTION.md` in the handy-pioneers website repo root. The vault summary is in [[Library/Brand Voice Rules]].

**Anchor phrases (use these):** standard of care, asset, steward / stewardship, consultation, written scope of work, complimentary, vetted tradesmen / skilled tradesmen / licensed specialists, proactive, seasonal cadence, compound / compounds in value.

**Forbidden vocabulary (never use):** handyman, estimate, free, cheap, affordable, fix, repair (as primary framing), simple, easy, "we are the best" (any superlative), save, discount, limited time.

Substitutions: free estimate → walkthrough, on-site review · fix/repair → restore, address, attend to · cheap/affordable → drop entirely · handyman → specialist, craftsperson, project manager · limited time → drop entirely.

**Anchor headline:** "Your Home Deserves the Same Standard of Care You Bring to Everything Else." Subhead: "Every season. Every system. Every detail — taken care of." Eyebrow: "For homeowners who hold their asset to a higher standard." CTA: "The First Conversation Costs Nothing."

**Voice posture:** identity-first. Calm, certain, adult. Never breathless, salesy, folksy. First-name basis always. No exclamation marks in stewardship copy.

**Stewardship test before every send:** would a chief of staff send this to a principal? If no, rewrite.

The `assertVoice()` helper warns when forbidden words appear in operator copy — never in customer's own words.

---

## 6. Org Chart — 31 Seats

Interactive HTML reference: `OneDrive\Documents\Claude\Projects\Marcin Micek\hp-org-chart-v2.html`.

**Top of house:**
- **Visionary** — Marcin Micek (Owner)
- **Integrator** — Main AI Agent (Cowork)
- **8 Departments** — reporting to Integrator except Strategy / Expansion which reports directly to Visionary

**The 8 departments:**
1. Sales & Lead Management
2. Marketing
3. Operations
4. Customer Success
5. Vendor / Trades Network
6. Technology / Platform
7. Finance
8. Strategy / Expansion

Total: **31 seats** in the registry (per `HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md` Seat Registry). 6 human seats; remainder AI (one hybrid: External Contractor Network).

**Human seats only:** Customer Experience Lead (in-person consults + walkthroughs) · Project Manager (Vancouver) · Internal Tradesmen · CPA / Tax (external fractional) · Member Concierge (Gold tier) · Software Engineer (Marcin + future technical partner).

**Three operating rules:**
1. **Own your territory.** Each seat decides within its charter.
2. **Direct messages.** No daisy-chain; ping the owning seat.
3. **Parallel start.** Independent work begins concurrently by default.

See [[Architecture/Agent Teams (3 rules)]] and [[Diagrams/Org Chart Hierarchy]].

---

# Migration History

> 65 migrations in `HP-Estimator-app/drizzle/`. Drizzle-kit-generated names in `0000_xxx … 0064_xxx`. Below: rationale and rough lineage. Some early names are auto-generated codenames (`sweet_quasar`, `lonely_elektra`); only the meaningful ones are annotated.

## 0000 – 0029 — Foundation
Initial schema: customers, opportunities, conversations, messages, callLogs, gmailTokens, portal* tables, snapshots, adminAllowlist, serviceZipCodes. Built up across the first month of the project.

## 0030 – 0036 — Customer + Property hierarchy
- **0030–0036** — properties table, customerAddresses, portalServiceRequests, portalJobMilestones, portalJobUpdates, portalJobSignOffs, portalChangeOrders, portalDocuments. Built the customer-centric SSOT model (see [[Decisions/2026-04-22 — HP Estimator as the Nucleus]]).

## 0037 — Path A ↔ Path B bridge
Added `hpOpportunityId` to `threeSixtyWorkOrders` and `membershipId` to `opportunities`. The single migration that makes Path A → Path B reporting coherent. See [[Decisions/2026-04-16 — Customer ↔ Property ↔ 360 Membership bridge]].

## 0038 — Onboarding modal
Portal onboarding modal state.

## 0039 — Off-cycle visit request
`portalServiceRequests` extension for off-cycle 360° visit asks.

## 0040 — Portal photo URLs
`photoUrls` column on `portalServiceRequests` for the photo attachment area (up to 8 photos, S3 upload).

## 0041 — Phone settings
`phoneSettings` table — voicemail prompt, call greeting, business hours, voicemail mode. Carried by commit `2c441f2` and the `ensurePhoneTables` boot-time guard (commit `b60ec4c`).

## 0042 – 0048 — Quality and stability
Mixed migrations across automations, financial scopes, schedule events, expense scope, voicemail recording URL on `callLogs`.

## 0049 — Recording metadata
Stripe / Twilio recording metadata (re-uploaded to app S3 — `recordingAppUrl`).

## 0050 — Married supernaut (auto-named)
Cleanup migration.

## 0051 — Tier rename
Bronze → Essential / Silver → Full Coverage / Gold → Maximum Protection display labels. Internal keys preserved. See [[Decisions/2026-04-18 — Tier rename Bronze→Essential, Silver→Full Coverage, Gold→Maximum Protection]].

## 0052 — Email templates
8 email template fields added to `appSettings`. 4 transactional bodies pre-filled (magic link, estimate approval, job sign-off, change order). See [[Decisions/2026-04-17 — Settings + Automations as operator SSOT]].

## 0053 — Campaigns
`campaigns`, `campaignRecipients`, `campaignSends` tables for the outbound campaign engine.

## 0054 — Settings fields
Additional `appSettings` fields for branding and pricing defaults. **No-op safe** on missing tables (commit `c5a6b17`).

## 0055 — Phone forwarding
Phone forwarding settings polish; **no-op safe**.

## 0056 — Automation categories
Lifecycle category grouping for automation rules.

## 0057 — Opportunities auto-archive
Stage cleanup automation.

## 0058 — Priority translations
Roadmap Generator (NOW/SOON/WAIT) backend port from the lead-magnet site (commit `8652c94`).

## 0059 — Repair phone tables
Idempotent guard for phone tables drifting from the schema; complements `ensurePhoneTables`.

## 0060 — Lead routing — opportunity assignment
Three-role pipeline: `assignedConsultantId`, `assignedPmId`, `memberConcierge` columns. See [[Decisions/2026-04-23 — Three-role lead routing]].

## 0061 — Lead routing — notifications table
Customer-centric notifications: `customerId`, `opportunityId` (secondary), unread tracking.

## 0062 — Lead routing — pipeline events
Append-only event log for stage changes (`pipelineEvents`).

## 0063 — Lead routing — seeds
Initial role-aware notification preferences and routing rules.

## 0064 — Portal continuity flag
`bypassAutoNurture` on `customers` for per-customer override (machine yields to the human read).

## Pending
- **0065 / 0066** — charter + KPI + playbook tables. Deployment script: `node scripts/seed-ai-agents.mjs` then `node scripts/seed-charters.mjs` (writes `docs/agents/SEAT_AUDIT.md`). Auto-applied via `ensureCharterTables` on boot.

## Operational notes

- **Drift recovery.** When drizzle-kit drifts from the live DB, the server uses boot-time guards (`ensurePhoneTables`, `ensureCharterTables`) to patch missing tables before request handling.
- **Pre-start migration.** Railway runs `drizzle-kit migrate` before server boot.
- **Healthcheck.** `/api/health` returns `db: connected` once migrations are clean.

# Roadmap

## Now (this week — 2026-04-29)
- **Marketing launch** — green-lit per [[Decisions/2026-04-28 — Greenlight marketing launch]]. Money path is clean on `main`; prod externals are green.
- **Yellow follow-up.** Re-verify `loadReportText` Roadmap PDF parser. If still stubbed, hold the Roadmap funnel out of campaigns until fixed.

## Phase 4 — Activation (next 30 days)
Move agent seats from `draft_queue` → `active` in this order:
1. **AI SDR** — already drafting; flip to active per the auto-send tier rules.
2. **AI Dispatch** — once A2P 10DLC clears.
3. **AI Bookkeeping** — once QBO production keys + `QBO_REALM_ID` are pasted.
4. **AI System Integrity** — health digest + post-deploy verification.
5. **AI Margin Monitor** — server-side floor is already enforced; activate the audit-trail logging.
6. **AI Onboarding (Customer Success)** — first member triggers the 7-day cadence.
7. **AI Membership Success** — silent-member sweep, renewal letters.
8. **AI Community / Reviews (Marketing)** — once GBP credentials are pasted.

Source: [[HANDOFF_CHECKLIST|HANDOFF_CHECKLIST.md]] § "Activation order".

## Phase 5 — White-label / Multi-tenant
The strategic flywheel. See [[Charters/Strategy Expansion|Strategy & Expansion charter]] for the gate.

**Readiness gate** (all sustained for 2 consecutive quarters before any outbound licensing motion):
- Tenant-zero gross margin ≥35% (vs. 30% floor)
- Estimator product uptime ≥99.5%
- Customer NPS ≥50
- Internal nucleus-readiness composite ≥80
- Documented SOP coverage ≥90% of operating surface
- Estimator usable by a non-HP user without Marcin's intervention (proven via tabletop)

**What this requires:**
- CMS surface in HP Estimator for non-technical operators to edit copy, tiers, automations.
- Tenant separation in DB (current schema is single-tenant; multi-tenant plan TBD).
- Branding switch — logo, color palette, fonts, company name, license number, service area, configurable per tenant.
- White-label partner onboarding SOP.
- Tabletop simulation completed with a fictional licensee.

## Phase 6 — Replication into a second metro
After Phase 5 readiness. Same operating system; local crew; remote brain.
Decision criteria live in [[Charters/Strategy Expansion|Strategy & Expansion charter]] § Market Scorecard rubric.

## Open product questions

- **Stripe Terminal vs. Stripe Checkout link** for on-site Consultant payment. Marcin decision pending.
- **Gold-tier Concierge staffing** — Marcin / hired account manager / hybrid. Marcin decision pending.
- **A2P 10DLC sample messages** — 3–5 to draft for Marcin approval before Twilio submission.
- **Brand voice in product copy** — the 360° Membership value prop uses words on the forbidden list (`discount`, `repair`, `save`). Re-evaluate post-launch.

## Open infra rotations

- **HCP API key** — revoke once cutover verified.
- **Old Cloudflare DNS-only token** (`cfut_Rl4tz…`) — revoke in Cloudflare Dashboard.

# SETUP — Read This First

This vault was built in `outputs/HP-Brain/`. Move the entire `HP-Brain` folder to `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\HP-Brain\` before opening it in Obsidian. OneDrive sync gives you cross-machine access automatically.

---

## Step 1 — Move the vault

1. Open File Explorer.
2. Cut the `HP-Brain` folder from the Cowork outputs location.
3. Paste it into `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\`.
4. Final path should be: `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\HP-Brain\`

---

## Step 2 — Open the vault in Obsidian

1. Launch Obsidian.
2. Click **Open folder as vault**.
3. Select `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\HP-Brain`.
4. Trust the author when prompted.

---

## Step 3 — Install the three core plugins

Settings → Community Plugins → Turn on community plugins → Browse:

1. **Dataview** — query notes like a database (used for the Customer Index and Daily Briefs roll-up).
2. **Templater** — auto-fills date stamps and front-matter on new notes.
3. **Smart Connections** — chat with the vault as context, powered by Anthropic.

Enable all three after install.

---

## Step 4 — Configure Smart Connections

1. Settings → Smart Connections.
2. **API Provider:** Anthropic.
3. Paste your Anthropic API key (the same one already in your Railway env).
4. **Embedding model:** default is fine.
5. Hit "Re-embed" once so it indexes the whole vault.

You can now ask questions of the vault directly inside Obsidian.

---

## Step 5 — Connect the Obsidian MCP to Cowork

1. Open Cowork → Settings → MCP Connectors → Add → Obsidian.
2. Vault path: `C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\HP-Brain`
3. Approve.

---

## Step 6 — Set the Cowork user preference

Add this to your Cowork user preferences so every session starts grounded:

```
Before answering any question, search the Obsidian vault at
C:\Users\marci\OneDrive\Documents\HP-Brain for relevant notes.
Always read Memory.md at session start.
Reference customers as [[Customer Name]] when relevant.
Write session summaries to Sessions/.
```

---

## Step 7 — Optional next phase (not built today)

When ready, set up call recording (Fathom or Otter) and route transcripts to a Drive folder. Then ask Claude to wire those transcripts into the vault and auto-populate Customers, Decisions, and Sessions.

---

## What's in the vault right now

- **Memory.md** — the AI onboarding doc. Most important file.
- **Home.md** — daily landing page.
- **Charters/** — 9 department charters, one per seat.
- **Library/** — 7 reference notes (brand voice, ICP, pricing math, org chart, tech stack, etc).
- **Templates/** — 6 paste-ready templates.
- **Customers/, Actions/, Daily Briefs/, Sessions/, Transcripts/** — populated by sync workflows later.

Iterate from here. Nothing is precious.

docs.body:
---
id: HP-DOC-002
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: HP Estimator — Deterministic App + Workflows-in-Git (architecture direction)
status: draft
created: 2026-06-04
updated: 2026-06-04
---

# HP Estimator — Deterministic App + Workflows-in-Git

## Why this doc

Getting the Proactive Path membership funnel + fulfillment working (sell → member →
welcome email → portal) surfaced a structural problem: the app's internal **"AI agents"**
layer was drafting member-facing emails and parking them in a manual **approval queue**
that sat idle, so onboarding never auto-sent. Marcin's direction: **the app should do
deterministic execution; the workflows/instructions should live in the Micek-OS Git repo
as the source of truth; retire the idle in-app agents.**

The agent engine is now **OFF** (an `AGENTS_ENABLED` kill-switch, default off, added
2026-06-02 — gates the trigger bus, the agent scheduler, and the lead-nurturer draft
worker; deterministic schedulers untouched; reversible). This doc scopes the durable
target and a phased path. **It is explicitly sequenced AFTER the 2026-06-15 launch (Rock 1)
so it does not derail launch** — the heavy revamp is post-launch cleanup, not a blocker.

## The principle — two clean layers

1. **The app = deterministic execution.** Transactional flows (membership lifecycle,
   onboarding, scan/visit scheduling, estimates, invoices, transactional comms) are plain
   reliable code that just runs. **No human-approval gate on transactional sends.** This is
   what customers depend on; it must be boringly reliable.
2. **Git (Micek-OS) = the source of truth for the "how."** The member-journey playbook,
   comms templates + voice, seasonal-visit cadence, pricing/margin/discount rules, and SOPs
   live in the repo — version-controlled, edited by Marcin + this OS. This is where the
   thinking lives and evolves.

AI assists at the **edges** (drafting content for Marcin to approve, analysis), invoked
deliberately by Marcin or this OS — not an always-on swarm of in-app agents intercepting
transactional work.

## Current state

- **Deterministic core (working — keep):** Stripe webhook → membership; direct welcome
  email; portal magic-link auth + member dashboard; onboarding modal; estimates / invoices /
  portal; staff 360 roster + New-Enrollments queue + scheduling. Verified live 2026-06-02.
- **Dormant agent layer (OFF):** ~26 backend files, ~8 tables, ~10 admin pages — `ai_agents`
  seats, `ai_agent_tasks` + approval, `agentDrafts` (lead nurturer), trigger bus, scheduler,
  playbooks. Built before the Micek-OS structure. Gated off by `AGENTS_ENABLED`.
- **Coordination gap:** `LAUNCH_PLAN.md` (the app's launch source of truth) still lists "AI
  agent runs active" as a health signal — must be reconciled so a future session doesn't
  re-enable the agents and undo this decision.

## Target architecture

- **App owns deterministic flows:** membership create/renew/cancel, onboarding (welcome
  email + portal link + baseline-scan work order), scan/visit scheduling, labor-bank
  ledger, estimates, invoices, transactional comms.
- **Git holds the canonical "how":** the 360 Member Fulfillment Workflow, comms templates +
  voice, seasonal-visit cadence rules, pricing/margin/discount rules, SOPs.
- **"App references Git workflows" — pragmatic interpretation:** start as a **build-time
  source of truth**, not a runtime fetch. The repo docs are canonical; the app's
  templates/config stay in sync with them (Marcin/Claude edit the doc first, then the app
  follows). Runtime-reading a few items (email templates, cadence config) is a later option
  if the value beats the coupling — not needed first.
- **The agent system: retire.** Keep at most one deliberate primitive — "Claude drafts →
  Marcin approves" for genuinely *strategic* content (e.g. marketing posts), invoked on
  demand — not an always-on in-app engine.

## Phased path (post-launch)

- **Phase 0 — done (2026-06-02):** agent engine OFF; onboarding deterministic and verified.
- **Phase B1 — reconcile + document (small, do soon):**
  - Note in `LAUNCH_PLAN.md` + `connections.md` that the agent engine is intentionally off
    and fulfillment is deterministic.
  - Write the canonical **360 Member Fulfillment Workflow** doc in Git (the member journey,
    each step mapped to where the app executes it) as the source of truth.
  - Move the transactional comms copy (welcome email, etc.) into a Git doc as the canonical
    text, even while the app still hardcodes it — so future edits start in Git.
- **Phase B2 — shrink the surface (post-launch):** archive the unused agent tables / routers
  / admin pages (move to an `archived/` area or delete), keeping any reusable primitive.
  Cuts confusion and maintenance.
- **Phase B3 — optional runtime config (only if it pays off):** let the app read a small set
  of config from Git (email templates, cadence) so editing Git updates the app without a
  code deploy.

## What NOT to do

- Don't rip out the agent system before launch (06-15) — it's off and harmless; retirement
  is post-launch cleanup.
- Don't build a speculative "app reads Git at runtime" engine. Start with Git-as-source-of-
  truth (docs); keep the app deterministic.

## First concrete step (post-launch, on Marcin's go)

Write the **360 Member Fulfillment Workflow** doc in Git: the canonical member journey
(enroll → member record → welcome email + portal link → baseline scan scheduling → seasonal
visits → labor bank → renewal), each step mapped to where the app executes it. That becomes
the source of truth and the spec the app is measured against — and the seed of the
workflows-in-Git model.

## Open decisions for Marcin

- Archive vs delete the dormant agent code (B2).
- How far to take runtime Git-reading (B3) — likely just templates/config, later.
- Whether to keep one "draft → approve" primitive for strategic marketing content.
docs.title: HP Estimator — Deterministic App + Workflows-in-Git

docs.body:
# Handy Pioneers — Website Mock v1
**Built:** April 2026 | **Status:** Review draft — not production-ready

---

## Site Architecture

Single-page homepage with anchor navigation + three destination pages.

### Entry point
**`index.html`** — One long-scroll page. All nav links are anchor links to sections on this page. Every section funnels visitors down whichever path fits their situation.

| Anchor | Section | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `#hero` | Hero | Locked H1 / subhead / eyebrow. Two path CTAs scroll to #project-path / #proactive-path |
| `#transformation` | Reactive → Steward | Emotional arc: naming the gap, positioning the shift |
| `#method` | The 360° Method | AWARE / ACT / ADVANCE + seasonal cadence cards + living health record visual + disclaimer |
| `#project-path` | Project Path (Track A) | Specific-project buyer. 4-step process. CTA → baseline.html |
| `#proactive-path` | Proactive Path (Track B) | Full program buyer. Tier preview cards. CTA → baseline.html |
| `#about` | About | Locked H1 / subhead / values section / service area |
| `#faq` | FAQ | 10-question accordion. Buyer-voiced questions. JS toggle. |
| (bottom) | Final CTA | "The First Conversation Costs Nothing." → baseline.html |

### Destination pages (separate files)
| File | Purpose | Reached from |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline.html` | Booking form — complimentary Baseline Walkthrough | Every CTA on index.html + nav button |
| `thank-you.html` | Post-submit confirmation + Founding Member OTO | baseline.html form submit (GET action) |
| `enroll.html` | Tier selection — Essential / Silver / Gold | thank-you.html OTO button + proactive path tier cards |

---

## What Each Page Demonstrates

### `index.html`
- Single-scroll funnel: Recognition → Identification → Gap → Guide → System → Invitation (brand doc §7)
- Locked hero copy verbatim (brand doc §0 / rebrand plan §0.11)
- Dual-path architecture — Project Path and Proactive Path visible from hero scroll position
- 360° Method full explanation inline (replaces what would have been a separate method.html)
- Seasonal cadence: four cards (Spring / Summer / Fall / Winter) with specific PNW tasks
- Living health record: visual mock-up with NOW/SOON/WAIT example entries
- About section inline with all three locked copy elements (H1 / subhead / values heading)
- FAQ accordion — 10 questions, reframed in buyer voice, no forbidden vocab
- Transformation before/after visual (reactive cycle vs proactive stewardship)

### `baseline.html`
- Booking form: name, email, phone, property address, path selection, timing preference
- ICP filter question (optional): "How would you describe your current approach to home care?"
- Privacy note
- Sidebar: what the walkthrough includes, what it is NOT (disclaimer), service area, quote
- Form submits to `thank-you.html` via GET (mock — no backend)

### `thank-you.html`
- Confirmation header with check icon
- 4-step "What happens next" sequence
- **Founding Member OTO** — the ask before the walkthrough. Rate-lock framing (not a discount — a commitment honored in kind). Two CTAs: "Yes, secure standing → enroll.html" and "Continue to Walkthrough Only → index.html"
- "Preparing for your walkthrough" practical notes (no prep required, access tips, time estimate)

### `enroll.html`
- "Shared foundation" strip showing what every tier includes (4× visits, health record, roadmap, walkthrough)
- Three tier cards: Essential / Silver / Gold — transformation statement + inclusions + best-for description
- No prices anywhere — "Investment available on request" on every card
- Comparison table (feature × tier grid)
- Founding Member reminder band → links back to thank-you.html OTO

---

## Copy Status — Locked vs Draft

### LOCKED (verbatim — do not redraft)
- Hero H1: "Your Home Deserves the Same Standard of Care You Bring to Everything Else."
- Hero subhead: "Every season. Every system. Every detail — taken care of."
- Hero eyebrow: "For homeowners who hold their asset to a higher standard."
- 360° Method subhead (used inline): "The Standard of Care Your Home Has Always Deserved."
- About H1: "The Standard Doesn't Change. Regardless of Who's on the Job."
- About subhead: "Owner-founded. Crew-executed. Accountable at every level."
- About values heading: "What You Can Expect, Every Time"
- CTA headline: "The First Conversation Costs Nothing."
- Pull quote (brand doc §8): "The homeowners who sleep well at night..."

### DRAFT — review and edit freely
- Transformation section body copy
- 360° Method phase descriptions (framework is locked; prose is draft)
- Seasonal cadence task lists (specific tasks — confirm against Marcin's actual seasonal checklist)
- Project Path step-by-step descriptions
- Proactive Path selling copy
- All FAQ answers (10 questions — voice is right, answers need Marcin validation)
- Founding Member OTO copy (rate-lock framing is brand-correct; exact offer terms TBD)
- Tier card inclusions (framework matches rebrand plan §4f — prose is draft)
- Comparison table rows (confirm exact Silver in-scope task list)
- About story section body copy
- Footer contact details (phone number is placeholder: (360) 637-6000)

---

## Open Questions for Marcin — Review These First

1. **Phone number.** Used `(360) 637-6000` as a placeholder throughout. Replace with actual number before any external share.

2. **Silver tier task list.** The comparison table and Silver card list specific in-scope seasonal tasks (gutter care, HVAC filter, deck surface care, exterior washing, weatherproofing/caulking). Confirm this matches your actual service delivery scope — any mismatch here creates expectation problems.

3. **Founding Member OTO mechanics.** The thank-you page presents the rate-lock offer. Two questions: (a) Is the rate-lock permanent as long as membership stays active — or does it lock for a defined period? (b) Does founding member status survive a tier upgrade (e.g., Essential → Silver)? These are framing decisions that affect the OTO copy and the enrollment terms.

4. **Baseline Walkthrough duration.** The thank-you page says "approximately 90 minutes to two hours." Is that accurate for a typical Clark County home? Adjust if needed — buyers will hold you to what's stated here.

5. **Essential tier — execution included or not?** The rebrand plan §4f says Essential includes seasonal cadence visits "assessment and documentation only; execution quoted separately via Track A." The mock reflects this. Confirm this is still the model — it affects whether Essential members feel the visits are passive (they observe) vs active (they fix). A sentence on the Essential card may need to be crisper.

6. **Annual asset valuation review (Gold).** Listed as a Gold inclusion. The rebrand plan flagged this as an open question (§4d Q5): internal model, partner RE agent, or formal appraisal? Don't present this Gold feature to real prospects until the delivery model is confirmed.

7. **"Most Selected" label on Silver card.** Currently displays as a design element. This needs to be true before it's live — or change to "Recommended" which makes no empirical claim.

8. **FAQ answer — membership investment.** Answer says "we discuss it after the Baseline Walkthrough." Confirm this is the actual sales flow. If you're showing pricing on a call before the walkthrough, the FAQ answer needs to shift.

9. **Unsplash image licensing.** All images are linked via direct Unsplash URLs with auto-format params. These are fine for internal review/mock. For live production, download and self-host the selected images, or purchase licenses through Unsplash+ or a stock library.

10. **Disclaimer language.** The assessment disclaimer appears in the footer of every page and inline on the Method section and baseline form sidebar. Recommend attorney review of the final disclaimer text before the site goes live — particularly around "latent defect issues" and the WA contractor liability framing.

---

## Top 3 Things to Critique First

**1. The Founding Member OTO on thank-you.html.**
This is the highest-leverage conversion moment in the mock — it's the difference between a booked walkthrough and a booked walkthrough with a commitment. Read it fresh. Does the rate-lock framing feel compelling without feeling like a tactic? Does the two-CTA choice ("Yes, secure standing" vs "Continue to walkthrough only") feel like the right fork? This is where the brand voice is under the most pressure.

**2. The FAQ accordion on index.html.**
Ten questions, all reframed from the buyer's voice. Read each question and answer as the ICP — a Clark County business owner or physician who has been burned by contractors before. Does the language feel like it was written for them specifically? Is anything hedging where it should be confident? Is anything missing that they would definitely ask?

**3. The Essential tier card on enroll.html.**
Essential is the lowest tier but it's likely the most common trial entry. The card currently frames it as "assessment only — execution quoted separately." That's accurate to the rebrand plan, but it may feel thin compared to Silver. Read the Essential card and ask: does a high-performer read this and feel the tier is worth a relationship, or does it feel like a diagnostic-only service? If it feels thin, we should add one more tangible deliverable or reframe the execution model.

---

## Technical Notes

- All pages load cleanly from `file://` with no build step required
- Tailwind CSS loaded via CDN Play (no configuration required)
- Google Fonts loaded via CDN (Playfair Display + Manrope)
- Images are direct Unsplash CDN URLs — no downloads required for mock review
- FAQ accordion is vanilla JS (no dependencies)
- All internal links tested: anchor links on index.html, cross-page links to baseline/thank-you/enroll all point correctly
- Form on baseline.html uses GET method → thank-you.html (mock only — no backend)
- Mobile nav implemented on all pages (hamburger toggle, closes on link click on index.html)
- No console errors expected — all assets load from CDN

---

*Built overnight per Marcin's directive, April 2026. All copy passes brand doc §10 checklist. Zero forbidden-vocab hits confirmed by grep sweep.*
docs.title: Handy Pioneers — Website Mock v1

# Yates — Kitchen Remodel + Painting

First Handy Pioneers project run through the interim **Notion** delivery setup
(before the HP Estimator app carries it). Matthew Yates is the first founding
**Full Coverage** member; this brief is the repo-side record. Live status lives in
Notion.

## Client
- **Matthew Yates** · matthewdyates@gmail.com · 503-888-7907
- **Property:** 1523 NE 141st Ave, Vancouver, WA 98684 — duplex, vacant unit, built 1974
- **Member:** Full Coverage ($99/mo), enrolled 2026-06-03. Discounts: 2.5% member + 3% cash/check.

## The one job (combined scope)
- **Job #: 2702** — the booked job (on the jobs calendar; operational identifier).
- **Estimate #: 2690** — the approved estimate this job is built from (Approved; services
  subtotal $29,110, total $29,957.25). **Signed 2026-06-03** by Matthew. Signed PDF lives in
  Google Drive: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mqCfmzU3qxppY6JRrj_xB1qZA0C0jGWn/view
  (also in the help@ email "Copy of signed estimate #2690"). Client docs folder (Drive):
  https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zgw1pDgrGWLQQ1h_iTKUCylkKvWdDK9s
- Estimate # and Job # are **separate sequences** and won't always align — change orders /
  revisions attach to the job as their own estimate rows. See the estimate-vs-job model in the
  2026-06-04 decision log.
- *`HP-PRJ-001` is this brief's OS doc id only, not a job or estimate number.*

Kitchen remodel + flooring + whole-unit painting (+ asbestos/ceiling). Estimate #2690 totals:
services subtotal **$29,110**; less 5.5% discount (3% cash + 2.5% Full Coverage) −$1,601.05;
plus Vancouver tax $2,448.30 = **total $29,957.25**. **Deposit $11,644** collected Sat **Jun 6**
(the "07/03" on the estimate was an HCP default placeholder); job starts Mon **Jun 8**. Re-check
margin at the $29,110 subtotal; the **$300 labor bank is not applied** to this job.

**Ownership split:**
- **HP:** demo + haul (starts **Mon Jun 8**), subfloor, cabinets + hardware, **LVP flooring
  (kitchen/entry/hall/laundry) + baseboards (after cabinets)**, painting, punch list. Quartz via
  HP fabricator.
- **Client's own subs:** **carpet** (bedrooms + living room; fixed install **Jun 26**) and
  appliances (client ordered; his installer) — not HP's scope.

**Materials supply split (2026-06-06):** HP buys all finished materials via Home Depot
delivery — cabinets, pulls, toe kick/fillers, LVP flooring package. The sub supplies his
own consumables and all subfloor-repair materials (plywood, screws, adhesive, leveler).
Order list: `yates-hd-order-list.md` (HP-DOC-004) in this folder. Supersedes the earlier
"Sub A buys everything" note on the Notion re-measure page.
**Ordered 2026-06-06:** cabinets + toe kick + pulls (HD order WN60745011, $2,074.06,
delivery Wed Jun 10) and 10 cases Sterling Oak LVP (WN60745502, $539.70, est. Jun 8).
Both tax exempt. Sub picks up the rest: stair nose, T-mold, quarter round, filler strips
(plus his consumables + subfloor materials).

**Scheduling rule:** no crew work on weekends unless a special circumstance (e.g., the
client-requested Saturday deposit). Applies to this schedule and HP scheduling generally.

**Ceiling:** flat ceiling-white paint only — **no scrape/skim/re-texture** (client did not agree
to a skim). Painted with the rest of the unit.

**Asbestos test:** not being done (we're painting the ceiling, not scraping it). It was **never in
the signed bid (#2690)** — an earlier draft listed it, but the signed $29,110 subtotal did not — so
there's **no credit and no revised estimate**; contract value stays **$29,110** (total $29,957.25).
Electrical panel + GFCI still deferred (may move to the membership roadmap).

**Key dates:** deposit Sat **Jun 6** · HP demo Mon **Jun 8** · client carpet **Jun 26** ·
target wrap ~**Jun 30**.

## Where it lives (Notion)
- **Customer hub:** https://app.notion.com/p/3748807401c2817a9af7dd6316ff32c3
- **Project page:** https://app.notion.com/p/3748807401c281f1a65af78937a797b4 (titled
  "2702 · Kitchen Remodel + Painting")
- **Materials & Selections — Job 2702** (client sign-off; visual gallery, **no prices**):
  https://app.notion.com/p/fefdee5ffb684c18a440d24cb21f7fc7 — selections pulled from the
  client-facing estimate; client confirms by reply. Open picks: **faucet** (and who supplies —
  estimate says HP, re-measure notes say TBD/customer), **LVP color**, **quartz slab**. Add product
  photos to the cards. Reusable per job by duplicating.

**Comms split:** the **project** (schedule + material selections) and the **membership** (Full
Coverage cadence + a member portal) go to Matthew in **separate threads/portals**. The welcome
email (2026-06-04) is **project-only** (schedule + selections links); the membership will be sent
as its own thread/portal later. Don't bundle them.
- **Handy Pioneers — Jobs** (business-wide jobs calendar, one row per job):
  https://app.notion.com/p/f1f0a5d316764576ab4dd14dfd02d063 — this job is **Job # 2702**
  (Customer → Matthew, so it also shows under his profile's **Jobs** relation; the row's
  Schedule link opens the task-level Project Schedule). High-level layer; project jobs only.
  The job's **Contract value** auto-sums the Approved estimates (a rollup over the Estimates
  relation), so change orders update it automatically.
- **Handy Pioneers — Estimates** (all estimates / change orders / revisions across customers):
  https://app.notion.com/p/de4fde9e8ab9477882b592a66c6a4635 — this job's estimate is **#2690**
  (Approved, $29,410), related to Job 2702 and to Matthew (shows under his profile's **Estimates**
  relation). Change orders attach here as new rows on the same job.
- **Project Schedule DB** (shareable to client + trades; Timeline / By-Owner / Table):
  https://app.notion.com/p/8cece39ad2a146cea18eccf9f0435628
- **Membership Cadence DB** (Full Coverage; Calendar / Roadmap board / Included-each-visit / Table):
  https://app.notion.com/p/6722def74f7042e18789ac57dd684da2

  **Scope rule:** seasonal visits include ONLY the listed maintenance tasks (Coverage =
  Included) — the current **PNW regional set**: universal US home maintenance (detectors,
  HVAC filter, gutters, plumbing leak check, dryer vent, winterize faucets, water-heater
  visual) + PNW-specific (moss, downspout/drainage flow, crawlspace/attic moisture,
  north-siding mildew, deck/fence seal, pest check). Light recurring tasks are included;
  heavy remediation/repair and every roadmap NOW/SOON/WAIT line is a project: estimate →
  approve → member discount applies. A `Coverage` property tags every row; the "Included
  each visit" view lists the covered set. **Interim:** region-based seasonal task sets to
  be established later.

- **Seasonal Visit Checklist DB** (field worksheet engine):
  https://app.notion.com/p/4e0014e42cef41489aeea04b9b2bb3b3

  Each seasonal visit page opens to its own worksheet (a filtered checklist view): a quick
  **spot inspection** of all 9 systems (each carrying a baseline note so the tech re-checks
  the right things) + the **16 included seasonal tasks**. Per item: **Done** checkbox,
  **Result** (Done / Not done / N/A), **Why not / notes**, and **Photos**. Seeded for all
  four visits (Summer 2026 → Spring 2027), 25 items each. Reuse = duplicate a visit's 25
  rows with a new Visit value (or duplicate the DB for a new customer).

  **Consistent cadence — inside → outside.** A `Step` number (1–25) orders every visit the
  same way and the checklist sorts by it: Steps 1–9 = spot inspection (Interior Finishes →
  Electrical → HVAC → Plumbing → Appliances → Foundation/crawlspace → Exterior Envelope →
  Roof & Gutters → Drainage); Steps 10–25 = seasonal tasks, interior first (detectors, HVAC
  filter, plumbing, water heater, dryer vent, crawlspace, pest) then outward to the envelope,
  deck/fence, faucets, and finally roof → gutters → drainage. The Scope of Work list mirrors
  this order.

  Each of the 16 seasonal-task rows has a **"How we do it"** step list (3–4 bullets) in its
  body, so the tech, client, and HP all see the method when the task is opened. Each seasonal
  visit also carries a **Technician** field (set to **Marcin**) so the client knows who's
  coming out and the visit is assigned to one person.

  Each seasonal page also carries a **Scope of Work** block: the 16 included tasks as
  plain bullets (task names only — no descriptions). **No time is shown to the client:**
  externally HP prices by project/task, never by the hour.

  **Internal-only** time-on-site guideline (for scheduling; not on any client-facing page):
  ~1.5 hr minimum · small home ~2 hr · average ~2.5 hr · large ~3 hr · largest homes ~3.5 hr.

**Client-facing rule:** material shared with clients/trades must not reveal that HP uses
subcontractors. HP-managed work shows as **HP** (the trade is in the task name); the client's
own people are "installers," not "subs." Internal repo docs may still say sub.
- **Workstream row:** https://app.notion.com/p/3758807401c281639923cab9830e81b0

## To reuse for the next client
Duplicate the two databases (Project Schedule, Membership Cadence) into the new
customer's hub and reseed. The structure is the reusable asset; there is no separate
empty template to maintain.

## Open / Marcin's hands
- Publish each Notion page (Share → Publish, editing off); send the schedule link to the
  trades + Matthew, the cadence link to Matthew.
- Confirm/adjust the placeholder HP dates once subs lock in.
- Resolve the electrical decision (in this job vs. deferred to the roadmap).
- **Demo change orders (2026-06-09)** — subfloor full replacement +$2,100 (sub
  cost $2,100 confirmed via Hook & Hammer Quote #47-adjacent; ready to send);
  electrical (range pigtail + microwave whip) **on hold** pending electrician;
  plumber water stops $150 absorbed. Detail + margins in `yates-job-pnl.md`.

---
id: HP-DOC-005
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: Yates Job 2702 — Profitability Tracker (P&L)
status: final
created: 2026-06-07
updated: 2026-06-07
---

# Job P&L — Yates 2702 — INTERNAL

**Live tracker (update costs there):** Notion "Job P&L — 2702" under the project
page: https://app.notion.com/p/3788807401c28142bc27d307b4b5a546
One row per cost line in the Cost Ledger DB; Status moves Estimate → Committed →
Actual → Paid. Never show any of this to Matthew or any trade.

**Auto-calculated (2026-06-07):** a "P&L Summary" DB on the same page holds the
Job 2702 row (Net Revenue $27,508.95) related to every Cost Ledger row. A rollup
sums Cost; formulas compute Profit and Margin %. Editing any ledger number
updates the P&L instantly — no spreadsheet, no manual math. Only rule: new cost
rows must have their `Job` relation set to "Job 2702". Decision: no Google Sheet
interim; Notion is the single surface until the Estimator app absorbs job
costing. The snapshot below is static reference; live numbers live in Notion.

## Revenue (fixed)

- Services subtotal $29,110.00, less 5.5% discounts −$1,601.05
- **Net revenue to HP: $27,508.95** (tax pass-through; customer total $29,957.25)
- Deposit collected $11,644.00 (Jun 6)

## Snapshot 2026-06-07 (final structure)

Two corrections from Marcin: (1) Sub A's quote carried materials at the
20%-margin customer price from the re-measure table (kitchen ~$3,151, flooring
~$1,175; hard ~$2,521 / ~$940) — HP self-supplied, so those prices come off and
HP keeps the full 30% materials margin. (2) **Sub A's $10,700 was ONE bid that
already included the $1,920 flooring** — not a separate line.

| Line | Supplier | Status | Cost |
|------|----------|--------|------|
| HD cabinets + toe kick + pulls | Home Depot WN60745011 | Actual | $2,074.06 |
| HD LVP flooring 10 cases | Home Depot WN60745502 | Actual | $539.70 |
| Paint (HP buys) | Sherwin-Williams | TBD | ~$800 placeholder |
| Sub A — kitchen + flooring install ($10,700 − $4,326 materials@20%) | Hook and Hammer | Estimate, confirm | $6,374 |
| Subfloor repair | Hook and Hammer | Estimate | $650 |
| Quartz (sink incl.) | Albrus Construction #1112 | Committed | $2,330.00 |
| Plumbing (matte black faucet incl.) | Alpine Plumbing Est1063 | Committed | $900.00 |
| Painting labor ($4,225 − ~$800 paint) | Painting sub | Estimate, confirm | $3,425 |
| **Total** | | | **$17,092.76** |

**Profit $10,416 → margin 37.9%. Floor (30% = $19,256 max cost) cleared with
$2,163 of headroom. IDS closed. Confirm $6,374 with Zoe in writing; painting
labor and SW paint receipt still to true-up.**

## Supply split (current, decided 2026-06-07)

- **HP bought:** cabinets, toe kick, pulls, LVP planks. **HP also buys: paint.**
- **Sub A buys everything else:** trim (stair nose, T-mold, quarter round,
  fillers), consumables, subfloor materials — and anything not on HP's receipts.
- Trades supply their own (quartz incl. sink; plumber incl. faucet).

## Status: margin locked (2026-06-06)

Zoe verbally confirmed both numbers on 2026-06-06: **$6,374** kitchen + flooring
and **$4,225** painting (prep + two coats on ceilings, walls, trim — paint
included, so HP does not buy paint; that ledger row is $0). With Alpine at $900
and quartz at $2,330, every line is Actual or Committed.

**Costs $17,092.76 → profit $10,416 → 37.9% margin.** Floor cleared by $2,163.

Remaining housekeeping:
1. ~~Get Zoe's verbal in writing~~ **DONE 2026-06-09** — Hook and Hammer Quote #47
   ($10,599, signed) puts the kitchen + flooring + trim + paint labor in writing.
2. True up lines as final invoices land.

## Snapshot 2026-06-09 — Quote #47 in writing + subfloor confirmed

Hook and Hammer **Quote #47 ($10,599, signed 2026-06-09)** = kitchen + flooring
install + trim + paint labor, in writing — the $6,374 + $4,225 Zoe had confirmed
verbally (paint material is in the sub's quote, so HP's separate paint row stays
$0). Subfloor is **not** in #47; it's the separate **$2,100** full-replacement
quote (CO below). Two cost lines move vs. the 06-07 snapshot: subfloor $650 →
$2,100, plumbing $900 → $1,050 (water stops absorbed).

| Line | Supplier | Cost |
|------|----------|------|
| HD cabinets + toe kick + pulls | Home Depot WN60745011 | $2,074.06 |
| HD LVP flooring 10 cases | Home Depot WN60745502 | $539.70 |
| Kitchen + flooring + trim + paint labor (Quote #47) | Hook and Hammer | $10,599.00 |
| Subfloor full replacement (demo old + new) | Hook and Hammer | $2,100.00 |
| Quartz (sink incl.) | Albrus #1112 | $2,330.00 |
| Plumbing (faucet incl.) + water stops $150 absorbed | Alpine Est1063 | $1,050.00 |
| **Total** | | **$18,692.76** |

Revenue with the subfloor CO: $27,508.95 + $1,932.00 net = **$29,440.95**.
**Profit $10,748.19 → 36.5% margin.** Dollars up ~$332 (the CO adds revenue), %
down ~1.4 pts (thin subfloor line + the absorbed $150). Floor cleared wide.
Electrical CO not included (on hold).

## Pending change orders

**Bathroom exhaust fan (2026-06-06)** — mold found in the bathroom corner. Roadmap
quote $700–$1,000; estimate row in the HP Estimates DB (Draft, $850 mid,
Change order, linked to Job 2702 + Matthew). Not in the P&L until approved.
On approval: +$850 revenue (≈$803 net of discounts) and a cost row (fan +
install, est. $300–$400) linked to Job 2702. Sequence the mold cleanup before
the painters reach that bathroom (bath is paint-only in the signed scope).

## Demo discoveries — 2026-06-09

Three items surfaced once the kitchen + laundry were opened up.

### CO — Subfloor: patch → full replacement (kitchen + laundry) [READY]
Two layers of vinyl over old particleboard; a patch won't hold, so the whole
kitchen + laundry subfloor gets replaced. Signed scope carried a $1,000 subfloor
patch (cost $650). Full replacement reprices the subfloor to **$3,100 to the
client** — a **+$2,100 change order** on top of the $1,000 already in the contract.
- Client side: +$2,100, less **8%** (5% member out-of-scope rate, $1k–$5k tier +
  3% cash/check) = **+$1,932.00 net**, plus Vancouver sales tax.
- **Sub cost (confirmed):** Hook and Hammer charges **$2,100** to demo/remove the
  old subfloor + vinyl layer and install new subfloor (materials + labor).
  Replaces the $650 patch line.
- **Margin read:** the subfloor line on its own is thin — $2,877.00 net subfloor
  revenue ($945 original + $1,932.00 CO) vs $2,100 cost = **27.0% net**, under 30%
  in isolation. But it's a change order on the active project, and the **whole job
  stays at 36.5%**. We're passing the sub's full-replacement cost through at a fair
  member markup, not squeezing the line to hit 30% alone. Keep at +$2,100.

> **Member rate on out-of-scope work (tiered, applies to all change orders):**
> under $1,000 → 8% · $1,000–$5,000 → 5% · over $5,000 → 2.5%. This is the
> *membership* discount; the 3% cash/check discount stacks on top when the client
> pays cash/check. (Belongs in the canonical membership pricing doc, not just here.)

### CO — Electrical: range pigtail + microwave whip [ON HOLD]
Range plug pigtail came off / not to code; the microwave wire in the wall is
dead-ended (connected to nothing). Electrician cost to HP ~**$300**. **Marcin is
confirming scope + price with the electrician before this CO is priced or sent.**
- Prior working number: $485 sticker → $458.33 net → 34.5% net margin (clears the
  30% project floor, not the 40% small-job floor). Revisit once the electrician
  confirms the real cost.

### Plumber water stops — ABSORBED, not a change order
Plumber adds **$150** to replace the washer + kitchen-sink water stops. Marcin
eats it: raises HP's plumbing cost line ($900 → $1,050); does **not** go to the
client. Job-level effect: profit $10,416 → ~$10,266, margin 37.9% → ~37.3% (still
well over the 30% floor). The two COs above add their own profit on top of this.

docs.body:
---
id: HP-DOC-006
type: DOC
area: Handy Pioneers
title: Yates Job 2702 — Money Flow (where each dollar lands)
status: final
created: 2026-06-08
updated: 2026-06-08
---

# Where the Yates money goes — INTERNAL

The reason the deposit "feels" like it's sitting in the wrong place: two different
questions are getting mixed together. Keep them separate and it's simple.

- **Cash question:** which bank account does the money physically sit in? (Profit
  First.)
- **Books question:** when does it count as income vs. money you still owe back?
  (QuickBooks, once it's live.)

Most of the Yates deposit is **not yours yet** in either view. That's correct, not
a problem.

## 1. Cash lens — Profit First (what to actually do at the bank)

All client money lands in the HP **Revenue** hub account. On the **10th and 25th**,
empty the hub in two moves:

**Step 1 — take the state's money off the top.** Move the *actual* WA sales tax
collected to the WA Sales Tax account. It is never a percentage; it's a pass-through.

- Yates customer total: **$29,957.25**
- Yates net to HP: **$27,508.95**
- Sales tax to set aside: **$2,448.30**

**Step 2 — split the pre-tax remainder ($27,508.95) by the HP percentages:**

| Account | % | Yates $ | What it covers |
|---|---|---|---|
| OPEX | 70% | $19,256 | Pays the subs + materials ($17,093 hard cost) — ~$2,163 overhead buffer left |
| Owner Comp | 15% | $4,126 | Marcin's pay — owner's draw to personal Opex |
| Owner Profit | 5% | $1,375 | Profit habit; 50% distributed quarterly |
| Marketing | 5% | $1,375 | Proactive Path acquisition |
| WA Sales Tax (B&O / income cushion) | 3% | $825 | On top of Step 1 |
| Liabilities | 2% | $550 | Warranty callbacks, annual bills |

The **70% OPEX bucket is the pass-through that pays the subs** — that's the whole
reason it's sized so high. It is not "profit you get to keep."

### The deposit timing (the part that's tripping you up)
You've only collected the **$11,644 deposit** so far; the rest comes at
completion. **Only allocate cash that's actually in the hub on each date.** Do the
split on the $11,644 now, then again on the balance when it lands.

Why this is safe: subs are paid **Net-7 after the client pays** (P5 SOP), so the
hub always holds the client's money before a sub invoice is due. You never front
sub money out of pocket.

The one rule that keeps you out of trouble: **never skim Owner Comp or Owner
Profit off a deposit as if the job's done.** The deposit is mostly OPEX
(sub/material money you're holding). If you pull "your" cut early, you'll be short
when the subs invoice. Take the full Owner Comp/Profit cut only once the job's
final payment is in and the job's actual costs are settled.

## 2. Books lens — QuickBooks (why it's not all "revenue" yet)

This is the "liability book" you were reaching for. In proper books a customer
deposit is **not income until you've done the work**. So in QuickBooks:

1. The **$11,644 deposit books to a liability account** called *Customer Deposits
   (Unearned Revenue)* — money you'd owe back if the job stopped. Not revenue.
2. **Costs hit the job as they're incurred** (subs, materials → COGS, tagged to
   Job 2702 via QB Projects).
3. **At job completion**, the deposit + final payment move *out* of the liability
   account and *into* Revenue all at once, and the job's P&L closes ($27,508.95
   revenue − $17,092.76 cost = **$10,416 profit, 37.9% margin**).

So today, accrual-correctly, most of the Yates money is still a **liability**, not
revenue. Once QB is set up (Track C of the finance plan) it'll show exactly that,
and the per-job P&L replaces the interim Notion Cost Ledger.

## TL;DR
- The deposit isn't "extra cash" — it's mostly sub/material money you're holding.
- At the bank: allocate only what's collected, sales tax off the top, then the
  6-way split; pay subs Net-7 after the client pays.
- In the books: it's a Customer Deposits **liability** until the kitchen's done,
  then it becomes revenue and the $10,416 profit lands.
- This pattern is the standard for every HP project — written into the Profit
  First doc (LIFE-DOC-002) so it's reusable, not a one-off.
docs.title: Where the Yates money goes — INTERNAL

docs.body:
# HANDY PIONEERS — PROJECT CONTEXT

## Context contract (per `../../references/icm-methodology.md`)
- **Role:** Integrator / Chief of Staff for Handy Pioneers. Autonomous execution, no preamble.
- **Jobs:** estimates + the Estimator app, Proactive Path enrollment, sub coordination, scorecard/IDS, margin enforcement.
- **Pull (L3):** `Pioneers-Compass/HP-Context.md` (canonical), `../../references/voice.md`, the apex/faith lens + privacy rule (`../../CLAUDE.md`), the non-negotiable margin rules below.
- **Working material (L4):** the live job/estimate, the customer's walkthrough findings, current STATUS + scorecards in `Pioneers-Compass/`.
- **Output:** client-facing copy in warm-but-efficient voice (never expose cost/markup/margin math); internal reports as Done / In progress / Blocked, max 5 bullets.

## NORTH STAR (apex link)
Vision/values canon: `Pioneers-Compass/HP-BOS-Filled.md` Step 2 (5 values, purpose, 2029 vision, BHAG). Portfolio map: `../../context/north-star.md`. HP points back to the apex as honest stewardship of the homes families shelter in. Roots of the 5 BOS values (internal only; the BOS "what it looks like" lines are the public-ready layer, already plain):
- Show Up Ready = diligence; respect for the person served
- Own It = honesty; repairing what you got wrong
- Straight Talk = truthfulness; let your yes mean yes
- Quality That Lasts = excellence as work offered to God
- Earn the Referral = witness through conduct, not words

## OPERATING SYSTEM
Read this file at the start of every task:
`Pioneers-Compass/HP-Context.md`

This file contains the full operating context: business identity, Proactive Path pricing, locked rock sequence, current bottlenecks, financial rules, core values, brand standards, 360° Method quick reference, org structure, and meeting pulse.

## DOCUMENT LIBRARY
- Full BOS: `Pioneers-Compass/HP-BOS-Filled.md`
- SOP Library Index: `Pioneers-Compass/SOPs/SOP-Index.md`
- Weekly Scorecard: `Pioneers-Compass/Scorecards/HP-Weekly-Scorecard.md`
- IDS Issues Log: `Pioneers-Compass/Scorecards/IDS-Issues-Log.md`
- Independence Plan: `Pioneers-Compass/HP-Independence-Plan.md`
- Visual Reference: `Pioneers-Compass/BOS-Visual-Reference.html`

## NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES (enforced on every task)
- Minimum gross margin: 30% standard / 40% small job (hard cost <$2,000)
- Internal labor: $100/hr cost / $150/hr billed — NEVER expose to clients
- Never expose: hard costs, markup multipliers, cost math to any client
- IDS trigger: any job below margin floor or estimate variance >15% — flag same day
- Sub #1 (Carpenter) at $100/hr = 33% GM → fails 40% small job floor — do not use on small jobs

## CURRENT STATUS (as of 2026-05-29)
- BOS Phase: PHASE 1 — BASECAMP, Step 4 (Write the Playbook) — COMPLETE
- All 9 Step SOPs + 14 Supporting Processes: written, DRAFT status
- Scorecard, IDS Log, Independence Plan: built
- Rock 1: HP Estimator app (90% done) + S1/S4/S5 SOPs written — Due Jun 15
- Rock 2: Enroll first 3–5 Proactive Path founding members — Due Jun 15
- Zero Proactive Path clients enrolled — no recurring revenue yet

## MEMBERSHIP — the Proactive Path (360° Membership)
HP's recurring revenue model. **Handy Pioneers delivers the 360° Method framework**
to Proactive Path members (the 360° Method is the IP HP teaches — its own brand
lives in `../360 Method/`). Enrolling the first founding members is 90-day Priority
#2. Stack: `handypioneers.com/roadmap-generator` (funnel, manus site) +
HP Estimator backend (`pro.handypioneers.com`) + Stripe. The old
`360.handypioneers.com` funnel is discontinued (301 to the new funnel,
2026-06-06).
Launch checklist: `01_Operations/proactive-path-launch.md`. Pricing/tiers:
`Pioneers-Compass/HP-Context.md` (canonical).

## ROLE
Act as Integrator / Chief of Staff. Autonomous execution. No preamble, no pleasantries. Report: Done / In progress / Blocked. Max 5 bullets per update.
docs.title: HP Identity (Context Contract)

docs.body:
# THE PIONEERS COMPASS
## Handy Pioneers Business Operating System

*Adapted from Business Operating System v2.0*
*Integrated with the 360° Method and EOS Operating Plan*
*Clark County, WA — handypioneers.com — Version 2.0 — May 2026*

---

## THE SINGLE ORGANIZING PRINCIPLE

> Every role, every process, every metric, every Rock at Handy Pioneers connects to one purpose:
> **Deliver the 360° Method — 3 phases, 9 steps — to Clark County homeowners who treat their property as the asset it is.**

The Proactive Path recurring client model is the primary business.
Project work is the entry point that earns the conversion.

---

## THE THREE PHASES AT A GLANCE

```
PHASE 1: BASECAMP          PHASE 2: THE ASCENT         PHASE 3: THE SUMMIT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Audit & Structure            Document & Operationalize    Measure & Sustain

Step 1: Survey the           Step 4: Write the            Step 7: Track the Numbers
        Territory                    Playbook
Step 2: Set True North       Step 5: Set Up               Step 8: Clear the
                                     Communications               Bottlenecks
Step 3: Map the Team         Step 6: Choose Your          Step 9: Step Back
                                     Tools
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Output: Business             Output: SOP Library          Output: Weekly Scorecard
        Assessment                   (9 Step SOPs +               Issues Log
        Vision Document              16 Supporting)               Independence Plan
        Accountability Chart
```

---

# PHASE 1 — BASECAMP
## *Audit & Structure*

---

## STEP 1: SURVEY THE TERRITORY
### Output: Business Assessment Report

### 1.1 — Business Profile

```
BUSINESS PROFILE — HANDY PIONEERS LLC
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Business Name:             Handy Pioneers LLC
Owner:                     Marcin Micek
What we deliver:           The 360° Method — systematic, documented,
                           relationship-based home stewardship.
                           3 phases. 9 steps. One relationship.
Founded:                   [Year]
License:                   WA Lic. HANDYP★761NH  |  $1M Insured
Phone:                     (360) 838-6731
Website:                   handypioneers.com
Where we operate:          Clark County, WA — Vancouver, Camas, Battle Ground,
                           Washougal, Ridgefield, La Center.
                           Territory depth over breadth — no spread thin.
What clients pay us for:   A management system for their most valuable asset.
                           One relationship. No vendor coordination.
                           Protected, maintained, growing in value — every season.
Best client:               Asset-conscious homeowner, $500K–$2M+ Clark County home.
                           Already pays for recurring services (lawn, cleaning, pest).
                           Time-poor. Trust-deficient. Not price-shopping.
Current headcount:         Owner (Marcin) only — Lead Tech hire underway (Aug 1 target)
Annual Revenue:            $[Current]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

### 1.2 — Business Assessment

```
BUSINESS ASSESSMENT — HANDY PIONEERS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Area                              Rating (1–5)    Notes
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Revenue — is it steady?            2    Feast or famine. No recurring revenue yet.
Lead flow — do clients find us?    2    Word of mouth only. SEO/ads paused.
Sales — do inquiries become jobs?  1    Zero PP memberships sold. Rebrand not yet
                                        reaching the right client profile.
Delivery — is the work consistent? 3.5  Consistently good but person-dependent
                                        (Marcin + one contractor). Not systematized.
Quality — do we get callbacks?     4    No rework, no problem calls.
Client communication               4    Clients always informed. Strong.
Staff/crew reliability             2    One external contractor. No agreements,
                                        no performance system in place.
Financial clarity                  3    30% margin rule exists mentally. No job
                                        costing worksheet. No monthly P&L review.
Documented processes / SOPs        2.5  Framework built. Zero SOPs written.
Systems / technology               3    HP Estimator app 90% done — moving off
                                        Housecall Pro. Full platform once live.
Leadership structure               1    Solo. No delegation possible today.
Owner's time / bandwidth           1    Corporate sales job running parallel.
                                        20–30 hrs/week available for HP.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
AVERAGE SCORE: 2.4 / 5  |  Assessed: 2026-05-28
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TOP 3 BOTTLENECKS:
1. Zero active leads or revenue — marketing paused; rebrand not yet reaching
   the right client ($500K+ asset-conscious homeowner)
2. HP Estimator app not fully live — client portal, intake, scheduling, and
   estimates cannot run consistently until the final 10% is complete
3. Marcin covering all 6 functional seats on 20–30 hrs/week — nothing
   delegates until the app is live and first clients are enrolled
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

### 1.3 — Assessment Summary

```
BUSINESS ASSESSMENT SUMMARY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
What's working — don't touch it:
- Quality and client communication are strong (4s) — delivery is trusted
- The 360° Method is fully defined and differentiated — no competitor in
  Clark County offers systematic home stewardship at this level
- Margin rules and financial discipline are already established
- HP Estimator app nearly done — will replace Housecall Pro and become
  the operational platform for the entire business

What's broken or missing — must fix:
- Zero active leads or revenue — marketing paused during rebrand/build phase
- App not fully live — client portal, intake, scheduling all depend on it
- No SOPs written — the method runs on Marcin's memory, not documented process
- Wrong client base from old model — price shoppers, not asset-conscious owners

The single thing costing us the most right now:
  Marketing stopped while building the system. The 360° Method exists on
  paper but no one has seen the new offer yet. No leads = no clients =
  no revenue = no ability to hire. Finishing the app and getting first
  clients enrolled by Jun 15 is the move that unlocks everything else.

If nothing changes over the next 3 years:
  HP stays a side business generating project work at commodity rates.
  Marcin never escapes the price comparison trap. The 360° Method
  stays a document, not a delivered product. Corporate job stays primary.

If bottlenecks are resolved over the next 3 years:
  20+ Proactive Path clients generating $100K+ ARR. Lead Tech runs
  Phase 2. Marcin focused on Phase 1 assessments, Phase 3 advisory,
  and growth. HP Estimator app becomes a competitive moat.
  The corporate job becomes optional.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## STEP 2: SET TRUE NORTH
### Output: Vision Document

### 2.1 — Core Values

```
CORE VALUES — HANDY PIONEERS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Value 1: Show Up Ready
  What it looks like: On time. Clean truck. Full kit. Clients at this price
  point expect the professional they paid for — every visit, no exceptions.

Value 2: Own It
  What it looks like: If something's wrong, say so and fix it.
  No deflection, no excuses, no waiting to see if the client notices.

Value 3: Straight Talk
  What it looks like: Honest assessments. No upselling what a home doesn't
  need. No softening what it does. The roadmap reflects reality.

Value 4: Quality That Lasts
  What it looks like: Every repair done to last. No shortcuts that come back.
  The 1-year labor guarantee means we do it right the first time.

Value 5: Earn the Referral
  What it looks like: Every engagement is a marketing event.
  We perform like the next client is watching — because they often are.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
These are filters — not slogans. Hire people who have them.
Let go of people who repeatedly violate them, even strong performers.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

### 2.2 — Core Focus

```
PURPOSE — WHY WE EXIST:
To give Clark County homeowners a management system that makes their most
valuable asset perform the way it should: protected, maintained, and
growing in value.

NICHE — WHAT WE DO:
We deliver the 360° Method — systematic, documented, relationship-based
home stewardship — to asset-conscious homeowners in Clark County, WA.
```

---

### 2.3 — Long-Range Vision Statement (3–5 Years)

```
LONG-RANGE VISION STATEMENT — 2029
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
It is 2029. Handy Pioneers manages 50+ Proactive Path clients across
Clark County, generating $250K–$350K in annual recurring revenue plus
$200K+ in project revenue at a 35%+ blended gross margin. The team is
Owner + Lead Technician + 2 Field Technicians + Office/Estimator. The
Lead Tech runs every Phase 2 seasonal visit without Marcin in the field.
I work in the business less than 20% of my time in field operations —
focused on Phase 1 assessments, Phase 3 advisory, and growth. Seasonal
visits are 100% on schedule — no missed visits, ever. We have 20+ clients
in active Phase 3 upgrade advisory. Our Google rating is 4.9★ with 60+
reviews. Five or more vetted specialists are on standing sub agreements.
When a Clark County homeowner thinks "someone who manages my home" —
they think Handy Pioneers.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

### 2.4 — 10-Year BHAG

```
The most trusted home stewardship company in Clark County —
managing 150+ Proactive Path clients, generating $1M+ in recurring
annual revenue, with a team that delivers the 360° Method without
the owner in the field.
```

---

### 2.5 — 1-Year Goals (2026)

```
1-YEAR GOALS — 2026
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Goal                                   Metric                 Owner   Due
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Enroll first 20 Proactive Path clients  20 signed + baselines  Marcin  Dec 31
First full seasonal cycle delivered     4 visits/client        Lead    Dec 31
All 9 Step SOPs documented             9 SOPs written + live   Marcin  Sep 30
Hire Lead Technician                   Running visits solo     Marcin  Aug 1
Sign 3+ sub agreements                 HVAC/plumbing/elec.     Marcin  Jul 31
Launch PP intake workflow + portal     Live; first enrollment  Office  Jun 30
Reach 50 Google reviews at 4.9★       50 verified reviews     Marcin  Dec 31
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Gross margin target:   30%+ standard / 40%+ small jobs — every job
Owner field time:      <20% by Q4 (requires Lead Tech hired by Aug 1)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

### 2.6 — 90-Day Rocks — Q3 2026

```
90-DAY ROCKS — Q3 2026  (locked 2026-05-28)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Rock 1: Finish HP Estimator app + write S1/S3/S5 SOPs embedded
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jun 15
  Success: App live, moved off Housecall Pro, three core SOPs
  running inside the platform.

Rock 2: Enroll first PP clients — warm outreach to previous clients
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jun 15
  Success: First paying Proactive Path founding members signed.
  Target: 3–5. Founding member rate acceptable if time-limited.

Rock 3: Organic marketing launch
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jul 31
  Success: Google profile optimized, referral program live,
  truck branded, content cadence started.

Rock 4: Paid marketing
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Aug 15+
  Success: Campaign live. Amplifying what organic already proved.
  Do not start until organic is working.

Rock 5: Lead Technician hire
  Owner: Marcin   Due: When PP volume creates a delivery problem
  Trigger: Not a date. Hire when client load makes it impossible
  for Marcin to deliver Phase 2 without help.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Sub agreements: After Lead Tech is hired. Route work through
confirmed relationships, not standing contracts on zero volume.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## STEP 3: MAP THE TEAM
### Output: Accountability Chart + GWC Assessment

**The rule:** The structure is permanent. The people filling it are not.
Build the chart around what the business requires — not just who currently exists.
Every seat must have a named owner. If it doesn't, that function has no accountability.

---

### 3.1 — Accountability Chart

```
HANDY PIONEERS — ACCOUNTABILITY CHART
Pioneers Compass Structure → HP Roles Mapped In
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

                        OWNER / CEO
                          Marcin
               Vision · V/TO · Culture · BHAG
          Phase 1 (AWARE) assessments — owner-led always
          Final accountability on every client relationship
                              │
                      MANAGER / COO
                         [VACANT]
              Day-to-day operations · Team accountability
               L10 meeting pulse · Rock tracking · IDS
                   360° delivery QA across all phases
                              │
    ┌─────────────────┬────────────────────┬──────────────────┐
    │                 │                    │                  │
MARKETING         SALES             OPERATIONS            FINANCE
    │                 │                    │                  │
[VACANT]          [Marcin            [VACANT —             [VACANT —
                  currently]          hire Lead             hire Office/
                                      Tech Aug 1]           Estimator]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

MARKETING DEPARTMENT                    [GAP — no seat filled]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Function:   Build brand, generate inbound PP inquiries, manage
            reputation. The 360° Method must be visible in
            Clark County — online and through referral.
Seat:       Marketing Lead — [VACANT / Marcin by default]
What this seat owns:
  - Google Business Profile (target: 50 reviews / 4.9★ by Dec 31)
  - Website content + SEO (handypioneers.com)
  - 360° Referral Program (SOP P10 — BUILD)
  - Review request workflow (SOP P11 — BUILD)
  - Social presence and local brand awareness
  - Email/nurture for PP prospects
HP gap:    No dedicated marketing function today. Marcin handles
           all of this reactively. Needs a process and a seat.

SALES DEPARTMENT                        [Marcin — wearing this hat]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Function:   Convert PP inquiries to signed agreements. Qualify
            Project Path clients for conversion. Close the
            Proactive Path enrollment.
Seat:       Sales Lead — Marcin (currently)
What this seat owns:
  - New PP inquiry response (within 2 business hrs)
  - Phase 1 consultation / baseline close
  - Proactive Path enrollment (SOP P1 — BUILD)
  - PP conversion rate target: >40%
  - Project Path to PP conversion
  - Pipeline tracking (CRM)
HP gap:    No formal pipeline tracking or CRM in use today.
           All sales is Marcin. No documented inquiry → close SOP.

OPERATIONS DEPARTMENT                   [VACANT — Lead Tech Aug 1]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Function:   Deliver the 360° Method. Phase 2 (ACT) and Phase 3
            (ADVANCE). Every seasonal visit, every roadmap item,
            every trade coordinated. Consistent. Documented. On time.
```

## STEP 4: WRITE THE PLAYBOOK
### Output: SOP Library (9 Step SOPs + 16 Supporting Processes)

See `SOPs/SOP-Index.md` for the full library with build order and file paths.

```
3. S5 — Schedule               Sets the seasonal calendar — the recurring promise.
```

**All 9 Step SOPs are now in DRAFT status** (written, not yet field-tested).
See SOPs/SOP-Index.md for full library, build order, and file paths.

### The Three Work Types — HP Context

```
TYPE 1 — PREDEFINED WORK (HP examples)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Four seasonal visits per client (standing protocol, PNW calendar)
- Weekly L10 meeting (Scorecard → Rocks → Headlines → IDS)
- Monthly financial review (margin audit, cash, ARR tracking)
- Portal updates after every visit (same-day, within 48 hrs)
- Weekly invoice processing

TYPE 2 — UNPLANNED WORK (HP examples)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Scope change discovered during a visit (hidden damage, new system failure)
- Sub no-show or quality callback
- Client complaint or PP cancellation risk
- Emergency repair request from PP client
- Margin warning on a job in progress
→ Run through Work Engine. Write SOP if it repeats.

TYPE 3 — DEFINING WORK (HP examples)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Writing the 9 Step SOPs and 16 supporting processes (THIS IS THE WORK)
- Building the client portal workflow
- Training the Lead Technician on the seasonal protocol
- Quarterly V/TO review and Rock setting
→ Protect time for this every week. All 9 SOPs are defining work.
  Without them, the method cannot be delivered at scale.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```
