# HP 90-DAY IMPLEMENTATION SEQUENCE — Q3 2026

```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
BUILD SEQUENCE — ORDERED BY DEPENDENCY
(From Section 9 of EOS Operating Plan)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

#1  Step 1 SOP — Baseline Assessment
    Phase 1 is the entry point for every PP client. Must exist before client #1.

#2  Step 3 SOP — Priority Roadmap
    The roadmap is the close. Consistent from day one.

#3  Step 5 SOP — Seasonal Visit Protocol
    The recurring promise. Needs a standing protocol before enrollment.

#4  Proactive Path Enrollment SOP + intake form
    Cannot enroll clients at scale without a defined onboarding path.

#5  Seat Descriptions — all 5 seats
    Hiring the Lead Tech requires a clear, written seat description.

#6  Step 4 SOP — Roadmap Execution + Margin Audit SOP
    Every roadmap item needs a scoped, margin-checked estimate before approval.

#7  Step 6 SOP — Trade Coordination + Sub Agreements
    Cannot run Phase 2 without a vetted, contracted sub roster.

#8  Invoice & Collections SOP
    Cash flow depends on a consistent billing process from job one.

#9  L10 Scorecard + weekly tracking
    Start the meeting pulse once the Lead Tech is hired.

#10 Step 2 SOP — Condition Log
    Refine after first 5 assessments — real data shapes the format.

#11 Step 9 SOP — Home Score & Documentation
    Build when first clients have 3+ visits on record.

#12 Steps 7–8 SOPs — Phase 3 framework
    Build when first clients approach Year 2 of the program.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
THE NON-NEGOTIABLE FIRST THREE:
Steps 1, 3, and 5 are the spine of the 360° Method delivery.
Before any other SOP is written, these three must exist and be in
active use. Everything else is downstream of these three.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

# APPENDIX — 360° METHOD QUICK REFERENCE

> The 360° Method is a proactive home maintenance framework.
> Handy Pioneers is the company that delivers it.
> Steps 2–6 are the recurring cycle — running 4x per year per client.

```
PHASE 1 — AWARE (Steps 1–3): Know the Home
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  Step 1: Baseline Assessment         [ONE-TIME — Owner-led always]
          Full walkthrough. 10 systems rated and photographed.
          Condition: Critical / Needs Attention / Monitor / Good
          PNW flags: moss, moisture, drainage, freeze vulnerability.
          Output: Foundational record. Locks before any future visit.

  Step 2: Inspect (Seasonal Log)      [RECURRING — 4x per year]
          What changed since the last visit?
          Measures against baseline — not a fresh start each time.
          Any condition change flagged for Step 4 (Prioritize).

  Step 3: Tracking (Client Portal)    [ONGOING — after every visit]
          Every visit, every finding, every project — logged same day.
          The portal is what clients see. No documentation = no value delivered.

PHASE 2 — ACT (Steps 4–6): Take Action  [The Recurring Cycle]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  Step 4: Prioritize                  [After baseline + after each S2 log]
          Holistic view: urgency + client budget + goals + seasonal timing
          + one-off requests. NOW / SOON / WAIT framework.
          Output: Agreed Priority Roadmap in client portal.
          Owner-led meeting. Client acknowledgment required in writing.

  Step 5: Schedule                    [After prioritization]
          Sets calendar with client: 4 seasonal dates annually
          + confirmed dates for all approved project work.
          No project scheduled without signed estimate.
          Sub availability confirmed before client date is locked.

  Step 6: Execute                     [Scope → Estimate → Approve → Execute → Document]
          Written scope before every job.
          Signed estimate + client approval before any work starts.
          Margin floors: 30% standard / 40% small job (<$2K hard cost).
          Same-day portal documentation. Margin audit before invoice.

PHASE 3 — ADVANCE (Steps 7–9): Build Value  [Activates at 12+ months]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  Step 7: Preservation Projects
          Proactive life-extension before failure. Triggered when systems
          enter preservation window. Always cheaper to preserve than replace.
          Framing: investment, not cost. Good / Better / Best options.

  Step 8: Strategic Upgrades
          ROI-driven. Aligned with client's goal: Sell / Refinance / Stay.
          Three-option structure (Core / Enhanced / Premium).
          Never more than 2 upgrades presented simultaneously.

  Step 9: Scale (Advance the Home)
          Home Score (0–100) across 10 systems — updated after every visit.
          Annual review at client anniversary: score history, next year plan,
          referral ask. Resale/refinance documentation package available.
```

---

*The Pioneers Compass — Handy Pioneers LLC*
*Business Operating System integrated with the 360° Method and EOS Operating Plan*
*Last updated: 2026-05-28 — Version 2.0*

docs.body:
# HANDY PIONEERS — AI OPERATING CONTEXT
## Pioneers Compass Framework — Session Context File

> **How to use:** Feed this file into any Claude session inside the Handy Pioneers project.
> Claude orients every task — estimating, content, SOPs, ops decisions — to this framework.
> Update each quarter as the business evolves.

---

## BUSINESS IDENTITY

```
Business Name:          Handy Pioneers LLC
Owner:                  Marcin Micek
What we deliver:        The 360° Method — 3 phases, 9 steps — systematic, documented,
                        relationship-based home stewardship for Clark County homeowners.
Founded:                [Year]
License:                WA Lic. HANDYP★761NH  |  $1M Insured  |  1-Year Labor Guarantee
Phone:                  (360) 838-6731
Website:                handypioneers.com
Where we operate:       Clark County, WA — Vancouver, Camas, Battle Ground,
                        Washougal, Ridgefield, La Center. Territory depth over breadth.
What clients pay for:   A management system for their most valuable asset — protected,
                        maintained, and growing in value. One relationship. Zero vendor
                        coordination. No surprises.
Best client:            Asset-conscious homeowners, $500K–$2M+ Clark County homes.
                        Already pays for recurring services (lawn, cleaning, pest).
                        Time-poor, trust-deficient, not price-shopping.
Primary business model: Proactive Path — recurring annual program.
                        Project Path — one-off work that earns the conversion.
```

---

## PROACTIVE PATH PRICING — 360° MEMBERSHIP (Canonical — matches live site + app)

```
All tiers include: annual 360° Home Scan, prioritized findings report w/ written scope,
member rates, HP direct line. Three billing cadences: monthly / quarterly / annual.

ESSENTIAL (DB: bronze) — "Protect the basics. Catch issues early."
  $59/mo  |  $169/quarter  |  $588/yr ($49/mo equivalent)
  2 seasonal visits: Spring + Fall
  Labor bank: $0
  Member discount: 5% (<$1k) / 3% ($1k–$5k) / 1.5% (>$5k)
  No priority scheduling

FULL COVERAGE (DB: silver) — "Four seasons of protection + pre-paid labor." [MOST POPULAR]
  $99/mo  |  $279/quarter  |  $948/yr ($79/mo equivalent)
  4 seasonal visits: all four seasons
  Labor bank: $300/yr
  Member discount: 8% (<$1k) / 5% ($1k–$5k) / 2.5% (>$5k)
  No priority scheduling
  Includes: everything in Essential + summer prep/weatherization, winter freeze/moisture
  visit, annual equity documentation report

MAXIMUM PROTECTION (DB: gold) — "The full system. Priority access. Maximum stewardship."
  $149/mo  |  $419/quarter  |  $1,428/yr ($119/mo equivalent)
  4 seasonal visits: all four seasons
  Labor bank: $600/yr
  Member discount: 12% (<$1k) / 8% ($1k–$5k) / 4% (>$5k)
  Priority scheduling: YES
  Includes: everything in Full Coverage + dedicated HP account manager,
  pre-negotiated vetted-tradesman rates, home equity maintenance log
```

> ARR target (2026): $20,000+ at 20 clients — mixed tier (40% Essential / 40% Full Coverage / 20% Max Protection)
> Monthly run rate at target: $1,860/mo. Annual billing equivalent: $18,000.

---

## CURRENT BOS PHASE & STEP

```
Active Phase:     PHASE 1 — BASECAMP
Active Step:      Step 4: Write the Playbook (SOP Library — in build)

Steps Complete:
  [X] Step 1: Survey the Territory — Business Assessment complete (avg 2.4/5)
  [X] Step 2: Set True North — Vision, BHAG, core values confirmed
  [X] Step 3: Map the Team — Accountability chart built, GWC complete, Role Cards written
              Sub roster assessed. Lead Tech = external hire, triggered by demand.

Next milestone:   Complete S1 / S3 / S5 SOPs (non-negotiable first three)
                  + Finish HP Estimator app
                  Target: Jun 15, 2026 — before first PP client enrolled
```

---

## LOCKED ROCK SEQUENCE — 2026

```
Constraint: Marcin runs HP part-time (20–30 hrs/wk alongside corporate sales job).
Sequence locked: Revenue and systems before hiring. Demand before headcount.

Rock 1:  Finish HP Estimator app + write S1/S4/S5 SOPs (Baseline, Prioritize, Schedule)
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jun 15, 2026
  Why: App is 90% done — must close out and move off Housecall Pro.
       S1/S3/S5 SOPs are required before any PP client is enrolled.

Rock 2:  Enroll first 3–5 Proactive Path founding members
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jun 15, 2026
  Why: Zero revenue without clients. Test the model before scaling it.
       Target: previous clients + warm network. Founding member pricing (modest,
       time-limited discount) — do NOT deep discount. Anchors wrong expectation.

Rock 3:  Organic marketing launch (Google profile, content, referral SOP)
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Jul 31, 2026
  Why: Build inbound pipeline without paid spend. Reviews + referrals first.

Rock 4:  Paid marketing (Google ads, targeted outreach)
  Owner: Marcin   Due: Aug 15+, 2026
  Why: Only after organic systems are live and conversion process is proven.

Rock 5:  Lead Tech hire
  Owner: Marcin   Trigger: When PP volume creates a delivery constraint
  Why: Hiring before demand = overhead without revenue.
       Profile: Generalist maintenance tech — NOT a remodel specialist.
       Carpenter sub (Sub #1) is NOT this hire — different ambition, wrong skill set.

Rock 6:  Sub agreements — Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC (COIs on file)
  Owner: Marcin   Trigger: After Lead Tech hired
  Why: Sub agreements formalize the roster that Lead Tech will coordinate.
       HVAC sub is missing — must be sourced before first full PP seasonal cycle.
```

---

## CURRENT TOP 3 BOTTLENECKS

```
1. Zero PP clients enrolled — No recurring revenue yet. Everything depends on Rock 2
   (first 3–5 founding members by Jun 15). Without clients, nothing else matters.
   Sequence: App done → SOPs written → enroll clients → then market → then hire.

2. HP Estimator app not fully live — 90% done. Must close the last 10% (front-end
   to back-end integration) before enrolling clients. This is the operational backbone.
   Without it, there's no system to trust with client data or job management.

3. SOPs exist only in Marcin's head — S1 / S3 / S5 must be written before client #1.
   The 360° Method cannot be delivered consistently or delegated without them.
   S1 (Baseline), S4 (Prioritize), S5 (Schedule) are Rock 1 alongside the app. No skipping or deferring.

Sub-constraint: Electrical sub is unreliable (often busy with bigger contractors).
   Need a backup electrical sub before seasonal visit delivery begins at scale.
   HVAC sub entirely missing — required for PP seasonal protocol. Source now.
```

---

## 1-YEAR GOALS — 2026

```
Proactive Path clients enrolled:    20 (Dec 31)
Seasonal visits delivered on time:  100% — no missed visits
SOPs complete:                       All 9 steps (Sep 30)
Lead Technician hired:              Aug 1
Sub agreements signed:              3+ (HVAC, plumbing, electrical) — Jul 31
Google reviews:                      50 at 4.9★ (Dec 31)
Revenue (project, non-recurring):   $200K+
```

---

## 3-YEAR PICTURE — 2029

```
Proactive Path clients (active):    50+
ARR (Proactive Path):               $250K–$350K
Project revenue:                    $200K+
Blended gross margin:               35%+
Team:                               Owner + Lead Tech + 2 Field Techs + Office/Estimator
Seasonal visits on schedule:        100% — always
Phase 3 clients:                    20+ in active upgrade advisory
Google rating:                      4.9★ / 60+ reviews
Sub roster:                         5+ vetted specialists on standing agreement
Owner time in field:                <20% — system runs without owner on every job
```

---

## 10-YEAR BHAG

```
The most trusted home stewardship company in Clark County —
managing 150+ Proactive Path clients, generating $1M+ in recurring annual revenue,
with a team that delivers the 360° Method without the owner in the field.

When a Clark County homeowner thinks "someone who manages my home" —
they think Handy Pioneers.
```

---

## ORG STRUCTURE — ACCOUNTABILITY CHART

*Pioneers Compass functional structure first. HP people mapped in. Gaps labeled.*

```
Dept              Seat                  Person        Status
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Leadership        Owner / CEO           Marcin        Active
                  Manager / COO         VACANT        GAP — needed for scale
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Marketing         Marketing Lead        VACANT        GAP — Marcin by default
                  (brand, reviews,                    No process or seat exists
                   referral program)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Sales             Sales Lead            Marcin*       STRETCHED
                  (PP inquiries,        *also in      No CRM. No close SOP.
                   enrollment close,    Owner + Ops   >40% conversion target
                   pipeline)            + Finance
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Operations        Lead Technician       VACANT        HIRING — Aug 1
                  (Phase 2 ACT)                       Primary scaling constraint
                  Field Tech(s)         VACANT        Post-Lead hire
                  Office / Estimator    VACANT        HIRING
                  (scheduling, docs,
                   estimates, portal)
                  Sub Roster            Pending       HVAC/Plumbing/Electrical
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Finance           Finance / Admin       VACANT*       GAP
                  (invoicing, AR,       *Office seat  No job costing. No P&L
                   job costing, P&L)    will cover    review. Billing is manual.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CRITICAL: Marcin currently covers Owner + COO + Marketing + Sales +
          Operations + Finance. First hire (Lead Tech) takes Ops off
          his plate. Second hire (Office/Est.) takes Sales coord +
          Finance + Marketing admin off his plate.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
360° Phase → Department mapping:
  Phase 1 (AWARE):    Owner/CEO (Marcin) — non-delegated
  Phase 2 (ACT):      Operations (Lead Tech + Office)
  Phase 3 (ADVANCE):  Owner advisory + Operations execution
  Sales closes P1.  Marketing generates P1 opportunities.
  Finance protects margin across all phases.
```

---

## FINANCIAL RULES — NON-NEGOTIABLE

```
Standard project gross margin minimum:     30%
Small job gross margin minimum:            40%
  (Small job = hard cost under $2,000)
Internal labor cost rate:                  $100/hr  [INTERNAL — never share with clients]
Customer billing rate (labor):             $150/hr  [INTERNAL — never share with clients]
Never expose:                              markup multipliers, hard costs, cost math

IDS trigger on margin:    Any job finishing below margin floor.
                          Any estimate variance >15%.
                          Flag immediately — not at month-end.
```

---

## CORE VALUES — CONFIRMED

```
Value 1: Show Up Ready
  What it looks like: On time. Clean truck. Full kit. Clients at this price point
  expect the professional they paid for — every visit, no exceptions.

Value 2: Own It
  What it looks like: If something's wrong, say so and fix it.
  No deflection, no excuses, no waiting to see if the client notices.

Value 3: Straight Talk
  What it looks like: Honest assessments. No upselling what a home doesn't need.
  No softening what it does. The roadmap reflects reality.

Value 4: Quality That Lasts
  What it looks like: Every repair done to last. No shortcuts that come back.
  The 1-year guarantee means we do it right the first time.

Value 5: Earn the Referral
  What it looks like: Every engagement is a marketing event.
  We perform like the next client is watching — because they often are.
```

---

## BRAND STANDARDS

```
Primary brand colors:
  Dark gold:   #7A5D12
  Mid gold:    #A07C1E
  White:       #FFFFFF

Voice / tone:
  Professional, reliable, straightforward. Confident but approachable.
  Emphasize: coordination, cleanliness, clear scope, documented delivery.
  Avoid: high-pressure language, vague promises, filler.
  Soft CTAs always. Never hard close.

Web stack:    React / Tailwind CSS / shadcn — mobile-first
```

---

## 360° METHOD — QUICK REFERENCE

> The 360° Method is a proactive home maintenance framework.
> Handy Pioneers is the company that delivers it.

```
PHASE 1 — AWARE (Steps 1–3): Know the Home
  Step 1: Baseline Assessment     — one-time, full walkthrough, 10 systems, owner-led always
  Step 2: Seasonal Inspection Log — recurring 4x/year, measures change from baseline
  Step 3: Tracking (Client Portal)— every visit and finding documented same day

PHASE 2 — ACT (Steps 4–6): Take Action  [The Recurring Cycle]
  Step 4: Prioritize              — holistic: urgency + budget + goals + cadence + one-offs
                                    produces the Priority Roadmap the client has agreed to
  Step 5: Schedule                — confirms dates with client: seasonal cadence + projects
                                    no work scheduled without signed estimate
  Step 6: Execute                 — Scope → Estimate → Approve → Execute → Document
                                    margin floors enforced on every job

PHASE 3 — ADVANCE (Steps 7–9): Build Value  [Activates at 12+ months]
  Step 7: Preservation Projects   — proactive life-extension before failure
  Step 8: Strategic Upgrades      — ROI-driven, aligned with client's Sell/Refinance/Stay goal
  Step 9: Advance the Home        — Home Score (0–100), annual review, referral ask, resale docs
```

---

## MEETING PULSE (L10 / EOS)

```
Daily Huddle:         M–F, 15 min — What's on track / stuck / needed
L10 Meeting:          Weekly, 90 min — Scorecard → Rocks → Headlines → To-Dos → IDS
Quarterly Planning:   Full day — review Rocks, set new Rocks, update V/TO
Annual Planning:      2 days — full V/TO reset, BHAG check-in, 3-Year Picture update
```

---

## HOW EVERY TASK FLOWS THROUGH THE PIONEERS COMPASS

```
Step 1 — Classify the work:
  Predefined → execute per SOP (all 9 step SOPs + supporting processes)
  Unplanned  → clarify next action → check authority → act or escalate
  Defining   → protect time → execute in that block (SOP-writing IS defining work)

Step 2 — Check financial rules:
  Any estimate/pricing → enforce margin floors (30% standard / 40% small job)
  Any spend → check authority threshold
  Any job finishing below margin floor → IDS trigger, same day

Step 3 — Check brand rules:
  Client-facing output → apply voice, no internal cost exposure
  Design/web task → HP gold + white, mobile-first

Step 4 — Log or surface issues:
  Any threat to delivery quality, client relationship, or margin → IDS
  Five categories: Phase 1 quality / Phase 2 execution / trade coordination /
                   margin erosion / documentation

Step 5 — Report format (internal only):
  5 bullets max. Status: Done / In progress / Blocked.
  One ask per escalation. No preamble.
```

---

## LINKED DOCUMENTS

```
Full BOS Framework:    Pioneers-Compass/HP-BOS-Filled.md
SOP Library Index:     Pioneers-Compass/SOPs/SOP-Index.md
Visual Reference:      Pioneers-Compass/BOS-Visual-Reference.html
Weekly Scorecards:     Pioneers-Compass/Scorecards/
EOS Operating Plan:    HP_EOS_Operating_Plan_v2.docx
360° Method Doc:       HP_Delivering_the_360_Method.docx
```

---

*Last updated: 2026-05-28 | Version: 2.0 — filled from EOS Operating Plan + 360° Method doc*
*Update this file each quarter: active phase/step, rocks, bottlenecks, team seats*

docs.body:
# HP INDEPENDENCE PLAN
## Pioneers Compass | Step 9: Step Back
## When Marcin Exits Each Seat — And What Replaces Him

```
Last updated: 2026-05-28
Purpose: Define the sequence and trigger conditions for Marcin to exit
         each operational seat — so the business runs without him
         in the field and without him in every function.

The goal is not to disappear. The goal is to be in the right seats only:
Owner/CEO + Phase 1 assessments + Phase 3 advisory.
Everything else gets handed off in the order below.
```

---

## CURRENT STATE — MARCIN IS IN EVERY SEAT

```
Seat                  Current Owner   Status
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Owner / CEO           Marcin          KEEP — always
Phase 1 Assessments   Marcin          KEEP — non-delegated indefinitely
Phase 3 Advisory      Marcin          KEEP — client relationship requires owner
Manager / COO         Marcin (default) EXIT — when team is large enough
Sales Lead            Marcin          EXIT #3 — after Office/Estimator hired
Marketing Lead        Marcin (default) EXIT #2 — after Office/Estimator hired
Operations / Field    Marcin          EXIT #1 — Lead Tech hire
Finance / Admin       Marcin (default) EXIT #2 — after Office/Estimator hired
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## EXIT SEQUENCE

---

### EXIT #1 — OPERATIONS (PHASE 2 FIELD EXECUTION)
**Who takes it:** Lead Technician (external hire)
**Trigger:** PP client volume creates a delivery constraint — Marcin cannot execute all seasonal visits and roadmap items without missing SLAs or burning out

**What Marcin hands off:**
```
[ ] All 4 seasonal visits per client — Lead Tech runs independently
[ ] Roadmap execution items (NOW/SOON) — Lead Tech executes with written scope
[ ] On-site sub supervision and quality sign-off
[ ] Same-day portal documentation (S3)
[ ] Delivery metrics at weekly L10
```

**What Marcin keeps:**
```
[ ] All Phase 1 baseline assessments — non-delegated, owner-led always
[ ] Prioritization meetings (S4) — owner attends until Office can support
[ ] Sub agreements and roster decisions
[ ] Any job with a scope change or margin issue — escalated immediately
```

**Handoff readiness checklist:**
```
[ ] P9 Seasonal Checklists reviewed with Lead Tech for every season
[ ] Lead Tech has completed 3+ supervised seasonal visits
[ ] Lead Tech portal entries reviewed and meeting HP standard
[ ] P12 (Hiring & Onboarding SOP) 30-day checklist complete
[ ] 90-day GWC check: Gets It ✓ / Wants It ✓ / Capacity ✓
[ ] Lead Tech can run a seasonal visit and full IDS debrief without Marcin present
```

**Time recovered:** ~15–20 hrs/week (field execution removed from Marcin's plate)

---

### EXIT #2 — FINANCE, MARKETING ADMIN, SCHEDULING
**Who takes it:** Office / Estimator (hire after Lead Tech is onboarded)
**Trigger:** PP client count reaches 8–10; scheduling and invoicing load becomes a bottleneck; Marcin is spending >5 hrs/week on admin tasks that don't require his judgment

**What Marcin hands off:**
```
[ ] Scheduling — all seasonal visit coordination and project scheduling (S5)
[ ] Estimates — Office builds first draft; Marcin reviews and approves
[ ] Client portal QC — Office checks entries are complete and accurate
[ ] Invoicing and collections (P5) — Office owns send and follow-up
[ ] Review request workflow (P11) — Office sends and tracks
[ ] Google Business Profile maintenance
[ ] Sub COI file maintenance
[ ] Monthly P&L inputs (P16) — Office gathers data; Marcin reviews
```

**What Marcin keeps:**
```
[ ] Final estimate approval — reviews every estimate before it goes out
[ ] Any pricing exception or margin flag decision
[ ] Sub agreement and roster decisions
[ ] P&L review and financial decisions
[ ] Any client escalation
```

**Handoff readiness checklist:**
```
[ ] Office has run P4 (Estimating) independently on 5+ jobs, reviewed by Marcin
[ ] Office has managed the scheduling calendar for 2+ full weeks
[ ] First invoice sent by Office without Marcin involvement (reviewed after)
[ ] P16 monthly review run by Office with Marcin walkthrough
[ ] Communications channels established — Office is the scheduling point of contact
```

**Time recovered:** ~10–15 hrs/week (admin, invoicing, scheduling off Marcin's plate)

---

### EXIT #3 — SALES COORDINATION
**Who takes it:** Office / Estimator (same hire — expanded role over time)
**Trigger:** Organic marketing is generating inbound inquiries that Marcin cannot respond to within 2 hours consistently; Office has been in the role for 60+ days and understands the client profile

**What Marcin hands off:**
```
[ ] Initial inquiry response — Office responds within 2 hours, qualifies the prospect
[ ] Scheduling the enrollment consultation (Marcin still runs the consultation)
[ ] Pipeline tracking — Office maintains the CRM/inquiry log
[ ] Follow-up on stale prospects
```

**What Marcin keeps forever in Sales:**
```
[ ] The enrollment consultation — Marcin closes every PP client, always
[ ] Pricing and tier decisions
[ ] Any prospect who has been referred by an existing client
[ ] Final say on who becomes a PP client
```

**Note:** Marcin does not exit the sales close. He exits the coordination around it.

---

### EXIT #4 — COO / MANAGER (FUTURE)
**Who takes it:** Promoted Lead Tech or external hire
**Trigger:** Team has 3+ seats filled; Marcin is spending >20% of his time on team coordination rather than client relationships and vision
**Timeline:** Not before 2028 — requires stable team, documented processes, and proven delivery

**What this exit enables:**
Marcin in: Owner/CEO + Phase 1 Baselines + Phase 3 Advisory + Vision
Marcin out of: Everything operational

---

## WHAT MARCIN NEVER EXITS

```
Owner / CEO seat                       Always
Phase 1 Baseline Assessments           Always — this is the trust-setter for every PP relationship
Phase 3 advisory conversations         Always — these are high-value, high-trust, owner-level
Core values and culture                Always
Strategic direction and BHAG           Always
Sub agreements and roster decisions    Until COO is hired
```

---

## INDEPENDENCE SCORECARD

Track quarterly: how much time is Marcin spending in each seat?

```
Quarter: ___________

Seat                    Hours/Week  Target      Gap
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Owner/CEO               ___         10+         ___
Phase 1 Baselines        ___         4–6         ___
Phase 3 Advisory         ___         2–4         ___
Operations/Field         ___         0 (post-LT) ___
Finance/Admin            ___         0 (post-Off) ___
Sales coordination       ___         0 (post-Off) ___
Marketing admin          ___         0 (post-Off) ___
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Total weekly hours:      ___         20–25       ___

If Marcin is spending >5 hrs/week in any seat marked "0 target" →
IDS trigger: what is blocking the handoff?
```

---

## 3-YEAR INDEPENDENCE PICTURE (2029)

```
Owner time in field:        <20% — system runs without owner on every job
Lead Tech:                  Running Phase 2 independently across 50+ clients
Office/Estimator:           Handling all scheduling, invoicing, and admin
Marcin's primary role:      Phase 1 baselines + Phase 3 advisory + strategic direction
Team size:                  Owner + Lead Tech + 2 Field Techs + Office/Estimator
```

# HP WEEKLY SCORECARD
## L10 Meeting | Reviewed Every Week — Every Metric Has One Owner

```
SCORECARD INSTRUCTIONS:
  Green  = On target or above
  Yellow = Within 10% of target — watch
  Red    = Below target or missed — IDS trigger at this L10
  N/A    = Not yet applicable (pre-hire seats)

Fill in "This Week" column before every L10. Carry forward trend (↑ ↓ →).
One owner per row. Owner is accountable — not responsible for doing the work,
responsible for knowing the number and surfacing issues.
```

---

## SCORECARD — HANDY PIONEERS

```
WEEK OF: ___________     L10 #: ___     Attendees: ___________
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Metric                              Owner    Target      This Week   G/Y/R  Trend
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PP CLIENTS & SALES
PP clients active (cumulative)      Marcin   20 by 12/31  ___        ___    ___
PP enrollments this week            Marcin   1+/wk        ___        ___    ___
PP inquiries this week              Marcin   Track        ___        ___    ___
PP at-risk clients (any flag)       Marcin   0            ___        ___    ___

DELIVERY / OPERATIONS
Seasonal visits on schedule (%)     Lead     100%         ___%       ___    ___
Portal entries current <48hrs (%)   Lead     100%         ___%       ___    ___
Open NOW roadmap items overdue      Lead     0            ___        ___    ___
Sub flags this week (callbacks/no)  Lead     0            ___        ___    ___

FINANCE
Open receivables >30 days ($)       Office   $0           $___       ___    ___
Gross margin — rolling 4-wk avg     Marcin   35%+         ___%       ___    ___
Jobs below margin floor this wk     Marcin   0            ___        ___    ___
PP subscription revenue (MTD)       Office   Per target   $___       ___    ___

MARKETING
Google reviews — cumulative         Office   50 by 12/31  ___        ___    ___
New Google reviews this week        Office   1+/wk        ___        ___    ___
Review requests sent this week      Office   Track        ___        ___    ___

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

ROCK STATUS (review each rock: On Track / Off Track)
Rock 1: App + S1/S4/S5 SOPs (Jun 15)           OT / OT
Rock 2: First 3–5 PP clients (Jun 15)           OT / OT
Rock 3: Organic marketing launch (Jul 31)       OT / OT
Rock 4: Paid marketing (Aug 15+)                OT / OT
Rock 5: Lead Tech hire (demand-triggered)       OT / OT

IDS ITEMS FROM THIS SCORECARD (any Red → IDS list):
1. _______________________________________________________________
2. _______________________________________________________________
3. _______________________________________________________________
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## METRIC DEFINITIONS

```
PP clients active          Running total of signed PP agreements in good standing.
                           Count only clients with signed agreement + first payment collected.

PP enrollments this week   New signed agreements this week. Target accelerates: 1/wk now,
                           2/wk once organic marketing is live.

PP at-risk                 Any client who has expressed dissatisfaction, missed a payment,
                           or not responded to 2+ scheduling attempts. 0 is always the target.

Seasonal visits on schedule % of scheduled seasonal visits that ran within the confirmed
                           season window (Spring=Mar–Apr, Summer=Jun–Jul, Fall=Sep–Oct,
                           Winter=Dec–Jan). Any visit outside the window = Yellow.

Portal entries current     % of visits from the past week with a portal entry submitted
                           within 48 hours. Below 100% = Red.

NOW roadmap items overdue  Any NOW-priority roadmap item that has passed its target date
                           without a scheduled execution date. 0 is always the target.

Open receivables >30 days  Dollar value of any invoice unpaid past 30 days. $0 always.

GM rolling 4-week avg      Blended gross margin across all project jobs closed in the past
                           4 weeks. Pulled from P6 job costing. Target 35%+.

Jobs below margin floor    Any job that closed at <30% GM (standard) or <40% GM (small job).
                           Every instance is an IDS item — not a trend to tolerate.

Google reviews cumulative  Running total on Google Business Profile. Target: 50 by Dec 31.
```

---

## SOLO L10 FORMAT (until Lead Tech + Office are hired)

When Marcin is the only attendee, run a 30-minute solo L10 weekly:

```
Minutes 0–5:    Fill in the scorecard — every row
Minutes 5–10:   Rock review — honest On Track / Off Track
Minutes 10–15:  Headlines — what happened this week that matters?
Minutes 15–25:  IDS — pick the top 1–2 Red items; write the solve
Minutes 25–30:  To-dos — no more than 5; name, action, due date
```

The solo L10 is not optional. Without it, issues accumulate unnoticed.
Running it alone is how Marcin holds himself accountable until the team exists.

---

## ANNUAL METRIC TARGETS — 2026

```
PP clients enrolled (Dec 31):          20
Seasonal visits on schedule:           100%
Google reviews (Dec 31):               50 at 4.9★
Project revenue:                       $200,000+
Blended gross margin:                  35%+
Open receivables >30 days:             $0 always
Jobs below margin floor:               0
```

# IDS ISSUES LOG
## Handy Pioneers | L10 Meeting — Identify · Discuss · Solve

```
IDS RULES:
  Identify:  Anyone adds an issue before the L10 — no blame, just the problem.
             Write the issue as a statement, not a question or a complaint.
             One sentence. Root cause focus — not the symptom.

  Discuss:   One discussion. Root cause only — not symptoms.
             5-minute max per issue. If it takes longer, it's not clear enough yet.

  Solve:     One owner. One action. One due date.
             "We'll look into it" is not a solve.
             A solve changes something — it doesn't study something.

  Rule:      Every Red scorecard item → IDS list. Every sub flag → IDS list.
             Every margin miss → IDS list. Solve at the L10 or assign to owner by EOD.
```

---

## ISSUE CATEGORIES (HP-Specific)

```
CAT-1   Phase 1 quality issue (baseline, roadmap, prioritization)
CAT-2   Phase 2 execution issue (seasonal visit, scheduling, execution)
CAT-3   Sub/trade issue (no-show, callback, quality, reliability)
CAT-4   Margin erosion (below-floor job, scope change uncontrolled)
CAT-5   Client relationship (complaint, churn risk, communication failure)
CAT-6   Documentation (portal not current, missing photos, no sign-off)
CAT-7   Financial / collections (overdue invoice, billing error)
CAT-8   Rocks / strategic (rock off track, bottleneck, hiring delay)
```

---

## ACTIVE ISSUES LOG

```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
#   Date      Cat  Issue (root cause statement)        Owner   Due     Status
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    OPEN — add issues here as they are identified
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## SOLVED ISSUES LOG

```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
#   Date      Cat  Issue                               Solution               Closed
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    SOLVED — move issues here once the solve is confirmed complete
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## HOW TO WRITE A GOOD ISSUE

```
Bad:  "The scheduling is a mess."
Good: "Two seasonal visits slipped outside their season window because
       client confirmation is not being collected at enrollment."

Bad:  "Sub #3 (electrician) is unreliable."
Good: "Sub #3 missed a confirmed HP job date with no notice — we have
       no backup electrical sub and no HP agreement on file."

Bad:  "We need more clients."
Good: "Rock 2 (3–5 founding member enrollments by Jun 15) is off track —
       no outreach to prior clients has been made since the rock was set."

The issue statement should make the solve obvious.
```

---

## CURRENT KNOWN ISSUES (pre-populate from BOS assessment)

```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1   2026-05-28  8   HP Estimator app not live — PP enrollment    Marcin  OPEN
                    blocked from scaling until front-end is
                    connected. Rock 1 deadline: Jun 15.

2   2026-05-28  8   Zero PP clients enrolled — no recurring      Marcin  OPEN
                    revenue. Every other rock depends on this.
                    Outreach to prior clients not yet started.

3   2026-05-28  3   Sub #3 (electrician) unreliable — no         Marcin  OPEN
                    agreement on file, often unavailable.
                    No backup electrical sub exists.

4   2026-05-28  3   HVAC sub not sourced — required before        Marcin  OPEN
                    first PP seasonal cycle begins. No candidate
                    identified yet.

5   2026-05-28  3   Sub #1 (carpenter) has no agreement or        Marcin  OPEN
                    COI on file despite active use on HP jobs.

6   2026-05-29  8   ARR target undefined after pricing update.     Marcin  SOLVED 2026-05-29
                    RESOLVED: Mixed tier target confirmed.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

docs.body:
# P1 — PROACTIVE PATH ENROLLMENT SOP
## Supporting Process | Sales → Operations Handoff

```
SOP Name:     P1 — Proactive Path Enrollment SOP
Work Type:    Predefined — runs for every new PP prospect
Who owns it:  Owner / CEO (Marcin) — enrollment close is always owner-led
How often:    Per new Proactive Path client
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Convert a qualified prospect into a signed Proactive Path client — from first conversation through signed agreement, deposit collected, baseline scheduled, and portal profile active.

---

## THE TWO ENTRY POINTS

```
Entry Point 1 — Project Path → PP Conversion
  Client hired HP for a one-off job. Relationship earned.
  The project created trust. The conversion happens at or after job completion.
  Pitch: "Based on what we found today, here's what a Proactive Path looks like for your home."

Entry Point 2 — Direct PP Inquiry
  Client heard about the program through referral, Google, or organic content.
  No prior HP relationship. Phase 1 conversation is the close.
  Pitch: "Let me walk you through exactly what we'd do in the first 90 days."
```

---

## QUALIFYING THE CLIENT

Before presenting the program, confirm the prospect fits the HP client profile:

```
[ ] Home value $500K+ in Clark County, WA
[ ] Already paying for at least one recurring home service (lawn, pest, cleaning)
[ ] Time-poor — doesn't want to manage multiple vendors
[ ] Has deferred maintenance or knows things need attention
[ ] Not primarily price-shopping — looking for a trusted relationship

Green flags: referred by existing client or agent / mentioned specific deferred items /
             expressed frustration with finding reliable contractors

Red flags: only asking about price / wants to compare quotes /
           wants to manage the work themselves and just needs labor
```

---

## THE ENROLLMENT CONVERSATION

**Do not pitch the program before listening.** The sequence is:

```
Step 1:  Ask about the home (age, systems, what they know needs attention)
Step 2:  Ask about their experience managing home maintenance
           "What does it look like when something needs attention at your home?"
Step 3:  Ask about their goals for the property (stay long-term / sell / refinance)
Step 4:  Present the 360° Method as the solution to what they described
           Do not read from a script — reflect their specific situation back to them.

Core framing:
  "Most homeowners operate in react mode — something breaks, they call around.
  What we do is get ahead of it. You get one relationship, a documented system,
  and a home that's building value instead of losing it."

Step 5:  Walk through the three tiers. Recommend the right fit based on the conversation.
Step 6:  Handle questions. Soft close: "Does this feel like the right fit for where you are?"
```

---

## PROACTIVE PATH TIERS — CLIENT-FACING (360° Membership)

All tiers include: annual 360° Home Scan, prioritized findings report w/ written scope,
member rates, HP direct line. Three billing options: monthly / quarterly / annual prepay.

```
ESSENTIAL                          $59/mo | $169/qtr | $588/yr
  2 seasonal visits: Spring + Fall
  Labor bank: none
  Member discount: 5% (<$1k) / 3% ($1k–$5k) / 1.5% (>$5k)
  Best for: client who wants baseline protection and early-warning monitoring.
  No known deferred work; wants the system, not the labor.

FULL COVERAGE  [MOST POPULAR]      $99/mo | $279/qtr | $948/yr
  4 seasonal visits: all four seasons
  Labor bank: $300/yr pre-paid
  Member discount: 8% (<$1k) / 5% ($1k–$5k) / 2.5% (>$5k)
  Best for: client with a 2–3 item list or who wants the full seasonal cycle
  covered. Labor bank absorbs small roadmap items.

MAXIMUM PROTECTION                 $149/mo | $419/qtr | $1,428/yr
  4 seasonal visits: all four seasons
  Labor bank: $600/yr pre-paid
  Member discount: 12% (<$1k) / 8% ($1k–$5k) / 4% (>$5k)
  Priority scheduling: YES (within 5 business days)
  Best for: high-value home, active upgrade goals, planning to sell/refinance,
  or client who wants one dedicated point of contact for all trade coordination.
```

**Which tier to recommend:**
- Client wants monitoring only, no deferred work → Essential
- Client has known items or wants full seasonal coverage → Full Coverage (labor bank covers it)
- Client with $1M+ home, complex systems, or sell/refi in 2–3 years → Maximum Protection

---

## FOUNDING MEMBER PRICING (Current — Time-Limited)

```
During initial enrollment phase (before client #6):
  Offer a modest, time-limited discount to the first cohort of PP clients.
  Framing: "We're enrolling our first founding members right now —
  I can offer you [X]% off the first year, locked in for your renewal."

Rules:
  [ ] Discount modest — do NOT go below 30% GM on the annual subscription fee
  [ ] Time-limited — tied to the founding cohort, not an ongoing offer
  [ ] Do not frame as a "trial" — they are real clients, not test cases
  [ ] Lock in the full-price renewal in writing at sign-up
```

---

## THE ENROLLMENT STEPS — IN ORDER

```
Step 1:  Verbal agreement — client says yes to a tier

Step 2:  Send the Proactive Path Agreement (P3 — Agreement Template)
           Email same day. Give 48-hour window to review and sign.

Step 3:  Collect deposit / first month payment
           Essential: $59 first month due at signing (or $169 quarterly / $588 annual)
           Full Coverage: $99 first month due at signing (or $279 quarterly / $948 annual)
           Maximum Protection: $149 first month due at signing (or $419 quarterly / $1,428 annual)
           Payment method: [HP Estimator app / preferred method]

Step 4:  Create client portal profile
           Enter: client name, property address, home details (age, sq ft, beds/baths),
           known systems, prior maintenance history (if shared)

Step 5:  Schedule the baseline assessment (S1)
           Within 10 business days of signed agreement.
           Confirm: Marcin leads all baselines — no exceptions.
           Send 48-hour confirmation with what to expect.

Step 6:  Schedule all 4 seasonal visit dates for Year 1
           Do this at enrollment — not later. Get dates on the calendar now.
           Enter all 4 dates in portal and send client calendar invites.

Step 7:  Send welcome communication
           Confirm: agreement signed, baseline date, what to expect at the visit.
           Tone: confident, warm, professional. No over-promising.
           "Your first baseline visit is [date]. That's where we start building
           the full picture of your home. Bring any questions you have."

Step 8:  Log enrollment in HP records
           Date enrolled, tier, founding member status (Y/N), baseline date confirmed.
```

---

## DECISION POINTS

```
If prospect is interested but "not ready yet" →
  Do not discount or pressure. Set a follow-up date.
  "When would be a good time to revisit this?" — get a specific date.

If prospect asks for references or proof →
  Offer to walk them through the portal and what a client profile looks like.
  Google reviews if available. HP track record on prior project work.

If prospect wants to negotiate price →
  Founding member framing only — modest discount, time-limited, locked renewal.
  If they're price-shopping, they're the wrong client. Do not chase.

If prospect's home is in significantly worse shape than expected →
  Do not over-promise what Year 1 will accomplish.
  "The baseline will tell us exactly what we're working with. The roadmap will
  lay out a realistic plan across 1–3 years. We'll prioritize together."
```

---

## HP MARGIN CHECK
```
Proactive Path subscription is a service retainer — not a project bid.
For any project work drawn from the Full Coverage or Maximum Protection labor bank:
  [ ] Standard: 30% GM minimum / Small job: 40% GM minimum
  [ ] Labor bank credit ($300 Full Coverage / $600 Maximum Protection) is pre-paid —
      ensure delivery cost does not exceed the credit without client-approved overage billing
  [ ] Founding member discount must not push the annual fee below break-even
      on seasonal visits + baseline + roadmap delivery costs
```

---

## DOCUMENTATION REQUIRED
```
[ ] Signed PP Agreement in client portal
[ ] First payment collected and logged
[ ] Client portal profile created — property details entered
[ ] Baseline date confirmed and in HP calendar
[ ] All 4 seasonal visit dates confirmed and in HP calendar + portal
[ ] Welcome communication sent
[ ] Enrollment logged in HP records
```

---

## COMMON MISTAKES
- Presenting pricing before understanding the client's situation — leads to sticker shock.
- Leaving without scheduling the baseline — "I'll call you to set that up" loses momentum.
- Over-promising what Year 1 will accomplish before the baseline is complete.
- Offering deep discounts instead of founding member framing — anchors the wrong price.

---

## HOW YOU KNOW YOU'RE DONE
Agreement signed, payment collected, portal profile active, baseline scheduled, all 4 seasonal dates confirmed, welcome communication sent.

---

## RELATED SOPs
- S1 — Baseline Assessment (first deliverable after enrollment)
- S4 — Prioritize (first prioritization meeting follows baseline)
- S5 — Schedule (all 4 seasonal dates set at enrollment)
- P3 — PP Agreement Template (sent at Step 2)
- P2 — Client Onboarding Workflow (runs parallel to enrollment)
docs.title: P1 — PROACTIVE PATH ENROLLMENT SOP

docs.body:
# P10 — 360° REFERRAL PROGRAM SOP
## Supporting Process | Earn the Referral

```
SOP Name:     P10 — 360° Referral Program SOP
Work Type:    Predefined — runs after every successful delivery touchpoint
Who owns it:  Owner / CEO (Marcin) + Office
How often:    Ask at every annual review. Acknowledge every referral given.
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Every satisfied PP client is a referral source. The ask is built into the delivery — not bolted on as a separate campaign. Build a referral pipeline that compounds as the client base grows.

---

## WHEN TO ASK

```
Primary ask — Annual Review (S9):
  Built into the annual review format. After showing the Home Score progression
  and confirming next year's plan: "Is there anyone in your network who would
  benefit from having a system like this for their home?"

Secondary ask — After a standout delivery moment:
  After completing a significant project the client is visibly happy with.
  After a seasonal visit where a serious issue was caught and addressed.
  Framing: "Clients like you tend to know other homeowners who care about
  maintaining their property. If anyone comes to mind, I'd be grateful for
  the introduction."

Do NOT ask:
  At billing time / when there's an open issue / during a difficult conversation
  At the baseline (too early — trust isn't built yet)
```

---

## THE ASK — LANGUAGE

```
Soft version (default):
  "Is there anyone in your network — neighbors, family, colleagues —
  who might benefit from having a system like this for their home?"

Direct version (after strong delivery moment):
  "This is the kind of work I want to do more of. If you know someone
  who'd appreciate this level of care for their home, I'd be grateful
  for the introduction. Even just a text from you means more than
  any ad I could run."

Response to "I'll think about it":
  "No pressure at all — if someone comes to mind, you have my number.
  I appreciate you even thinking about it."
```

---

## REFERRAL FOLLOW-THROUGH

```
When a referral is given:
  Step 1:  Thank the client immediately — personal text or call, same day
  Step 2:  Log the referral in the portal: who gave it, who was referred, date
  Step 3:  Reach out to the referred prospect within 24 hours
             Use the client's name: "I was introduced to you by [Client Name]..."
  Step 4:  Run normal P1 enrollment qualification process
  Step 5:  After referral converts to a client — thank the referring client again
             Handwritten note or meaningful gesture (not a gift card — too transactional)

When a referral does NOT convert:
  Step 1:  Log the outcome in portal
  Step 2:  No need to update referring client unless they ask
```

---

## REFERRAL TRACKING

```
Log in client portal under 'Referrals' for each PP client:
  Referrals given:       [Name, date, outcome]
  Referrals converted:   [Number, revenue generated]

Review at each annual review:
  "You've sent us [X] referrals this year — thank you. [Name] just enrolled
  last month. We're grateful for the trust."

Annual target: 30%+ of new PP clients come from referrals by Year 2
```

---

## COMMON MISTAKES
- Asking for a referral before delivering clear value — the ask must be earned.
- Making the ask transactional ("we offer $X for referrals") — HP's model is trust-based.
- Not following up with the referred prospect within 24 hours — momentum dies.
- Not acknowledging the referral with the referring client — they'll stop sending them.

---

## RELATED SOPs
- S9 — Advance the Home (primary referral ask moment is the annual review)
- P1 — PP Enrollment SOP (referred prospects enter here)
- P11 — Review Request Workflow (separate ask — reviews and referrals are different)
docs.title: P10 — 360° REFERRAL PROGRAM SOP

docs.body:
# P11 — REVIEW REQUEST WORKFLOW
## Supporting Process | Google Reviews — Target: 50 at 4.9★ by Dec 31

```
SOP Name:     P11 — Review Request Workflow
Work Type:    Predefined — triggered after every positive delivery moment
Who owns it:  Office / Estimator (Owner until Office hired)
How often:    After every completed project job + after every seasonal visit
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Build to 50 Google reviews at 4.9★ by December 31, 2026. Reviews are HP's primary trust signal for new PP prospects. Every satisfied client is an opportunity.

---

## WHEN TO ASK

```
Best moments (ask within 24 hours):
  - Project job completed and client sign-off given
  - Seasonal visit where client expressed satisfaction
  - After catching a problem the client didn't know about (built trust)
  - After any moment the client says "thank you" or "great job"

Do NOT ask:
  - When there's an open issue or unresolved concern
  - When billing is overdue
  - Within the first 30 days of enrollment (too early)
  - More than once in a 90-day period for the same client
```

---

## THE ASK — LANGUAGE

```
Text (preferred — highest conversion):
  "Hi [Name] — great to be at the house today. If you have 2 minutes,
  a Google review would mean a lot to us. Here's the direct link:
  [Google Review Link]. Thank you."

Email (if client prefers email):
  Subject: Quick favor — 2 minutes

  Hi [Name],

  It was great working on [project/visit] at your home today.
  If you have a moment, a Google review would genuinely help us
  reach more homeowners like yourself in Clark County.

  Direct link: [Google Review Link]

  No need for a long review — even a sentence or two means a lot.

  Thank you,
  Marcin
  Handy Pioneers LLC

In person (most natural, highest quality reviews):
  At the end of a visit, after client sign-off:
  "One quick ask — we rely heavily on Google reviews for new clients.
  If today met your expectations, a quick review would mean a lot.
  I can send you a direct link right now if that's easier."
```

---

## THE REVIEW LINK

Google Review direct link: `[Insert HP Google Business Profile review link here]`

Shorten the URL before sending — a long Google URL looks suspicious in a text.
Use: bit.ly or similar shortener. Label it: `[bit.ly/hp-review]` or similar.

---

## FOLLOW-UP PROTOCOL

```
If no review after 5 days → one follow-up only:
  "Hi [Name] — just wanted to make sure my earlier message came through.
  No pressure at all — whenever you have a moment: [link]"

If no review after second contact → drop it.
  Never ask a third time. It becomes pushy and damages the relationship.
```

---

## REVIEW MONITORING & RESPONSE

```
Check Google Business Profile: weekly minimum

When a new review comes in:
  [ ] Respond within 48 hours — every review, every time
  [ ] Thank the client by first name if possible
  [ ] Reference something specific from the review
  [ ] Keep responses warm and professional — 2–4 sentences

Response template (positive review):
  "Thank you, [Name]! [Reference specific detail from review].
  It's exactly the kind of work we take pride in at Handy Pioneers.
  We look forward to continuing to take care of your home."

If a negative review comes in:
  Step 1:  Do not respond immediately — wait 24 hours
  Step 2:  Contact the client directly to understand and resolve
  Step 3:  Respond publicly only after attempting private resolution
  Step 4:  Public response: acknowledge, do not argue, state what was done
  Step 5:  Flag to Marcin — every negative review is an IDS item
```

---

## REVIEW TRACKING

```
Monthly check:
  Current Google rating:     ____★
  Total reviews:             ____
  New reviews this month:    ____
  Reviews responded to:      ____
  Target Dec 31:             50 reviews at 4.9★
```

---

## COMMON MISTAKES
- Asking for a review when there's an unresolved issue — damages the relationship.
- Sending a review request more than twice — comes across as desperate.
- Not responding to reviews — looks like HP doesn't care about feedback.
- Waiting too long to ask — the moment of satisfaction fades fast.

---

## RELATED SOPs
- S6 — Execute (review ask happens after project job sign-off)
- S2 — Seasonal Inspection Log (ask after positive seasonal visits)
- P10 — 360° Referral Program (separate ask — don't combine in the same message)
docs.title: P11 — REVIEW REQUEST WORKFLOW

docs.body:
# P12 — HIRING & ONBOARDING SOP
## Supporting Process | Lead Technician Hire

```
SOP Name:     P12 — Hiring & Onboarding SOP
Work Type:    Defining — run when PP delivery volume creates a constraint
Who owns it:  Owner / CEO (Marcin)
Trigger:      PP client count creates scheduling or delivery pressure Marcin can't absorb
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Hire and onboard the right Lead Technician — a generalist maintenance professional who owns Phase 2 delivery — so Marcin can step back from field execution without delivery quality dropping.

---

## BEFORE YOU POST THE JOB

```
[ ] P13 (Seat Descriptions) is written — Lead Tech seat description complete
[ ] S2 (Seasonal Inspection Log) is written and field-tested — Lead Tech will own this
[ ] S5 (Schedule) is written — Lead Tech will manage the seasonal calendar
[ ] P9 (Seasonal Checklists) are written — Lead Tech will run from these
[ ] HP Estimator app is live — Lead Tech needs a system to log into
[ ] At least 3 active PP clients — enough volume to justify the hire
```

---

## LEAD TECH PROFILE — WHO YOU'RE HIRING

```
Right fit:
  - Generalist maintenance background (NOT a remodel specialist)
  - Comfortable with: general carpentry, caulking, weatherproofing, minor plumbing,
    HVAC filter service, gutter cleaning, seasonal maintenance tasks
  - Wants to own a process — not just complete tasks
  - Reliable, professional presentation, communicates well with homeowners
  - Clark County area — knows PNW conditions

Wrong fit:
  - Remodel specialist / framer (that's Sub #1's profile — different seat)
  - Someone who wants to run their own business (they'll leave)
  - Anyone who GWC the seat: Gets It? Wants It? Has Capacity? — all three required

Compensation range (estimate — verify against current market):
  $25–$35/hr depending on experience and skill set
  Consider: mileage reimbursement, tools allowance, cell phone stipend
```

---

## THE HIRING PROCESS

```
Step 1 — Post the job
  Platforms: Indeed, Craigslist (Clark County), local trade groups on Facebook
  Use the seat description from P13 as the job post basis
  Be specific: "generalist maintenance tech for residential home management company"
  Do NOT post a generic handyman ad — you'll get the wrong applicants

Step 2 — Screen applications (phone screen, 15 min)
  Questions:
    "What types of maintenance work are you most comfortable with?"
    "Describe the last time you caught a problem at a job site the client didn't know about."
    "What does a professional job site look like to you when you leave?"
    "Are you comfortable with client-facing work — walking homeowners through what you found?"
  Disqualify: anyone who can't communicate clearly, has spotty availability, or
              only wants remodel work

Step 3 — Working interview (half day, paid)
  Take the candidate to a real property (with client permission) or a test scenario
  Evaluate: how they approach a systematic inspection, how they communicate findings,
            how they handle a tool or situation they haven't seen before
  This is the most important step — do not skip it

Step 4 — Reference check
  Two prior employer references minimum
  Ask: "Would you rehire this person?" — if hesitation, that's an answer

Step 5 — Offer
  Written offer letter with: rate, schedule, mileage policy, probation period (90 days)
  Clear expectation: this is a Phase 2 ownership role, not task labor
```

---

## ONBOARDING — FIRST 30 DAYS

```
Week 1 — Systems and context
  [ ] Walk through the 360° Method start to finish — they need to understand the WHY
  [ ] Read S1, S2, S3, S4, S5, S6 SOPs together — discuss each one
  [ ] Walk through P9 Seasonal Checklists — every item, every rationale
  [ ] Introduce to HP Estimator app — portal login, how to log a visit
  [ ] Shadow Marcin on one baseline assessment

Week 2 — Supervised field work
  [ ] Lead Tech leads a seasonal visit with Marcin present
  [ ] Debrief after every visit — what was found, how it was documented, what's next
  [ ] Review portal entries together — correct anything that doesn't meet standard

Week 3 — Independent with check-in
  [ ] Lead Tech runs a seasonal visit independently
  [ ] Marcin reviews portal entry same day
  [ ] Weekly debrief: what went well, what needs adjustment

Week 4 — Operational handoff
  [ ] Lead Tech owns the seasonal visit schedule going forward
  [ ] Marcin reviews quality but is not on-site for every visit
  [ ] 30-day check-in: GWC re-assessment — is this the right person?
```

---

## 90-DAY GWC CHECK

At 90 days, formally assess the Lead Tech against the seat:

```
Gets It:   Do they understand the 360° Method and what each step requires?
           Do they document correctly without being reminded?
           Do clients respond positively to them?

Wants It:  Are they taking ownership of the seasonal calendar?
           Are they flagging issues proactively, or waiting to be asked?
           Do they want to grow in this role?

Capacity:  Are they handling the full seasonal visit scope reliably?
           Are they managing their time and schedule without Marcin's involvement?
           Are their portal entries accurate and complete?

Result:    All three YES → confirm the hire, discuss growth path
           Any NO → address directly; give 30 days to correct; then decide
```

---

## RELATED SOPs
- P13 — Seat Descriptions (Lead Tech seat description is the job post basis)
- S2, S5, P9 — the three SOPs Lead Tech must own before hire is declared complete
docs.title: P12 — HIRING & ONBOARDING SOP

docs.body:
# P13 — SEAT DESCRIPTIONS
## Supporting Process | All 5 Functional Seats

```
SOP Name:     P13 — Seat Descriptions
Work Type:    Defining — reference document for hiring and accountability
Who owns it:  Owner / CEO (Marcin)
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

> These seat descriptions define what each function owns — not who currently fills it.
> The structure is permanent. The people filling it are not.
> Every seat has one owner. If it doesn't, that function has no accountability.

---

## SEAT 1 — OWNER / CEO

```
Current person: Marcin Micek
Reports to:     N/A

WHAT THIS SEAT OWNS:
  - Vision, BHAG, and long-term direction of Handy Pioneers
  - Core values — set, modeled, and enforced
  - All Phase 1 baseline assessments (non-delegated — always owner-led)
  - All Phase 3 advisory conversations with clients
  - Final accountability on every client relationship
  - Strategic hires — Lead Tech and Office/Estimator decisions
  - Sub agreements and sub roster standards
  - Financial oversight — final approval on any expense or pricing exception

WHAT THIS SEAT DOES NOT OWN (when fully staffed):
  - Day-to-day scheduling (Office)
  - Field execution of seasonal visits (Lead Tech)
  - Invoice and collections (Office)
  - Portal documentation (Lead Tech + Office)

KEY METRICS:
  - PP client count and retention rate
  - Blended gross margin (35%+ target)
  - Team seats filled vs. vacant
  - Client satisfaction and referral rate
```

---

## SEAT 2 — LEAD TECHNICIAN

```
Current person: VACANT — hire triggered by PP delivery demand
Reports to:     Owner / CEO

WHAT THIS SEAT OWNS:
  - Phase 2 (ACT) field execution — Steps 4, 5, 6 for all PP clients
  - All 4 seasonal visits per client — on schedule, every year
  - Roadmap execution items: NOW first, SOON on schedule
  - On-site sub supervision and quality sign-off
  - Same-day portal photo and note logging (S3)
  - Sub performance flagging — 2 callbacks or 1 no-show reported immediately
  - Weekly delivery metrics at L10

WHAT THIS SEAT DOES NOT OWN:
  - Client enrollment or pricing conversations (Owner)
  - Invoice and collections (Office)
  - Sub agreements (Owner)
  - Phase 1 baseline assessments (Owner — non-delegated)

PROFILE — RIGHT FIT:
  - Generalist maintenance background
  - Comfortable with PNW seasonal maintenance tasks
  - Professional client-facing presentation
  - Wants to own a process, not just complete tasks
  - NOT a remodel specialist (that's the sub roster)

KEY METRICS:
  - Seasonal visits on schedule: 100%
  - Portal entries complete within 24 hours: 100%
  - Sub quality flags: 0
  - Client complaints on delivery: 0
```

---

## SEAT 3 — OFFICE / ESTIMATOR

```
Current person: VACANT — hire after Lead Tech is onboarded
Reports to:     Owner / CEO

WHAT THIS SEAT OWNS:
  - Scheduling — all PP client seasonal dates and project jobs
  - Estimates — written scope, margin audit (P4), client-facing format
  - Client portal QC — entries complete, accurate, current
  - Invoice and collections (P5) — open invoices >30 days: $0 target
  - Client communication between visits
  - Sub coordination — scheduling, job briefs, COI file maintenance
  - Monthly P&L inputs (P16)
  - Review request workflow (P11)

WHAT THIS SEAT DOES NOT OWN:
  - Phase 1 baseline assessments (Owner)
  - On-site execution (Lead Tech)
  - Sub agreements (Owner)
  - Strategic or pricing decisions (Owner)

PROFILE — RIGHT FIT:
  - Organized, detail-oriented, reliable communicator
  - Comfortable with scheduling, invoicing, and client communication
  - Can learn HP Estimator app and portal quickly
  - Does not need to be a trade expert — needs to be excellent at coordination

KEY METRICS:
  - Estimates sent within 48 hours of scope confirmation
  - Invoices sent within 24 hours of job completion
  - Open invoices > 30 days: $0
  - Portal entries QC'd within 48 hours of any visit
```

---

## SEAT 4 — MARKETING LEAD

```
Current person: VACANT — Marcin by default until Office/Estimator hired
Reports to:     Owner / CEO

WHAT THIS SEAT OWNS:
  - Google Business Profile maintenance and optimization
  - Review request follow-through (P11)
  - Referral program tracking (P10)
  - Website content — handypioneers.com
  - Social presence and local brand awareness (Clark County)
  - Email/nurture for PP prospects
  - Organic marketing content — posts, case studies, before/after

WHAT THIS SEAT DOES NOT OWN:
  - Paid advertising decisions (Owner)
  - Sales conversations or enrollment (Owner)

NOTE: In the current phase, this seat is secondary. Referrals + reviews are
the primary marketing engine. Organic content comes next. Paid is last.

KEY METRICS:
  - Google reviews: 50 at 4.9★ by Dec 31, 2026
  - New PP inquiries from organic sources: tracked monthly
  - Website traffic and lead form submissions: tracked monthly
```

---

## SEAT 5 — FINANCE / ADMIN

```
Current person: VACANT — Office/Estimator seat will cover this function
Reports to:     Owner / CEO

WHAT THIS SEAT OWNS:
  - Job costing (P6) — actual vs. estimated margin on every job
  - Monthly P&L review (P16)
  - Expense tracking and categorization
  - Payroll coordination (when team is hired)
  - Sub payment processing (Net-7 after client payment)
  - Annual tax preparation inputs

WHAT THIS SEAT DOES NOT OWN:
  - Pricing decisions (Owner + P4)
  - Invoice creation (Office/Estimator)

NOTE: Until a dedicated finance resource exists, Marcin runs the monthly P&L
review (P16) and the Office/Estimator handles job costing inputs.

KEY METRICS:
  - Monthly P&L reviewed by 5th of following month
  - Job costing complete on every closed job
  - No invoices paid to subs before client payment received
```

# P16 — MONTHLY P&L REVIEW TEMPLATE
## Supporting Process | Know the Numbers Before Month-End

```
SOP Name:     P16 — Monthly P&L Review Template
Work Type:    Predefined — runs by the 5th of every month for the prior month
Who owns it:  Owner / CEO (Marcin) — until Finance/Office seat is filled
How often:    Monthly — every month without exception
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Know HP's financial position every month — revenue, costs, margin, and cash. Not at tax time. Not when something is wrong. Every month, by the 5th.

---

## MONTHLY P&L REVIEW — TEMPLATE

Complete by the 5th of every month for the prior month.

```
MONTHLY P&L — HANDY PIONEERS LLC               [INTERNAL — CONFIDENTIAL]
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Month / Year:     ___________
Reviewed by:      Marcin Micek
Review date:      ___________
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

REVENUE
  PP Subscriptions (recurring):
    Essential clients × $59/mo:          ___ × $59  = $________
    Full Coverage clients × $99/mo:      ___ × $99  = $________
    Max Protection clients × $149/mo:    ___ × $149 = $________
    (Annual/quarterly billing: enter as monthly equivalent for this line)
    Total PP Revenue (monthly):                       $________

  Project / One-Off Revenue:                    $________
  Other Revenue:                                $________
  TOTAL REVENUE:                                $________

  MoM Change:   $________ ( ____% vs. prior month )
  YTD Revenue:  $________

COST OF GOODS SOLD (Direct Job Costs)
  HP Labor (actual hours × $100/hr):            $________
  Sub Labor (actual invoices paid):             $________
  Materials (actual receipts):                  $________
  Permits:                                      $________
  TOTAL COGS:                                   $________

GROSS PROFIT
  Gross Profit:        Revenue − COGS =         $________
  Gross Margin %:      GP / Revenue =           _____%
  Target:              35%+
  Status:              [ ] On target  [ ] Below target — IDS trigger

OPERATING EXPENSES
  Insurance (prorate monthly):                  $________
  Tools and equipment:                          $________
  Vehicle (fuel, mileage, maintenance):         $________
  Software / subscriptions (HP Estimator, etc): $________
  Marketing (if any paid spend):                $________
  Office / admin:                               $________
  Other:                                        $________
  TOTAL OPEX:                                   $________

NET INCOME
  Net Income:     Gross Profit − OPEX =         $________
  Net Margin %:   Net Income / Revenue =        _____%

CASH
  Cash on hand (start of month):                $________
  Cash on hand (end of month):                  $________
  Open receivables (unpaid invoices):           $________
  Receivables > 30 days:                        $________
  ⚠ Any receivable > 30 days: IDS trigger
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

PP CLIENT METRICS
  Active PP clients (end of month):   ___
  New enrollments this month:         ___
  Cancellations this month:           ___
  Net change:                         ___
  ARR (annual recurring revenue):     $________
  Target (Dec 31, 2026):              $20,000+ (20 clients, mixed tier — 40% Essential / 40% Full Coverage / 20% Max Protection)

JOB METRICS
  Jobs completed this month:          ___
  Jobs below margin floor:            ___   Target: 0
  Avg actual GM (from P6 costing):    ____% Target: 35%+
  Estimates sent:                     ___
  Estimates accepted:                 ___   Conversion rate: ____%
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

IDS ITEMS THIS MONTH
  (List any financial metric that triggers a flag — resolve at next L10)
  1. _______________________________________________________________
  2. _______________________________________________________________

DECISIONS / ACTIONS FROM THIS REVIEW
  1. _______________________________________________________________
  2. _______________________________________________________________
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

---

## IDS TRIGGERS FROM THE P&L REVIEW

```
Trigger IDS at next L10 if any of the following:
  [ ] Gross margin below 30% for the month
  [ ] Any job below margin floor (from P6 job costing)
  [ ] Open receivables > 30 days (any amount)
  [ ] PP client cancellation — understand why
  [ ] Revenue down more than 20% vs. prior month without known cause
  [ ] Cash on hand below 1 month of operating expenses
```

---

## QUARTERLY TREND VIEW

Run after each quarter-end (March, June, September, December):

```

Revenue Q:     $________    vs. prior Q: $________   Change: ____%
GM % Q:        _____%       vs. prior Q: _____%
PP clients Q:  ___          vs. prior Q: ___
ARR Q:         $________    vs. prior Q: $________
Net income Q:  $________
```

---

## ANNUAL TARGETS — 2026

```
PP clients enrolled (Dec 31):          20
ARR (Dec 31):                          $20,000+ (mixed tier, 20 clients)
Project revenue:                       $200,000+
Blended gross margin:                  35%+
Google reviews:                        50 at 4.9★
```

---

## RELATED SOPs
- P4 — Estimating & Margin Audit (estimated margins feed actuals here)
- P5 — Invoice & Collections (open receivables tracked here)
- P6 — Job Costing Worksheet (actual job margins feed this review)

# P2 — CLIENT ONBOARDING WORKFLOW
## Supporting Process | First 30 Days of Every PP Relationship

```
SOP Name:     P2 — Client Onboarding Workflow
Work Type:    Predefined — runs for every new Proactive Path client
Who owns it:  Office / Estimator (Owner until Office hired)
How often:    Once per new PP client — runs alongside P1 enrollment
Last updated: 2026-05-28
```

---

## OBJECTIVE
Every new PP client is fully set up — portal active, communication established, baseline delivered, and first seasonal date confirmed — within 30 days of signing. A disorganized onboarding loses clients before the relationship earns.

---

## THE 30-DAY ONBOARDING SEQUENCE

```
Day 1 — Agreement signed + deposit collected (P1 Enrollment SOP)
  [ ] Create client portal profile (name, address, home details, known systems)
  [ ] Send welcome email (template below)
  [ ] Log enrollment: date, tier, founding member Y/N

Days 1–3 — Pre-baseline setup
  [ ] Add all 4 confirmed seasonal visit dates to HP calendar and client portal
  [ ] Send calendar invites to client for all 4 seasonal visits
  [ ] Review any prior project history HP has with this client — note in portal
  [ ] If client mentioned specific known issues at enrollment → pre-flag in portal
      under 'Pending Assessment' before baseline visit

Day 5–10 — Baseline Assessment delivered (S1)
  [ ] Marcin leads. All 10 systems inspected, rated, photographed.
  [ ] Same-day portal upload — all photos labeled, all ratings entered
  [ ] Verbal debrief with client before leaving

Days 5–10 — Post-baseline
  [ ] Schedule Prioritization Meeting (S4) — within 5 business days of baseline
  [ ] Confirm meeting method: in-person or video

Days 10–15 — Prioritization Meeting (S4)
  [ ] Present Priority Roadmap — walk client through NOW / SOON / WAIT
  [ ] Get written acknowledgment — email reply on file
  [ ] Any NOW items: hand off to S5 (Schedule) immediately

Day 30 — Onboarding complete check
  [ ] Portal profile complete: all 10 systems rated, photos uploaded
  [ ] Priority Roadmap in portal — client has acknowledged
  [ ] All 4 seasonal dates confirmed and in both HP calendar and portal
  [ ] Any NOW items: scheduled or in active estimate process
  [ ] Client has received at least 2 communications from HP since signing
```

---

## WELCOME EMAIL TEMPLATE

```
Subject: Welcome to Handy Pioneers — Your Proactive Path Starts Here

[Client Name],

Your Proactive Path enrollment is confirmed. Here's what happens next:

Baseline Assessment: [Date] at [Time]
Duration: approximately 2.5–3 hours
What to expect: We'll walk the full property together — every major system,
documented and photographed. This becomes the foundation for everything we do together.

Please have the following accessible:
  • HVAC filter location
  • Water heater access
  • Electrical panel unlocked
  • Attic and crawl space access if available

Your seasonal visit dates for Year 1 are:
  Spring:  [Date]
  Summer:  [Date]
  Fall:    [Date]
  Winter:  [Date]

You'll receive a calendar invite for each. Any questions before your baseline,
reach us at (360) 838-6731 or help@handypioneers.com.

Looking forward to it.

Marcin Micek
Handy Pioneers LLC | WA Lic. HANDYP★761NH | handypioneers.com
```

---

## PORTAL PROFILE REQUIRED FIELDS

```
Client Information:   Name, address, phone, email, preferred contact method
Property:             Year built, sq footage, beds/baths, stories
Known Systems:        HVAC type/age (if known), water heater age, roof age
Prior History:        Any prior HP project work — dates, scope, invoice refs
Enrollment:           Tier, date enrolled, founding member Y/N, annual renewal date
Notes:                Anything client mentioned at enrollment about known issues
```

---

## COMMON MISTAKES
- Setting up the portal profile after the baseline instead of before — wastes time on-site.
- Not sending calendar invites for all 4 seasonal dates at enrollment — leads to scheduling friction later.
- Skipping the post-baseline follow-up scheduling — momentum dies without a next step.

---

## HOW YOU KNOW YOU'RE DONE
Portal profile complete. Baseline delivered. Prioritization meeting complete. Client has acknowledged the roadmap. All 4 seasonal dates confirmed. Day 30 check passed.

---

## RELATED SOPs
- P1 — PP Enrollment SOP (triggers this workflow)
- S1 — Baseline Assessment (core deliverable of onboarding)
- S4 — Prioritize (follows baseline within 5 business days)
- S3 — Tracking / Client Portal (portal setup is part of this workflow)
